steps.action:
Find and open the locations area. (Common labels: "Locations,"
"Sites," "Facilities.")
steps.expected: The locations list appears, empty by default.

steps.action:
Choose the action to create a new location. Enter:
- Name: "{{primary_location}} - Main Plant"
- Address: a plausible street address in {{primary_location}}
- Phone: a plausible phone number
- Type: production / primary
steps.expected: The form accepts the values and saves successfully.

steps.action: Mark this location as the company's default or primary location.
steps.expected: A primary or default flag is visible on the location record.

expected_overall:
The primary location exists with a complete address and is marked as
the default. Other records can now reference it.
pass_criteria: Location is created AND visible in the list AND flagged as primary.

negative_variants.title: Reject location with missing required fields
negative_variants.action: Try to create a second location with only a name (no address).
negative_variants.expected:
The form blocks submission and explains which fields are required,
in plain language.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
Submission was blocked AND the error message names the missing
field(s) clearly.

title: Create a second location
goal:
Add a non-primary location to verify the system handles multi-site
operations.
preconditions: The primary location exists with a complete address and is flagged as the company default. (Established by P1-LOC-001.)

steps.action:
Find and open the locations area. Choose the action to create a
new location.
steps.expected: The location creation form appears.

steps.action:
Enter:
- Name: "Springfield Warehouse"
- Address: a plausible warehouse address in a different city
- Phone: a plausible phone number
- Type: warehouse / non-primary
steps.expected: The form accepts the values and saves.

steps.action: Open the locations list.
steps.expected:
Both locations appear. The primary location is visually
distinguished from the secondary.

expected_overall:
Two locations exist. The primary remains the default; the secondary
is available for reference but not flagged as primary.
pass_criteria: Both locations exist AND only the primary carries the default flag.
why_this_matters:
Many ERPs handle a single location well and break in subtle ways
when a second is added (records that silently default to "the only
location," reports that don't filter by location). Catching this
early saves a lot of rework.

negative_variants.title: Reject duplicate location name
negative_variants.action:
Try to create a third location with the same name as the
primary.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "name must be unique" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Duplicate name rejected AND existing record is identified.

negative_variants.title: Cannot demote the only primary
negative_variants.action:
Try to remove the primary flag from the primary location while
no other location is flagged primary.
negative_variants.expected:
The change is blocked or warns that exactly one primary
location must exist.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Tenant is never left with zero primary locations.

title: Configure a sales tax code
goal:
Set up a tax classification for sales in the company's primary tax
jurisdiction.
preconditions: Tax calculation has been configured: a tax provider has been chosen (integrated provider or manual rates) and the choice persists across tenant settings. (Established by P0-INTEG-003.)
The chart of accounts is initialized.

steps.action:
Find and open the tax codes area. (Common labels: "Tax Codes,"
"Tax Rates," "Tax Configuration.")
steps.expected: A list of tax codes appears, possibly empty or with seed entries.

steps.action:
Create a new tax code:
- Name: "OR Sales Tax" (or wherever the primary location is)
- Rate: a plausible rate for the jurisdiction (e.g., 0% for
  Oregon, 7.25% for California base)
- Posting account: link to the appropriate liability account
  from the chart of accounts.
steps.expected: The form accepts the values and saves.

steps.action:
Verify the tax code is selectable on a sample customer or
sales-side record.
steps.expected: The tax code appears in the dropdown wherever taxes are applied.

expected_overall:
At least one tax code exists, has a defined rate, and posts to a
real GL account.
pass_criteria:
Tax code is created AND has a non-error posting account AND is
selectable on customer / order records.

negative_variants.title: Reject tax code without a posting account
negative_variants.action: Try to save a tax code with the GL posting account left blank.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "posting account is required"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Save blocked AND missing field is named.

negative_variants.title: Reject tax code with rate above 100%
negative_variants.action: Try to save a tax code with a rate of 250%.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; the rate must fall within a sensible
bound.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Out-of-range rate refused.

negative_variants.title: Posting account must be a liability
negative_variants.action: Try to set the posting account to a Revenue or Expense account.
negative_variants.expected:
The application either filters the dropdown to liability
accounts or rejects the save with a clear "tax must post to a
liability account" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Tax cannot post to a non-liability account.

title: Configure a tax-exempt classification
goal:
Set up a tax code that records "no tax was charged" on transactions
that are legitimately exempt (resale, government, out-of-state).
preconditions: At least one sales tax code exists with a defined rate, exemption flag, and posting GL account. (Established by P1-TAX-001.)

steps.action: Open the tax codes area. Create a new code.
steps.expected: The tax code form appears.

steps.action:
Enter:
- Name: "Tax Exempt - Resale"
- Rate: 0%
- Mark as exempt (if there's a flag separate from the rate)
steps.expected: The form accepts the values and saves.

expected_overall:
An exempt classification exists and is distinguishable on reports
from "0% rate that just happens to compute to zero."
pass_criteria:
Exempt code is created AND distinguishable from regular zero-rate
codes.
why_this_matters:
Tax authorities expect exempt sales to be reported as exempt, not as
"we charged 0%." Conflating the two is a common audit finding.

negative_variants.title: Exempt classification requires exemption reason
negative_variants.action:
Try to save a tax-exempt code without selecting or entering an
exemption reason / category.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns that an exemption reason is required
for audit purposes.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
Exempt code without reason is rejected or surfaces an explicit
warning.

title: Confirm standard units of measure are available
goal:
Verify that common units (each, kg, lb, hour, foot, square foot,
gallon, liter) exist out of the box. Most ERPs ship with these; if
yours doesn't, it's a setup gap to flag.
preconditions: The application is bootstrapped: company identity, locale, time zone, fiscal year, currency, costing model, and integrations are configured, and the first administrator can sign in. (Phase 0 outcomes.)

steps.action:
Find and open the units of measure area. (Common labels: "Units
of Measure," "UOM," "Measurement Units.")
steps.expected:
A list of units appears. The list is either pre-populated with
common units OR offers to seed them.

steps.action:
Verify the following are present (or seed them if the system
offers): each, kilogram, pound, hour, foot, square foot, gallon,
liter.
steps.expected: All units listed are available.

expected_overall:
The unit list contains the common units a fabrication shop will need
for parts, assemblies, and labor.
pass_criteria: All listed units are present after this case completes.
why_this_matters:
Inventing missing units one-by-one as parts are added is slow and
inconsistent. Confirming the standard set exists early avoids that.

negative_variants.title: Cannot delete a built-in unit that is in use
negative_variants.action:
Try to delete the "each" unit (a default that other records
will reference once parts exist).
negative_variants.expected:
Deletion is blocked or warns that the unit is built-in /
referenced. No silent removal that breaks downstream forms.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Built-in unit deletion is refused with a clear reason.

title: Add a custom unit of measure
goal:
Verify the system supports adding business-specific units beyond the
defaults.
preconditions: Standard units of measure are available (each, kilogram, pound, hour, foot, square foot, gallon, liter). (Established by P1-UOM-001.)

steps.action: Open the units of measure area. Choose to add a new unit.
steps.expected:
A form appears with fields for name, abbreviation, and possibly
a category (length, weight, volume, etc.).

steps.action:
Add a "sheet" unit (abbreviation: sht), categorized as count or
pieces.
steps.expected: The unit saves successfully and appears in the list.

expected_overall:
The custom unit exists alongside the defaults and is selectable when
creating parts later.
pass_criteria: Custom unit is created AND visible in the list.

negative_variants.title: Reject duplicate UoM abbreviation
negative_variants.action:
Try to add another custom unit with the same abbreviation as an
existing unit (e.g., "ea" if "each" already uses "ea").
negative_variants.expected:
Submission is blocked with a clear message that the abbreviation
is already in use.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Submission blocked AND duplicate is named.

title: Define a unit conversion
goal:
Set up a conversion factor between two units (e.g., 1 sheet = 32
square feet) so that the system can convert quantities between
measurements when issuing or receiving inventory.
preconditions: Standard units of measure exist (each, kilogram, pound, hour, foot, square foot, gallon, liter) plus at least one custom unit (e.g. "sheet"). (Established by P1-UOM-001 and P1-UOM-002.)

steps.action:
Find the unit conversions area. (May be a separate screen or a
tab on the unit detail page.)
steps.expected: A way to define conversions appears.

steps.action: Define: 1 sheet = 32 square feet.
steps.expected:
The conversion saves. Both directions of the conversion are
visible (sheet to square feet and square feet to sheet).

steps.action:
In a part record (or a calculator if the system provides one),
enter "2 sheets" and verify it equals 64 square feet.
steps.expected: The conversion is applied correctly.

expected_overall:
The conversion exists and is correctly applied wherever the system
needs to convert between sheet and square foot.
pass_criteria:
Round-trip conversion (e.g., 2 sht to 64 sqft to 2 sht) is exact,
no rounding loss.
why_this_matters:
Lots of real production processes consume material in a different
unit than they purchase it (buy coil by the foot, consume by the
pound). Wrong conversions are a top source of inventory variance.

negative_variants.title: Reject zero or negative conversion factor
negative_variants.action:
Try to define "1 sheet = 0 square feet" or "1 sheet = -32
square feet."
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a plain-English message that conversion
factors must be positive.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Non-positive factor is rejected.

negative_variants.title: Reject conversion across incompatible categories
negative_variants.action: Try to define "1 hour = 5 kilograms" (time to mass).
negative_variants.expected:
The application either blocks or warns clearly that a
conversion across categories is meaningless.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
Cross-category conversion is refused or surfaces an explicit
warning.

negative_variants.title: Reject conflicting reverse conversion
negative_variants.action:
After saving "1 sheet = 32 sqft", try to save "1 sheet = 50
sqft" (a contradicting factor).
negative_variants.expected:
The save is blocked or asks the user to confirm overwriting
the prior conversion.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Conflicting conversion is detected and surfaced.

title: Invite a second system user with a non-admin role
goal:
Create a second user account assigned to a less-privileged role,
to verify role-based access actually restricts what the user can
see and do.
preconditions: At least one non-admin role exists (P0-USER-001).
At least four employees exist covering varied pay classifications (hourly with overtime, salaried-exempt, salaried-non-exempt). (Established by P1-EMP-003.)

steps.action: Find and open the users area. Choose to invite a new user.
steps.expected: The invite form appears with fields for email, name, and role.

steps.action:
Invite a user with email "operator@example.com" assigned to a
Floor Operator role (or equivalent non-admin role from P0).
steps.expected:
The invite is sent (or the user is created directly with a
temporary credential, depending on your auth flow).

steps.action:
Optionally link the new user to one of the employee records
from P1-EMP-003.
steps.expected:
The link is recorded. The user record reflects the employee
linkage.

expected_overall:
A second user exists with a non-admin role and (optionally) is
linked to an employee record.
pass_criteria:
User exists with the assigned role AND can sign in (verify in
P1-USER-002) AND, if linked, the employee linkage is visible.

negative_variants.title: Reject invitation to deactivated role
negative_variants.action:
Deactivate or delete a non-admin role, then try to invite a
user assigned to that role.
negative_variants.expected:
The save is blocked with a clear message that the chosen role
is unavailable.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Invitation to a non-active role is refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject invite without email
negative_variants.action: Try to send an invite with the email field empty.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "email is required" error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Invite without email is refused.

title: Verify role-based access restricts what a non-admin user can see
goal:
Sign in as the user created in P1-USER-001 and confirm they cannot
see or change administrator-only settings.
preconditions: A second non-admin system user exists, assigned a Floor Operator (or equivalent) role and able to sign in. (Established by P1-USER-001.)
You can sign in as the user created in P1-USER-001 (a credential or invite link).

steps.action:
Sign out from the administrator account. Sign in as the second
user.
steps.expected:
Sign-in succeeds. The home / landing page is appropriate to a
Floor Operator (work order list, scanner workflow, etc.) — NOT
the administrator setup pages.

steps.action:
Try to navigate to the administrator-only areas: company
settings, user management, role permissions, integrations,
chart of accounts.
steps.expected:
Each area is either invisible in the navigation OR returns a
clear "you don't have permission" message — not a stack trace,
not a blank screen, not a partial page.

steps.action: Sign back out. Sign back in as the original administrator.
steps.expected: The administrator pages are visible and functional again.

expected_overall:
Role permissions actually restrict access. The non-admin user sees
only what their role allows; the administrator sees everything.
pass_criteria:
Each restricted area was inaccessible to the non-admin user AND
produced a clear, non-leaky error or omission.
why_this_matters:
Permissions checks that look right in the UI but don't actually
enforce on the backend are a common security bug — the UI hides
the menu but a direct URL still works. Spot-checking a few areas
in this case catches the most common version of that bug.

negative_variants.title: Direct URL access to a restricted page
negative_variants.action:
While signed in as the non-admin user, try to reach an
administrator-only page by typing its URL directly into the
browser address bar.
negative_variants.expected:
The page does NOT render. Either you're redirected to a
"permission denied" screen or back to your role's home page.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
Restricted page does not load AND no privileged data leaks
into the page that does load.

title: Deactivate a system user
goal:
Verify a user can be deactivated (so they can no longer sign in)
without losing their historical activity in the system.
preconditions: At least one non-admin user exists (P1-USER-001).

steps.action:
Open the user record from P1-USER-001. Find and use the
deactivate / disable action.
steps.expected: A confirmation dialog appears. Choose to confirm.

steps.action: Sign out and try to sign in as the deactivated user.
steps.expected:
Sign-in is rejected with a clear message — not a generic
"wrong password" error that would let an attacker enumerate
accounts.

steps.action:
Sign back in as the administrator. Search for the deactivated
user.
steps.expected:
The user still appears, marked as deactivated. Their
historical activity (audit log entries, anything attributed
to them) is preserved.

expected_overall:
Deactivated users cannot sign in but their records and history
remain. They can be reactivated if needed.
pass_criteria:
Sign-in is blocked AND the user record still exists AND
historical activity is preserved.
why_this_matters:
Deletion of users would orphan audit log entries and make it
impossible to reconstruct who did what. Deactivation preserves
the trail while preventing further access.

negative_variants.title: Cannot deactivate the last administrator
negative_variants.action:
As the only remaining administrator, try to deactivate
yourself.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked with a clear message that at least one
active administrator must exist.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Last-admin deactivation is refused.

negative_variants.title: Deactivation revokes active sessions
negative_variants.action:
While Sam Rivera is signed in (in another browser), have the
admin deactivate Sam Rivera. Wait briefly and have Sam Rivera
try any action.
negative_variants.expected:
Sam Rivera's session is invalidated. Their next action returns
a sign-in prompt or "session ended" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Existing sessions of deactivated users do not continue to act.

title: Create the first work center
goal:
Configure a physical or logical area where production work happens.
Work orders will be assigned to work centers; labor and overhead
will post against them.
preconditions: At least one location exists.
You are signed in as a user with permission to configure work centers.

steps.action: Find and open the work centers area.
steps.expected: The work centers list appears, empty by default.

steps.action:
Choose the action to create a new work center. Enter:
- Name: "Press Shop"
- Location: {{primary_location}} - Main Plant
- Default labor rate: a plausible hourly rate
- Capacity: a plausible hours-per-day value
steps.expected: The form accepts the values and saves.

steps.action: Open the work center you just created.
steps.expected:
All values you entered are visible. The location is correctly
linked to the location record from P1-LOC-001.

expected_overall:
The work center exists, is linked to a location, and carries default
values that routings will inherit.
pass_criteria:
Work center is in the list AND opens to show entered values AND
links to the correct location.

negative_variants.title: Reject work center without a location
negative_variants.action: Try to create a work center with no location selected.
negative_variants.expected:
Submission is blocked. The error explains that a location is
required.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Submission blocked AND error names the location field.

negative_variants.title: Reject negative capacity or labor rate
negative_variants.action:
Try to create a work center with capacity = -8 hours/day, then
again with labor rate = -$25.
negative_variants.expected:
Each submission is blocked with a plain-English error explaining
that the value must be non-negative. The application does NOT
silently accept a negative number that would later corrupt
capacity calculations.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
Both submissions blocked AND error wording is clear AND no
record was created.

negative_variants.title: Reject work center pointing at an inactive location
negative_variants.action:
Deactivate a location (or attempt to point a new work center at
a known-deactivated location).
negative_variants.expected:
Selection is either filtered out or rejected with a clear
message that the destination location is inactive.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: No work center created against an inactive location.

title: Create work centers for common operations
goal:
Round out the work center list to cover the operations a typical
fabrication shop performs. Subsequent routings will assign operations
to these.
preconditions: At least one work center exists (e.g. Press Shop), linked to the primary location, with a default labor rate and capacity. (Established by P1-WC-001.)

steps.action:
Create a work center for each of the following:
- "Cut & Saw" (location: primary)
- "Weld" (location: primary)
- "Paint" (location: primary)
- "Assembly" (location: primary)
- "Inspection" (location: primary)
steps.expected: Each work center is created successfully and appears in the list.

steps.action: Open the work centers list and sort or filter by name.
steps.expected:
All five new work centers (plus Press Shop) are visible. Sort
and filter behave as a normal list view should.

expected_overall:
Six work centers exist, all linked to the primary location, ready to
be referenced by routings in Phase 2.
pass_criteria: Six work centers exist AND each links to the primary location.

negative_variants.title: Reject duplicate work center name within location
negative_variants.action:
Try to create a second "Weld" work center at the primary
location.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "name must be unique within the
location" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Duplicate work center name within the same location is refused.

title: Configure a work center with a non-default calendar
goal:
Verify a single work center can run on a different schedule than the
rest of the plant (e.g., the paint booth running a single shift while
the rest runs two).
preconditions: At least one calendar exists. (See P1-CAL-001.)
Multiple work centers exist covering common operations (Press Shop, Cut & Saw, Weld, Paint, Assembly, Inspection), all linked to the primary location. (Established by P1-WC-002.)

steps.action: Open the "Paint" work center.
steps.expected: The work center detail view opens.

steps.action:
Find the calendar or schedule setting. Override the plant default
with a single-shift calendar.
steps.expected: The override is accepted. Save succeeds.

steps.action:
Open a different work center (e.g., "Weld") and verify its
calendar is unchanged.
steps.expected: The other work center still uses the plant default calendar.

expected_overall:
Paint runs on a non-default calendar; other work centers are
unaffected. Capacity calculations should reflect the per-work-center
schedule.
pass_criteria:
Paint shows the override calendar AND another work center confirms
the default is still in place.

negative_variants.title: Reject calendar override pointing at deleted calendar
negative_variants.action:
Delete (or deactivate) a calendar that a work center is using
as an override, then reload the work center.
negative_variants.expected:
The application prevents deletion of an in-use calendar OR
explicitly resets the work center to the default with a clear
warning. No silent dangling reference.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: No work center carries a reference to a non-existent calendar.

title: Create a single-level BOM for a finished part
goal:
Define what goes into making FG-BRACKET-A1. The BOM will be
consumed by work orders to issue material.
preconditions: A raw material part record exists (e.g. RM-STEEL-1018-3X3) with type, unit of measure, default vendor, and inventory GL account. (Established by P2-PART-001.)
A finished-goods part exists (e.g. FG-BRACKET-A1) with type, unit of measure, sales GL account, and a list price. (Established by P2-PART-002.)

steps.action: Open FG-BRACKET-A1. Find the BOM section. Choose to add a BOM.
steps.expected: A BOM editor opens with rows for components.

steps.action:
Add components:
- 0.5 ft of RM-STEEL-1018-3X3 per finished unit
- 4 each of "Hardware-Bolt-M8" (create this part if it doesn't
  exist)
- 1 each of "Hardware-Washer-M8"
steps.expected: Each component row accepts the values. UoMs match the parts.

steps.action: Save the BOM. Set the BOM as effective today.
steps.expected: BOM saves. It is now the active BOM for the part.

expected_overall:
FG-BRACKET-A1 has a BOM defining material consumption per unit
produced.
pass_criteria:
BOM exists AND lists all three components AND quantities are
per-finished-unit.

negative_variants.title: Reject BOM line with same component twice
negative_variants.action:
Try to add the same component (e.g., RM-STEEL-1018-3X3) on two
separate BOM lines without an explicit "alternate" relationship.
negative_variants.expected:
Application blocks the duplicate with a clear message OR merges
the lines automatically. Silent acceptance of two independent
lines for the same component is the failure mode.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Duplicate handled cleanly with no silent acceptance.

negative_variants.title: Reject BOM with circular reference
negative_variants.action:
Try to set up a sub-assembly where BOM(A) contains B, and BOM(B)
contains A.
negative_variants.expected:
Application detects the cycle and blocks the save with a clear
error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Cycle detected AND save blocked.

title: Create a multi-level BOM with a sub-assembly
goal:
Verify hierarchical BOMs work — a finished good that contains a
sub-assembly which has its own BOM.
preconditions: A finished-goods part has a single-level BOM listing component parts, quantities-per-unit, and units of measure, marked effective. (Established by P2-BOM-001.)

steps.action:
Create a sub-assembly part "SA-FRAME-A1" (type: WIP / sub-assembly).
Give it its own BOM with raw materials.
steps.expected: Sub-assembly part exists with its own BOM.

steps.action:
Create a new finished good "FG-CART-A1" that uses 1 SA-FRAME-A1
as a component.
steps.expected: Finished good has a BOM that references the sub-assembly.

steps.action: View the exploded BOM for FG-CART-A1.
steps.expected:
The full hierarchy is visible: FG-CART-A1 → SA-FRAME-A1 → its raw
materials.

expected_overall: Multi-level BOMs are supported and visible end-to-end.
pass_criteria:
Exploded view shows all levels AND raw material totals are correct
(rolled up across the hierarchy).

negative_variants.title: Reject sub-assembly with no BOM of its own
negative_variants.action:
Try to add SA-FRAME-A1 as a component before its own BOM has
been defined.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; sub-assemblies cannot be referenced
until they are built.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Empty-BOM sub-assembly cannot be referenced as a component.

negative_variants.title: Cycle detection across multiple levels
negative_variants.action:
Configure FG-CART-A1 → SA-FRAME-A1 → FG-CART-A1 (a 3-level
cycle).
negative_variants.expected:
The application detects the cycle even across multiple levels
and blocks the save with a clear error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Multi-level cycles are caught.

title: Revise a BOM and track the revision
goal:
Verify BOM changes are versioned — the previous version is preserved
and the change is auditable.
preconditions: A finished-goods part has a single-level BOM listing component parts, quantities-per-unit, and units of measure, marked effective. (Established by P2-BOM-001.)

steps.action:
Open the BOM for FG-BRACKET-A1. Make a change: increase the
bolt quantity from 4 to 6.
steps.expected:
The system either prompts for a change reason / revision letter,
or auto-creates a new revision visible alongside the old.

steps.action: Save the change. Note the new revision identifier.
steps.expected: Old BOM revision is preserved. New revision is now active.

steps.action: View BOM history.
steps.expected:
Both revisions are visible with their effective dates and
changes.

expected_overall:
BOM revisions are fully tracked. The previous BOM can be referenced
for any work orders that started under it.
pass_criteria: Old revision preserved AND new revision active AND history viewable.
why_this_matters:
In-process work orders should consume materials per the BOM that
was active when the work order was released — not the latest. Without
revision tracking, this is impossible to enforce.

negative_variants.title: Cannot edit a released BOM revision in place
negative_variants.action:
Open the previously active BOM revision and try to edit it
directly.
negative_variants.expected:
Edits are blocked; the user must create a new revision.
Released history is immutable.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Old revisions cannot be retroactively modified.

negative_variants.title: Reject revision with future effective date that overlaps current
negative_variants.action:
Save a revision effective today while the prior revision was
effective from yesterday with no end date.
negative_variants.expected:
Application either auto-closes the prior revision at today, or
blocks the save with a clear "overlapping effective dates"
error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: No two revisions are simultaneously effective.

title: Configure an approved alternate component
goal:
Verify a BOM line can carry one or more approved alternate parts
to use when the primary is unavailable.
preconditions: A finished-goods part has a single-level BOM listing component parts, quantities-per-unit, and units of measure, marked effective. (Established by P2-BOM-001.)

steps.action:
Open FG-BRACKET-A1's BOM. On the bolt line, find an "alternates"
or "substitute" option.
steps.expected: An option to add alternates exists.

steps.action:
Add "Hardware-Bolt-M8-Alt" as an alternate (create it as a part
if it doesn't exist).
steps.expected:
Alternate is recorded. A future material issue against this BOM
can substitute the alternate without breaking the work order.

expected_overall: BOM allows alternates. Production can swap when needed.
pass_criteria: Alternate is recorded AND can be selected at issue time.

negative_variants.title: Reject alternate with mismatched UoM
negative_variants.action:
Try to add an alternate component whose UoM differs from the
primary's.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; alternates must share the BOM line's
UoM (or the system must explicitly handle conversion).
negative_variants.pass_criteria: UoM mismatch is surfaced before issue time.

title: Create the first customer record
goal:
Add the first customer so sales orders, quotes, and invoices can
reference them.
preconditions: Foundational records exist: at least one location, one work center, standard units of measure, the chart of accounts, at least one tax code, the first employee, a default calendar, and at least one fixed asset. (Phase 1 outcomes.)
Tax codes from P1-TAX-001 exist.

steps.action:
Find and open the customers area. Choose to create a new
customer.
steps.expected: The customer creation form appears.

steps.action:
Enter:
- Name: "ACME Industrial"
- Billing address and shipping address (can be the same)
- Primary contact and email
- Default tax code
- Payment terms: Net 30
- Credit limit: $50,000
steps.expected: The form accepts the values and saves.

steps.action: Open the new customer record.
steps.expected: All entered values are visible. The customer has a stable ID.

expected_overall:
The first customer exists and is selectable on quotes and sales
orders.
pass_criteria: Customer exists AND saved values match AND credit limit is set.

negative_variants.title: Reject negative credit limit
negative_variants.action: Try to save a customer with credit limit = -$5,000.
negative_variants.expected: Submission is blocked with a clear error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Negative credit limit rejected.

negative_variants.title: Reject duplicate customer name without confirmation
negative_variants.action: Try to create a second customer with the same name as the first.
negative_variants.expected:
Either the application blocks (if names are unique by rule), or
surfaces a duplicate-detection warning that lists the existing
match and requires explicit confirmation. Silent creation of a
duplicate is the failure mode.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: No silent duplicate AND user gets a chance to merge or cancel.

title: Configure a customer with multiple ship-to addresses
goal:
Verify a single customer record can carry multiple ship-to
addresses (warehouses, branch offices) selectable per order.
preconditions: The first customer record exists with billing and shipping addresses, primary contact, default tax code, payment terms, and a $50,000 credit limit. (Established by P2-CUST-001.)

steps.action:
Open the ACME Industrial customer record. Find the addresses
section.
steps.expected: A way to add additional addresses is visible.

steps.action:
Add two more ship-to addresses:
- "ACME Industrial - East Plant" with a different state
- "ACME Industrial - West DC" with a different state
steps.expected: Both addresses save.

steps.action: Mark one as the default ship-to.
steps.expected:
The default flag is recorded; future orders default to this
address but can be changed per order.

expected_overall:
Customer carries one billing address and three ship-to addresses,
one marked as default.
pass_criteria: Multiple addresses exist AND default is selectable.

negative_variants.title: Reject ship-to address with missing required fields
negative_variants.action: Try to add a ship-to address with no street or postal code.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a per-field error listing the missing
required parts.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Missing required address fields are individually identified.

negative_variants.title: Cannot delete a ship-to referenced by an open order
negative_variants.action:
Once an open sales order ships to a given address, try to
delete that address.
negative_variants.expected:
Deletion is blocked or warns, naming the order that depends
on the address.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Address deletion gated by open references.

title: Set a credit limit and verify enforcement
goal:
Confirm that credit limits configured on customers actually block
(or warn on) sales orders that would exceed them, and that there's
a controller-override path.
preconditions: The first customer record exists with billing and shipping addresses, primary contact, default tax code, payment terms, and a $50,000 credit limit. (Established by P2-CUST-001.)

steps.action:
Open ACME Industrial. Note the $50,000 credit limit. Open or
create a sales-order draft for this customer worth $20,000.
steps.expected:
The order saves without warning. Credit utilization shows
$20,000 of $50,000 used (or similar).

steps.action:
Create a second draft order for $40,000 (would exceed the
limit when combined with the first).
steps.expected:
The system warns the user that credit limit will be exceeded.
Either: (a) blocks submission, OR (b) routes for credit
manager approval, OR (c) allows with a visible flag for
controller review.

steps.action:
As a Controller user (or with override permission), approve
the over-limit order.
steps.expected:
Override is recorded with the user, timestamp, and reason.
Order proceeds.

expected_overall:
Credit limits are enforced (warned, blocked, or flagged) and
controller override is recorded with an audit trail.
pass_criteria:
Over-limit was flagged AND override required action AND override
is auditable.
why_this_matters:
ERPs that hard-block credit limits with no override mechanism are
unusable in real businesses where exceptions are routine. ERPs
with no enforcement at all expose the business to bad debt.
Both extremes are bugs.

negative_variants.title: Sales rep cannot self-approve own over-limit order
negative_variants.action:
As the same Sales / Account Manager who created the over-limit
order, attempt to apply the controller override.
negative_variants.expected:
The override is unavailable to the same user who created the
order. A Controller (or other authorized user) must apply it.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Self-approval of own over-limit orders is refused.

negative_variants.title: Override requires a documented reason
negative_variants.action:
As the Controller, attempt to override without entering a
reason in the comments / justification field.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; the override requires a recorded
justification for audit.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Override without reason is refused.

title: Create an international customer with foreign currency
goal:
Verify the system handles a non-USD customer correctly: invoices
and statements in their currency, FX gain/loss accounting on payment.
preconditions: The first customer record exists with billing and shipping addresses, primary contact, default tax code, payment terms, and a $50,000 credit limit. (Established by P2-CUST-001.)
Multi-currency is enabled in tenant settings (P0).

steps.action:
Create a customer "Munich Manufacturing GmbH" with:
- Address in Germany
- Currency: EUR
- VAT ID
- Tax code: appropriate for EU export
steps.expected: Customer saves.

steps.action: On a draft sales order for this customer, verify amounts are in EUR.
steps.expected: Pricing displays in EUR. Order total is in EUR.

expected_overall:
Non-USD customer is fully supported, with correct currency
defaults on derived records.
pass_criteria: Customer carries EUR currency AND derived order amounts respect it.

negative_variants.title: Reject malformed VAT ID
negative_variants.action:
Enter a VAT ID that does not match the country's published
format (e.g., a German VAT ID without the "DE" prefix).
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; the VAT ID format is validated per
country.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
Malformed VAT ID is refused or flagged with country-specific
guidance.

negative_variants.title: Cannot mix currencies on a single sales order
negative_variants.action:
On a draft sales order for the EUR customer, try to add a line
priced in USD.
negative_variants.expected:
The application either converts to the order currency at the
configured rate or blocks the mix with a clear error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Currency consistency is enforced on the order.

title: Capture a sales lead
goal:
Record a new sales opportunity from a webform, trade show, or
inbound call. The lead is not yet a customer.
preconditions: Foundational records exist: at least one location, one work center, standard units of measure, the chart of accounts, at least one tax code, the first employee, a default calendar, and at least one fixed asset. (Phase 1 outcomes.)

steps.action:
Find and open the leads area. (Common labels: "Leads,"
"Opportunities," "Prospects.")
steps.expected: The leads list appears.

steps.action:
Create a new lead:
- Company: "Northwest Conveyors Inc."
- Contact: name, email, phone
- Source: "Trade show — IMTS 2026"
- Estimated deal size
- Status: New
steps.expected: Lead saves and appears in the list.

expected_overall: The lead is captured with source attribution.
pass_criteria: Lead exists AND has a source AND has a contact.

negative_variants.title: Reject lead with no contact information
negative_variants.action: Try to save a lead with no email and no phone number.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "at least one contact method is
required" error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Lead without contact info refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject negative estimated deal size
negative_variants.action: Try to save a lead with estimated deal size of -$10,000.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a "must be non-negative" error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Negative deal size refused.

title: Qualify a lead and convert it to a customer
goal:
Move a qualified lead through to a real customer record without
losing the contact, source, or activity history.
preconditions: A sales lead has been captured with company, contact info, source attribution, and estimated deal size. (Established by P2-LEAD-001.)

steps.action: Open the Northwest Conveyors lead. Use the convert action.
steps.expected:
A conversion form appears asking for any extra info needed for
a real customer record (billing terms, credit limit, default tax,
etc.).

steps.action: Fill in the customer-side fields and confirm.
steps.expected:
A new customer record is created. The lead's contact, source,
and notes are carried into the customer record. The lead is
marked converted.

steps.action:
Open the new customer. Verify the lead's history is preserved
and visible.
steps.expected:
Activity from the lead phase (source, notes, dates) is on the
customer record.

expected_overall: Lead-to-customer conversion preserves history.
pass_criteria:
Customer exists AND original lead source visible AND lead notes
preserved.

negative_variants.title: Cannot convert an already-converted lead twice
negative_variants.action:
After converting Northwest Conveyors, try to convert the same
lead again.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is unavailable or surfaces a clear "already
converted" message linking to the resulting customer.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Double-conversion is refused.

negative_variants.title: Conversion requires the customer-side required fields
negative_variants.action:
Attempt to confirm conversion with the new customer's billing
address blank.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a per-field error naming each missing
required value.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Conversion without required customer fields is refused.

title: Create a raw material part
goal:
Add the first raw-material part to inventory. Will be referenced
by BOMs and POs.
preconditions: At least one unit of measure exists (P1-UOM-001).
Default inventory GL account exists (P1-GL-001).

steps.action:
Find and open the parts / items area. Choose to create a new
part.
steps.expected: The part creation form appears.

steps.action:
Enter:
- Part number: "RM-STEEL-1018-3X3"
- Description: "Cold-rolled steel bar, 1018, 3in x 3in x 12ft"
- Type: Raw material
- Unit of measure: foot
- Default vendor: Pacific Steel Supply
- Inventory GL account: from P1-GL-001
steps.expected: The form accepts the values and saves.

steps.action: Open the part. Verify all values are visible.
steps.expected:
Part exists with stable ID. Type, UoM, and default vendor are
visible.

expected_overall: Raw material part exists, ready to be referenced on BOMs and POs.
pass_criteria:
Part record exists AND default vendor links correctly AND UoM
is what was entered.

negative_variants.title: Reject duplicate part number
negative_variants.action:
Try to create a second part with part number
"RM-STEEL-1018-3X3".
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "part number already exists"
message identifying the existing record.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Duplicate part number refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject part with no unit of measure
negative_variants.action: Try to save a part with the UoM field cleared.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "unit of measure is required"
error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Save refused without UoM.

negative_variants.title: Reject part pointing at deactivated vendor
negative_variants.action:
Deactivate Pacific Steel Supply, then try to save a new part
using it as the default vendor.
negative_variants.expected:
The vendor is filtered out of the dropdown OR the save is
blocked with a clear "vendor is inactive" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: No part references an inactive vendor as its default.

title: Create a finished-goods part
goal:
Add a finished-goods part the business sells. This part will get
a BOM and a routing in subsequent cases.
preconditions: A raw material part record exists (e.g. RM-STEEL-1018-3X3) with type, unit of measure, default vendor, and inventory GL account. (Established by P2-PART-001.)

steps.action:
Create a new part:
- Part number: "FG-BRACKET-A1"
- Description: "Mounting bracket, model A1"
- Type: Finished goods
- Unit of measure: each
- Default sales GL account
steps.expected: Part saves.

steps.action: Set a list price on the part (e.g., $45.00).
steps.expected:
Price field accepts value. Sales orders will default to this
price unless overridden.

expected_overall: Finished-goods part exists with a list price.
pass_criteria:
Part exists AND has a list price AND is classified as finished
goods (not raw material).

negative_variants.title: Reject negative list price
negative_variants.action: Try to set the list price to -$45.00.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a "price must be non-negative" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Negative price refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject finished goods with no sales GL account
negative_variants.action:
Try to save a finished goods part with the sales GL account
blank.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns that finished goods require a sales
account.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: FG without sales account refused.

title: Enable lot tracking on a part
goal:
Configure a raw material to be lot-tracked, so receipts record
lot numbers and consumption can be traced back to a specific lot
for recall purposes.
preconditions: A raw material part record exists (e.g. RM-STEEL-1018-3X3) with type, unit of measure, default vendor, and inventory GL account. (Established by P2-PART-001.)

steps.action: Open RM-STEEL-1018-3X3. Find lot-tracking settings.
steps.expected: A toggle or option to enable lot tracking is present.

steps.action: Enable lot tracking. Save.
steps.expected:
Setting persists. Future receipts of this part will require
a lot number; future issues will track which lot was consumed.

expected_overall:
The part is lot-tracked. Subsequent transactions enforce lot
number entry.
pass_criteria: Lot tracking is enabled on the part AND the setting survives reload.
why_this_matters:
Recall traceability is the killer feature for lot tracking.
Without it, a quality issue with a steel lot means recalling
every product that might have used that material rather than
the specific products that did.

negative_variants.title: Cannot disable lot tracking once on-hand inventory exists
negative_variants.action:
Once any lot-tracked inventory has been received for the part,
try to disable lot tracking.
negative_variants.expected:
The change is blocked with a clear message that historical
inventory still requires lot context, or requires explicit
acknowledgment of breaking traceability.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Lot tracking cannot be silently disabled with on-hand stock.

title: Enable serial tracking on a finished part
goal:
Configure a finished good to be serial-tracked, useful for
warranty claims and field returns.
preconditions: A finished-goods part exists (e.g. FG-BRACKET-A1) with type, unit of measure, sales GL account, and a list price. (Established by P2-PART-002.)

steps.action: Open FG-BRACKET-A1. Find serial-tracking settings.
steps.expected:
A toggle or option for serial tracking is present, distinct
from lot tracking.

steps.action:
Enable serial tracking. Configure serial format if available
(e.g., "BRA-{YYYY}-{nnnnn}").
steps.expected:
Setting saves. Future production completions of this part
will require a serial number.

expected_overall:
Part is serial-tracked. Each finished unit will get a unique
serial number traceable through ship and warranty.
pass_criteria:
Serial tracking is enabled AND the format (if configured) is
applied on the next completion.

negative_variants.title: Reject malformed serial format pattern
negative_variants.action:
Enter a serial format pattern with mismatched braces or
unsupported tokens (e.g., "BRA-{ZZZZ}-{nnnn").
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear message identifying the bad
token / unmatched delimiter.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Malformed format pattern is refused with actionable detail.

negative_variants.title: Reject duplicate serial number on completion
negative_variants.action:
In a later phase, try to complete two units with the same
serial number.
negative_variants.expected:
The second completion is blocked with a clear "serial number
already in use" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Duplicate serial refused at completion.

title: Import a batch of parts from a spreadsheet
goal:
Verify the system supports bulk import of parts so a customer
migrating from another ERP doesn't have to type 500 parts by hand.
preconditions: At least one UoM and one GL account exist.

steps.action:
Find and open the parts import area. (Common labels: "Import,"
"Bulk Upload," "Data Import.")
steps.expected: An import flow exists with a downloadable template.

steps.action:
Download the template. Fill in 5 plausible parts (mix of raw
and finished). Upload the file.
steps.expected:
The system shows a preview of what will be imported, with any
errors flagged. If errors, they reference specific row and column.

steps.action: Confirm the import.
steps.expected: Parts are created. The list view shows the new entries.

expected_overall: Bulk import works end-to-end with clear error reporting on bad rows.
pass_criteria:
All 5 valid parts imported successfully AND any rejected rows
have actionable error messages.
why_this_matters:
Manual data entry for migration is the #1 reason ERP rollouts run
long. Working bulk import saves weeks.

negative_variants.title: Import file with malformed row
negative_variants.action:
Add a row with an invalid UoM ("xyz") to the import file.
Re-upload.
negative_variants.expected:
The system flags row N as having an invalid UoM. Other rows
are still importable. The user can fix and re-upload, or
proceed without the bad row.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
Bad row is flagged AND good rows are not blocked AND error
message names the field and the bad value.

title: Create a price list and apply it to selected customers
goal:
Verify multi-tier pricing — different list prices for different
customer segments (distribution, government, retail).
preconditions: At least one customer and one finished part exist.

steps.action:
Find and open the price lists area. Create a new list:
- Name: "Distributor Pricing"
- Currency: USD
- Effective date: today
steps.expected: Price list saves.

steps.action: Add a line: FG-BRACKET-A1 at $35.00 (a 22% discount from list).
steps.expected: Line saves under the Distributor Pricing list.

steps.action:
Open the ACME Industrial customer. Assign the Distributor
Pricing list as their default.
steps.expected:
Customer record now shows Distributor Pricing as their default
list.

steps.action: Create a draft sales order for ACME for FG-BRACKET-A1.
steps.expected:
Default unit price is $35.00 (from the list), not $45.00 (the
part's list price).

expected_overall:
Customer-specific pricing overrides default list prices on derived
records.
pass_criteria: Order line shows $35.00, not $45.00.

negative_variants.title: Reject price list with effective date in the past requiring re-pricing
negative_variants.action:
Save a price list with an effective date earlier than today
while open quotes exist.
negative_variants.expected:
The application surfaces affected open quotes and asks how to
handle them (re-price, leave as-is) rather than silently
changing pricing.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
Backdated price list cannot silently retroactively change open
quotes.

negative_variants.title: Customer assigned to multiple price lists picks one deterministically
negative_variants.action:
Assign two price lists to the same customer with conflicting
lines for the same part.
negative_variants.expected:
The application either rejects the dual assignment or applies
a documented precedence (e.g., most-recent effective date)
visible to the user.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Conflict resolution is explicit and documented.

title: Configure quantity-break pricing
goal:
Verify price tiers based on quantity (e.g., 1–9 units at $45,
10–49 at $40, 50+ at $35).
preconditions: A finished-goods part exists (e.g. FG-BRACKET-A1) with type, unit of measure, sales GL account, and a list price. (Established by P2-PART-002.)

steps.action:
Open FG-BRACKET-A1's pricing. Add quantity breaks:
- 1–9 each: $45.00
- 10–49 each: $40.00
- 50+ each: $35.00
steps.expected: Tiers save with their quantity ranges.

steps.action: Create draft order lines for 5, 25, and 100 units of the part.
steps.expected: Each line uses the right tier price based on quantity.

expected_overall:
Quantity breaks resolve to the correct price tier based on order
quantity.
pass_criteria: All three lines show the expected price per their quantity tier.

negative_variants.title: Reject overlapping quantity break tiers
negative_variants.action:
Try to save tiers "1-10 at $45" and "5-25 at $40" (overlapping
ranges).
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "ranges must not overlap" error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Overlapping tiers refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject ascending price across ascending quantity
negative_variants.action:
Try to save tiers where higher quantity costs more per unit
(e.g., 1-9 at $35, 50+ at $45).
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or surfaces an explicit warning that quantity
breaks are typically descending. The user must confirm the
unusual configuration.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Inverted price-per-quantity surfaces an explicit warning.

title: Configure vendor-specific cost terms for a part
goal:
Set per-vendor cost on a purchased part, including quantity breaks
if applicable. POs default to these costs.
preconditions: The first vendor record exists with name, address, primary contact, payment terms, and default expense / inventory GL account. (Established by P2-VENDOR-001.)
A raw material part record exists (e.g. RM-STEEL-1018-3X3) with type, unit of measure, default vendor, and inventory GL account. (Established by P2-PART-001.)

steps.action: Open RM-STEEL-1018-3X3. Find vendor pricing.
steps.expected: A way to add vendor-specific costs is visible.

steps.action:
Add Pacific Steel Supply as a vendor with cost $4.50/foot,
lead time 14 days, MOQ 100 feet.
steps.expected: Vendor pricing record saves.

steps.action: Create a draft PO line for this part to Pacific Steel.
steps.expected: PO line defaults to $4.50/foot.

expected_overall: Per-vendor costs override generic part costs on POs.
pass_criteria:
PO line cost matches the vendor pricing record AND lead time and MOQ
are visible.

negative_variants.title: Reject negative lead time or MOQ
negative_variants.action:
Try to save vendor pricing with lead time of -7 days or MOQ
of -50.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a "must be non-negative" error per field.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Negative lead time and MOQ refused.

negative_variants.title: PO below MOQ is flagged
negative_variants.action: Create a draft PO line for 50 feet (below MOQ of 100).
negative_variants.expected:
The application warns or blocks at submission, naming the MOQ
requirement.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Below-MOQ order surfaces a clear warning before submission.

title: Create a prototype part marked as not-for-sale
goal:
Verify R&D can create parts marked as prototype (or pre-release) so
they don't accidentally appear on quotes or production schedules.
preconditions: At least one UoM and GL account exist.

steps.action:
Create a part "PROTO-WIDGET-V1" with type: prototype (or a flag
indicating not-for-production / not-for-sale).
steps.expected: Part saves with the prototype indicator.

steps.action: Try to add this part as a line on a sales order.
steps.expected:
The system either blocks selection OR warns the user that this
is a prototype not approved for sale.

expected_overall:
Prototype parts are visibly distinct and gated from sales /
production transactions.
pass_criteria: Prototype indicator visible AND sale of prototype is gated.
why_this_matters:
Real bugs in this area: a salesperson finds a part in search and
quotes it without realizing it's pre-release. The deal closes,
the customer expects shipment, and there's no production-ready BOM.

negative_variants.title: Prototype cannot be added to a customer-facing quote
negative_variants.action:
Open a quote for ACME Industrial and try to add the prototype
part as a quote line.
negative_variants.expected:
The application either filters out prototype parts from quote
search OR rejects the line with a clear "not approved for sale"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Prototype part cannot be quoted to a customer.

title: Submit an engineering change request (ECR) and route for approval
goal:
Verify engineering changes go through a formal request, review, and
approval workflow before changing production BOMs/routings.
preconditions: A finished-goods part has a single-level BOM listing component parts, quantities-per-unit, and units of measure, marked effective. (Established by P2-BOM-001.)

steps.action:
Find the engineering change request area. Submit a new ECR
against FG-BRACKET-A1 — proposed change: change the bolt size
from M8 to M10. Provide a reason.
steps.expected: ECR saves in "Pending review" status.

steps.action:
Sign in as a different user with approval authority. Open the
ECR queue.
steps.expected: The pending ECR is visible.

steps.action: Approve the ECR.
steps.expected:
ECR status changes to "Approved." A new BOM revision is either
auto-created or a follow-up task is generated.

expected_overall: ECR workflow exists: submit → review → approve → BOM revision.
pass_criteria:
ECR was submitted AND went through approval AND resulted in a
trackable BOM change.

negative_variants.title: ECR submitter cannot self-approve
negative_variants.action: As the same user who submitted the ECR, attempt to approve it.
negative_variants.expected:
The approve action is unavailable to the submitter. A
separate authorized user must approve.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Self-approval of own ECR is refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject ECR with no proposed change description
negative_variants.action:
Try to submit an ECR with the description / change field
blank.
negative_variants.expected:
Submission is blocked with a clear "description is required"
error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Empty ECR refused.

title: Create a prototype tied to a customer feedback record
goal:
When a customer requests a custom variant, the prototype work
should be traceable back to the customer / opportunity that drove it.
preconditions: At least one customer exists (P2-CUST-001).
Prototype part type exists (P2-RD-001).

steps.action:
Open ACME Industrial's customer record. Find a way to record a
feature request or feedback note.
steps.expected: A field or sub-record for feedback exists.

steps.action: Add a note: "Wants taller bracket variant for new product line."
steps.expected: Note saves with date and originator.

steps.action:
Create prototype part "PROTO-BRACKET-TALL" and link it to the
ACME feedback record.
steps.expected:
Prototype carries a back-link to the originating customer
feedback.

expected_overall:
Customer-driven prototypes are traceable from the customer record
forward to the prototype, and from the prototype back to the customer.
pass_criteria: Bi-directional link exists AND is visible from both ends.
notes:
Customer-feedback-to-prototype traceability is the foundation for
reporting on R&D ROI by customer / segment. Without it, leadership
can't answer "which customers are driving our product roadmap?"

negative_variants.title: Cannot link prototype to deactivated customer
negative_variants.action:
Deactivate ACME Industrial and try to create a prototype
linked to its feedback.
negative_variants.expected: The link is blocked or warns that the customer is inactive.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Inactive customer cannot be referenced.

title: Promote a prototype to a production-ready part
goal:
Once a prototype is validated, formally promote it to a production
part. The prototype's BOM and routing become the released versions.
preconditions: A prototype part exists, marked not-for-production / not-for-sale and gated from sales orders. (Established by P2-RD-001.)
The prototype has a BOM and routing.

steps.action:
Open the prototype part. Find the "promote to production" action
(or equivalent — "release," "approve," "graduate").
steps.expected:
The action is available; system prompts for any release info
(revision, effective date, approval signoff).

steps.action: Confirm the promotion.
steps.expected:
Part type changes from prototype to standard. The part is now
sellable and orderable. Original prototype history is preserved.

expected_overall: Prototype is promoted; sellable and orderable as a standard part.
pass_criteria: Type changed AND sellable AND prototype history retained.

negative_variants.title: Reject promotion of prototype without released BOM and routing
negative_variants.action:
Try to promote a prototype that has either no BOM or no
routing.
negative_variants.expected: Promotion is blocked with a clear list of missing prerequisites.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Promotion gated by complete BOM and routing.

negative_variants.title: Promotion requires approver signoff
negative_variants.action: Attempt to promote without an approval signature recorded.
negative_variants.expected: Promotion is blocked or warns that approval is required.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Promotion without signoff refused.

title: Create a routing for FG-BRACKET-A1
goal:
Define the operation sequence required to produce a finished part —
which work centers, in what order, with what setup and run times.
preconditions: A finished-goods part has a single-level BOM listing component parts, quantities-per-unit, and units of measure, marked effective. (Established by P2-BOM-001.)
Work centers from P1-WC-001/002 exist.

steps.action: Open FG-BRACKET-A1. Find the routing section. Add a new routing.
steps.expected: A routing editor opens with rows for operations.

steps.action:
Add operations in order:
1. "Cut" at Cut & Saw (setup 10 min, run 2 min/each)
2. "Weld" at Weld (setup 5 min, run 5 min/each)
3. "Paint" at Paint (setup 30 min, run 1 min/each)
4. "Inspect" at Inspection (setup 0 min, run 1 min/each)
steps.expected: Each operation accepts work center, setup, and run-time values.

steps.action: Save the routing. Mark it as effective.
steps.expected:
Routing is active. Work orders for this part will use this
routing by default.

expected_overall:
Routing exists. A work order generates 4 ordered operations at the
named work centers with the configured times.
pass_criteria:
All 4 operations exist in correct order AND each links to its work
center AND time values match what was entered.

negative_variants.title: Reject negative setup or run time
negative_variants.action:
Try to save an operation with setup of -10 minutes or run of
-2 minutes.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a "times must be non-negative" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Negative time values refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject operation pointing at deleted work center
negative_variants.action:
Try to save a routing operation referencing a work center that
has been deactivated.
negative_variants.expected:
The dropdown excludes inactive work centers, or the save is
blocked with a clear message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Routing cannot point at an inactive work center.

negative_variants.title: Reject routing with no operations
negative_variants.action: Try to save a routing with zero operations.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "at least one operation is
required" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Empty routing refused.

title: Create a routing that includes a subcontracted operation
goal:
Verify a routing can include an operation performed by an outside
vendor (heat treatment, plating). Material is shipped out and
comes back to continue.
preconditions: A finished-goods part has a routing with ordered operations at the right work centers, including setup and run times for each. (Established by P2-ROUTE-001.)
At least one vendor that does subcontract work exists (or can be added).

steps.action:
Create a part "FG-SHAFT-X1" with a BOM. Open its routing.
Add operations:
1. Cut at Cut & Saw
2. Heat Treat (subcontracted) — vendor: a vendor of your choice
   (or create one), turn-time: 5 days
3. Final inspection at Inspection
steps.expected:
The subcontracted operation accepts a vendor, turn-time, and
cost. The system understands the operation involves shipping out
and receiving back.

steps.action: Save the routing.
steps.expected:
Routing saves. Work orders against this part will include
automatic shipping-to-vendor and receiving-from-vendor steps
around the subcontracted operation.

expected_overall:
Routing supports subcontracted operations end-to-end with vendor,
turn-time, and cost.
pass_criteria:
Subcontract operation exists AND links to a vendor AND has a
turn-time AND has a defined cost or pricing.
why_this_matters:
Many shops can't do every operation in-house. Routing must
natively understand "this part leaves the building, comes back,
and continues" or every subcontract becomes a manual one-off.

negative_variants.title: Reject subcontract op without vendor
negative_variants.action: Try to save a subcontract operation with no vendor selected.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "vendor is required for
subcontract operations" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Subcontract op without vendor refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject subcontract turn-time of zero
negative_variants.action: Set the subcontract operation's turn-time to 0 days.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; subcontracts that take no time are
almost always a misconfiguration.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Zero turn-time refused or flagged.

title: Configure parallel operations in a routing
goal:
Verify a routing supports operations that can happen at the same
time (parts that get split into two parallel paths and rejoin).
preconditions: A finished-goods part has a routing with ordered operations at the right work centers, including setup and run times for each. (Established by P2-ROUTE-001.)

steps.action:
In a complex routing, configure two operations as parallel
(e.g., "Machine Side A" and "Machine Side B" can run
concurrently after "Cut" and before "Assembly").
steps.expected:
The routing editor accepts parallel sequencing — visualizes
the branch and merge.

steps.action: Save the routing. Generate a work order from it.
steps.expected:
Work order shows both parallel operations as available
simultaneously.

expected_overall: Parallel operations are supported and visualized correctly.
pass_criteria:
Both parallel operations are released for work simultaneously
AND the routing diagram shows the branch.
notes:
Some ERPs implement only sequential routings. If parallel routing
doesn't work, that's a real limitation worth documenting; flag it
as a bug rather than working around it.

negative_variants.title: Parallel branches must merge before downstream operations
negative_variants.action:
Try to save a routing where one branch has a downstream
operation that does not wait for the other branch to complete.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; downstream operations must explicitly
depend on the merge of all parallel branches.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
Branches merge cleanly before downstream operations are
released.

title: Create the first vendor record
goal:
Add the first supplier so purchase orders can later be issued
against them.
preconditions: Foundational records exist: at least one location, work center, standard UoMs, the chart of accounts, at least one tax code, employees, calendar, and asset records. (Phase 1 outcomes.)
You are signed in as a user with permission to manage vendors.

steps.action:
Find and open the vendors area. (Common labels: "Vendors,"
"Suppliers," "Payees.")
steps.expected: The vendors list appears, empty by default.

steps.action:
Choose to create a new vendor. Enter:
- Name: "Pacific Steel Supply"
- Address: a plausible address in the US
- Primary contact name and email
- Payment terms: Net 30
- Default expense / inventory GL account
steps.expected: The form accepts the values and saves.

steps.action: Open the vendor record you just created.
steps.expected:
All entered values are visible. The vendor has a stable ID
visible in the URL or on the record.

expected_overall:
The first vendor exists and is selectable on POs and other
vendor-referencing records.
pass_criteria: Vendor exists AND has a unique ID AND saved values match what was entered.

negative_variants.title: Reject vendor without required address fields
negative_variants.action: Try to save a vendor with name only (no address, no contact).
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a list of missing required fields in plain
language.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Save blocked AND missing fields are named.

negative_variants.title: Reject invalid email on contact
negative_variants.action: Enter "not-an-email" in the contact email field. Try to save.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns with a clear "valid email required"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Save blocked or warned AND error message is plain.

title: Add multiple contacts to a vendor
goal:
Verify a vendor record supports more than one contact (a sales rep,
an AP contact, a technical contact) — common for any non-trivial
supplier relationship.
preconditions: The first vendor record exists with name, address, primary contact, payment terms, and default expense / inventory GL account. (Established by P2-VENDOR-001.)

steps.action:
Open the Pacific Steel Supply vendor record. Find the contacts
section.
steps.expected: A way to view and add contacts is visible.

steps.action:
Add a second contact:
- Name, email, phone
- Role / type: "Accounts Receivable" or similar
steps.expected: The second contact saves and appears alongside the primary.

steps.action:
Mark the original contact as the default for purchasing
communications and the AR contact as the default for invoicing.
steps.expected:
Each contact's role is recorded. Future emails can be routed
to the right person.

expected_overall:
The vendor has multiple contacts with distinct roles, each
addressable separately for different kinds of communication.
pass_criteria:
Two or more contacts exist AND each has a distinct role AND the
defaults can be set per-purpose.

negative_variants.title: Cannot demote the only default contact for a purpose
negative_variants.action:
Remove the "AR contact" flag from the only contact carrying
it, leaving no AR default.
negative_variants.expected:
The change is blocked or warns; an explicit reassignment is
required to leave a vendor without an AR contact.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
Vendor never silently ends up with no default for a required
contact role.

negative_variants.title: Reject duplicate contact email on same vendor
negative_variants.action: Add a second contact with the same email as an existing one.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; duplicate emails are surfaced before
they confuse routing.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Duplicate contact email refused or flagged.

title: Set per-vendor payment terms and currency
goal:
Confirm payment terms (Net 30, 2/10 Net 30, etc.) and currency are
configurable per vendor and override company defaults.
preconditions: The first vendor record exists with name, address, primary contact, payment terms, and default expense / inventory GL account. (Established by P2-VENDOR-001.)

steps.action:
Open the Pacific Steel Supply vendor. Find payment terms and
currency.
steps.expected: Both fields are present and editable.

steps.action:
Change terms to "2/10 Net 30" (2% discount if paid within 10
days, full payment due in 30).
steps.expected: Terms accept the format and save.

steps.action:
Create a second vendor "Tokyo Bearings Co." with currency JPY
and terms Net 60.
steps.expected:
The vendor saves with non-USD currency. POs against this vendor
will be in JPY by default.

expected_overall:
Payment terms and currency are per-vendor, not a single
company-wide value.
pass_criteria:
Each vendor has independent terms AND currency AND the values
drive defaults on subsequent POs.
why_this_matters:
Real businesses have different terms with different vendors and
often deal with multi-currency. ERPs that hard-code a single
currency or single terms break this from day one.

negative_variants.title: Reject malformed payment terms
negative_variants.action:
Try to enter terms in an unparseable format ("pay whenever",
"Net negative-30").
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear error explaining the expected
format options.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Bad terms refused AND format guidance is given.

negative_variants.title: Cannot change vendor currency once a PO exists
negative_variants.action:
After at least one PO references this vendor (in a later
phase), return and try to change the vendor's currency.
negative_variants.expected:
The change is blocked or requires explicit migration of open
transactions. Silent currency change must not affect existing
records.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Currency change post-transactions is gated.

negative_variants.title: Reject discount percent above 100
negative_variants.action: Try to save terms "150/10 Net 30" (150% discount).
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear out-of-range error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Discount above 100% refused.

title: Track 1099 / W-9 status on vendors (US)
goal:
Confirm the system tracks tax-form status (W-9 received, 1099-eligible)
per vendor so year-end 1099 reporting is possible.
preconditions: The first vendor record exists with name, address, primary contact, payment terms, and default expense / inventory GL account. (Established by P2-VENDOR-001.)

steps.action: Open a vendor record. Find tax-form fields.
steps.expected:
A way to record W-9 status, EIN/SSN, and 1099 eligibility
is present.

steps.action:
For Pacific Steel Supply, mark W-9 received with today's date
and EIN visible. Set 1099-eligible: No (corporation).
steps.expected: Values save.

steps.action:
Create a third vendor "Jane Doe Welding" (sole proprietor),
mark W-9 received, 1099-eligible: Yes.
steps.expected:
Vendor saves. The 1099-eligible flag is visible on a vendor list
filter or report.

expected_overall: Each vendor's tax-form status and 1099 eligibility are tracked.
pass_criteria:
Both vendors have W-9 dates AND eligibility flags AND can be
filtered by 1099 status.
notes:
This case is US-specific. International deployments may track
equivalent per-jurisdiction info (W-8BEN for foreign vendors,
GST/VAT registration, etc.).

negative_variants.title: Reject malformed EIN
negative_variants.action:
Enter an EIN with the wrong digit count (e.g., "12-345" or
"1234567890").
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "EIN must be 9 digits" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Malformed EIN refused.

negative_variants.title: 1099-eligible vendor cannot save without TIN
negative_variants.action: Mark a vendor 1099-eligible but leave the EIN/SSN blank. Save.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns that 1099 reporting requires a TIN
on file.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
1099-eligible without TIN is refused or surfaces an explicit
warning.

title: Deactivate a vendor without deleting historical activity
goal:
Verify vendors can be deactivated to prevent new POs while
preserving historical transactions.
preconditions: At least two vendors exist.

steps.action: Open a vendor. Use the deactivate or disable action.
steps.expected: Confirmation dialog appears. Confirm.

steps.action: Try to create a new PO and select the deactivated vendor.
steps.expected:
The deactivated vendor either does not appear in the dropdown
OR appears with a clear "Inactive" indicator and the system
blocks selecting them.

steps.action: Open the historical activity for the deactivated vendor.
steps.expected: Past POs, payments, and other records are intact.

expected_overall:
Deactivated vendors are unusable for new transactions but their
history remains.
pass_criteria: New POs blocked AND historical records preserved.

negative_variants.title: Cannot deactivate vendor with open POs
negative_variants.action: Once an open PO exists for the vendor, try to deactivate them.
negative_variants.expected:
Deactivation is blocked or warns, naming the open POs that
must be closed or reassigned first.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Deactivation gated by open transactions.

negative_variants.title: Reactivation restores selectability
negative_variants.action: Reactivate the deactivated vendor. Try to create a new PO.
negative_variants.expected:
Vendor is selectable again. No phantom "deactivated" flag
remains on the record.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Reactivation is clean and complete.

title: 3-way match a vendor invoice to PO and receipt
goal:
Verify the standard 3-way match — invoice from vendor matches the
PO (price) and the receipt (quantity) before payment is approved.
preconditions: A purchase order has been issued to a vendor, has a stable PO number, and has at least one line for a part with quantity and price. (Established by P3-PO-001.)

First inventory exists from a vendor receipt: the PO is in Received or Closed status, lot numbers have been recorded for lot-tracked items, and GR/IR is pending the vendor invoice. (Established by P3-RECV-001.)

steps.action:
Find the AP invoice entry area. Enter a vendor invoice from
Pacific Steel Supply: 500 ft at $4.50/ft, total $2,250.
steps.expected: Form prompts for PO/receipt matching.

steps.action: Match against the PO and receipt from earlier.
steps.expected:
The system shows that price ($4.50/ft = PO), quantity
(500 ft = receipt), and total ($2,250) all match. Invoice
is ready for approval.

steps.action:
Approve the invoice for payment. Verify GL impact: GR/IR
cleared, AP credited.
steps.expected: AP increases. GR/IR clears. Invoice scheduled per terms.

expected_overall: 3-way match works correctly. GR/IR clears at invoice receipt.
pass_criteria: Match passed AND AP entry posted AND GR/IR account cleared.

negative_variants.title: Invoice with price mismatch
negative_variants.action: Enter a vendor invoice with a price of $5.00/ft (PO had $4.50).
negative_variants.expected:
System flags the price mismatch. Either blocks approval or
routes to controller for tolerance approval.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Mismatch flagged AND requires extra approval to proceed.

negative_variants.title: Reject invoice dated before the PO date
negative_variants.action: Enter a vendor invoice dated one week before the PO's issue date.
negative_variants.expected:
System flags the date mismatch. Either blocks the entry or
requires controller override with reason.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Backdate vs. PO is flagged AND override is auditable.

negative_variants.title: Reject duplicate vendor invoice number
negative_variants.action:
