negative_variants.action: Override the price to a value below the part's standard cost.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or routes for controller approval. The
below-cost condition is named explicitly.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Below-cost override is gated.

negative_variants.title: Reject override with no documented reason
negative_variants.action: Override the price without entering an override reason.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked or warns that overrides require a reason.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Reason-less override refused.

title: Convert an accepted quote to a sales order
goal:
Move from quote to sales order — the customer agreed, now we
commit production and delivery.
preconditions: A quote has been created for a customer with the right customer-list pricing applied, validity dates set, and the quote sent. (Established by P4-QUOTE-001.)

steps.action: Open the quote. Use the convert-to-SO action.
steps.expected:
A new sales order is created with all quote lines, prices, and
terms carried over.

steps.action:
Add or confirm the customer PO number, requested ship date,
and ship-to address.
steps.expected: All values save.

steps.action: Submit / confirm the SO.
steps.expected:
SO transitions to "Open" or "Booked." Quote is marked as
converted.

expected_overall: SO exists and references the original quote for traceability.
pass_criteria:
SO is open AND links to the original quote AND carries all the
quote's pricing.

negative_variants.title: Cannot convert an expired quote
negative_variants.action:
Set the quote's validity to a date in the past, then try to
convert.
negative_variants.expected:
The convert action is blocked or warns; expired quotes require
explicit re-issue or extension.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Expired quote conversion is gated.

negative_variants.title: Cannot convert the same quote twice
negative_variants.action: Convert the quote, then attempt to convert it again.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is unavailable or surfaces a clear "already
converted" message linking to the SO.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Double conversion refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject SO without a customer PO number when required
negative_variants.action:
Confirm the SO without entering the customer's PO number on a
customer that requires PO references.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; PO reference is enforced per
customer setting.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Missing required PO is surfaced before SO confirmation.

title: Revise a sent quote and preserve the prior version
goal:
Verify a quote that has been sent to a customer can be revised
without losing the original — both versions are preserved, the
customer-facing copy clearly shows it's revision 2, and prior
revisions remain auditable.
preconditions: At least one sent quote exists.

steps.action: Open a sent quote. Use the revise / amend action.
steps.expected:
A new revision (e.g., Rev B) is staged. The original is
preserved.

steps.action: Change one line price. Add a new line. Save and re-send.
steps.expected:
Customer-facing document shows Rev B clearly. Quote number
stays the same; revision letter / number increments.

steps.action: Open the quote history.
steps.expected:
Both revisions visible. The customer can be told which revision
they should look at.

expected_overall:
Quote revisions preserve prior versions and clearly mark the active
one.
pass_criteria: Both revisions visible AND active rev marked AND prior rev is read-only.

title: A quote past its validity date cannot be converted without re-acceptance
goal:
Verify that a quote whose validity date has passed cannot be
converted to an SO without explicit re-acceptance / re-issue, and
is visually flagged as expired in the quote list.
preconditions: At least one quote with a validity period exists.

steps.action:
Find or create a quote with validity ending yesterday (or
backdate the quote so its window has lapsed).
steps.expected: Quote saves.

steps.action: Open the quote list.
steps.expected:
The expired quote is visibly marked (e.g., "Expired" badge or
strikethrough date).

steps.action: Try to convert the expired quote to an SO.
steps.expected:
Conversion is blocked or warned, with an option to re-issue
the quote (extend validity) before converting.

steps.action: Re-issue the quote with a new validity. Convert to SO.
steps.expected: New conversion succeeds.

expected_overall: Expiration is enforced; re-issue is the proper path forward.
pass_criteria:
Expired conversion blocked AND re-issue path works AND list flags
expired status.

title: Ship the order
goal:
Hand off the packed order to a carrier — domestic or international —
capturing tracking information and triggering the customer invoice.
preconditions: The order is packed into shipping containers, the packing slip has been generated, and items have been verified against the SO. (Established by P4-PACK.)
A shipping integration is configured (P0-INTEG-002) — or manual shipment entry.

steps.action:
Open the ship area. Select the packed order. Choose a carrier
and service level (or use the default).
steps.expected:
System produces a shipping label and tracking number — either
via carrier integration or manual entry.

steps.action: Confirm the ship.
steps.expected:
SO marks as shipped. Tracking number is recorded on the SO.
Inventory clears the staging area. The customer is (optionally)
notified.

steps.action:
Verify the next step: an invoice is either auto-generated or
surfaces in the AR queue.
steps.expected: Invoice is created from the shipped SO.

expected_overall:
Shipment is on its way. SO is shipped. Customer invoice is queued
or created.
pass_criteria: Shipment created AND tracking number recorded AND invoice triggered.

negative_variants.title: Reject ship without packing complete
negative_variants.action: Try to ship before the order has been packed.
negative_variants.expected:
The ship action is unavailable or blocked with a "pack first"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Ship-before-pack refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject ship to address with no postal validation
negative_variants.action:
Try to ship to an address missing postal code or with a
malformed one.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; the carrier cannot accept the
shipment without a valid postal code.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Bad ship-to address surfaces a clear validation error.

title: Ship internationally with customs documentation
goal:
Verify international shipments produce the right customs paperwork
(commercial invoice, harmonized codes, country-of-origin).
preconditions: At least one international customer (P2-CUST-004).
That customer has an SO ready to ship.

steps.action:
In the ship workflow for the Munich Manufacturing GmbH order,
verify the customs documentation prompts appear.
steps.expected:
Form requests harmonized tariff codes, country of origin,
commercial-invoice value, and incoterm.

steps.action: Fill in the required fields and confirm.
steps.expected:
Commercial invoice and any other required documents (e.g.,
certificate of origin) are generated.

expected_overall: International shipments produce complete customs paperwork.
pass_criteria:
Commercial invoice generated AND harmonized codes captured AND
incoterm recorded.

negative_variants.title: Reject international shipment without harmonized code
negative_variants.action:
Try to confirm the shipment leaving the harmonized tariff code
blank on at least one line.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear list of lines missing the code.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Missing tariff codes refused at ship.

negative_variants.title: Reject incoterm not in the supported list
negative_variants.action:
Try to free-text an incoterm value (e.g., "DDPish") rather
than picking from the controlled list.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or restricts input to the supported set
(EXW, FOB, DAP, DDP, etc.).
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Invalid incoterm refused.

title: An SO ships in two partial shipments, each with its own invoice
goal:
Verify a sales order can ship in multiple deliveries, each with
its own packing slip and invoice, with the SO remaining open until
fully shipped.
preconditions: A confirmed SO with a quantity that can be split exists.
Inventory is partially available (e.g., 60 of 100 on hand).

steps.action:
Pick, pack, and ship 60 of the 100 ordered units. Generate
shipment 1.
steps.expected:
First packing slip and tracking are produced. SO remains open
with 40 units on backorder.

steps.action: Generate the partial invoice for the 60 shipped.
steps.expected: Invoice 1 posts for the 60 units. AR increases.

steps.action:
Once 40 more units become available, pick / pack / ship them
as shipment 2.
steps.expected: Second packing slip generated. SO closes after this shipment.

steps.action: Generate invoice 2 for the remaining 40.
steps.expected: Invoice 2 posts. SO is fully invoiced.

expected_overall:
Split shipments produce separate invoices and close the SO when
fully fulfilled.
pass_criteria:
Two shipments AND two invoices AND SO closes only after second
shipment.

title: Release a work order from a sales order
goal:
Generate and release a work order to fulfill the SO. The WO carries
the BOM and routing from the part's master data and is assigned to
the appropriate work centers.
preconditions: A sales order has been created from an accepted quote, in Open or Booked status, with customer PO number, requested ship date, and ship-to address all confirmed. (Established by P4-QUOTE-003.)
The part on the SO has a BOM and routing.

steps.action:
Open the sales order. Use the "release to production" or
"create work order" action.
steps.expected:
Work order is generated. WO number, target quantity, BOM
revision, and routing are all populated.

steps.action:
Verify the operations match the part's routing — Cut, Weld,
Paint, Inspect at the right work centers.
steps.expected:
All operations are present in the right order with the work
centers from P2-ROUTE-001.

steps.action: Release the WO to production.
steps.expected:
WO status changes from "Planned" to "Released" (or equivalent).
It now appears on the floor's work-to-do list.

expected_overall:
Work order is on the floor, with BOM and routing locked at the
revision active when released.
pass_criteria:
WO released AND links to its source SO AND its BOM/routing
revisions match what was active at release.

negative_variants.title: Warn on insufficient material at release
negative_variants.action:
Reduce on-hand of a BOM component below what the WO would need.
Attempt to release the WO.
negative_variants.expected:
Application warns about projected material shortage, and either
blocks release until material is on order, or releases with a
clear "shortage" flag visible to the floor and planner.
negative_variants.pass_criteria:
Shortage was surfaced AND release outcome is explicit (blocked
or flagged), not silent.

negative_variants.title: Reject release without an active routing
negative_variants.action:
Mark the part's routing as draft / inactive (or pick a part
without a routing). Try to release.
negative_variants.expected: Release is blocked with a clear "no active routing" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Release blocked AND error names missing routing.

moot.reason:
Per Option A SO/Job unification (WU-18 locked decision), Job is the
canonical work-execution entity and a stage transition replaces
"release WO from SO". The case wording assumes Option B
architecture (separate WO entity created from SO). qb-engineer does
not have a "release WO from SO" endpoint by design.
moot.consultant_guidance:
WO release is a Job stage transition, not a separate endpoint. The
Sales Order is the same Job at the "Order Confirmed" stage; moving
the Job into a production-released stage performs the operation
this case targets. Use the Job stage-transition surface rather than
looking for a /release endpoint on Sales Order.
moot.alternative_behavior:
POST /jobs creates the Job (= SO + WO unified). Stage transitions
on the Job carry the work into production. BOM/routing snapshots at
the stage transition time supply the "lock at release" semantic.

title: Verify the work order appears on the kanban / sprint board
goal:
When a WO is released, it should show up as a card on the
kanban-style task board so the production team can see and pick
it up.
preconditions: A work order has been released from a sales order. The WO carries a stable number, references the BOM and routing active at release, and is in Released status — visible to the floor but not yet started. (Established by P4-WO-001.)

steps.action: Open the kanban / task board for the production area.
steps.expected:
The newly released WO appears as a card in the appropriate
"Ready" or "Released" column.

steps.action: Open the card.
steps.expected:
Card shows: WO number, part, target quantity, due date, current
operation, work center.

expected_overall: Released WOs are surfaced as kanban cards for the floor to pick up.
pass_criteria:
WO is visible on the board AND card has identifying details AND
state matches the WO's released status.

negative_variants.title: Closed WOs do not appear on the active kanban board
negative_variants.action:
Close (or cancel) a WO. Open the kanban board for that
production area.
negative_variants.expected:
The closed WO is not shown on the active board; it appears
only in a "completed" or filtered view.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Active board does not show closed WOs.

title: A WO configured to backflush consumes BOM materials at completion
goal:
Verify that a part / WO marked for backflush automatically consumes
BOM-defined component quantities from inventory at completion time,
rather than requiring an explicit issue per operation.
preconditions: At least one finished part has its BOM configured for backflush.
A WO for that part is released and at completion.

steps.action: Note the inventory of the BOM components before completion.
steps.expected: Quantities visible.

steps.action:
Complete the final operation on the WO with good quantity, e.g.,
100 units.
steps.expected:
Completion accepts. The BOM components are automatically
consumed at the per-unit quantity × 100 — no explicit issue
step required.

steps.action: Verify component inventory dropped by the expected amount.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action:
For lot-tracked components, verify a lot was consumed (FEFO
default).
steps.expected: Lot trace recorded.

expected_overall: Backflush correctly consumes materials at completion.
pass_criteria:
Inventory dropped by exact BOM-extended quantity AND lot trace
recorded for lot-tracked components.

title: Start the first operation on a released work order
goal:
Begin work at the floor: scan or pick the WO, identify the work
center, and transition the operation from Released to In Progress.
preconditions: A work order has been released from a sales order. The WO carries a stable number, references the BOM and routing active at release, and is in Released status — visible to the floor but not yet started. (Established by P4-WO-001.)

steps.action:
At the work center's station (paired scanner workstation, or
mobile device). Sign in or scan badge. Scan the WO ID. Scan
the start barcode (or tap the start action).
steps.expected:
The combination of barcodes resolves: this WO, this work
center, this operator, start. Operation transitions to
"In Progress."

steps.action: Verify the kanban card has moved from "Ready" to "In Progress."
steps.expected:
The card moved automatically without anyone touching the kanban
UI.

steps.action:
Verify labor time is now being tracked against the WO and
operator.
steps.expected: A labor entry is open and accumulating against the operator.

expected_overall: Operation is in progress. Labor is tracking. Kanban reflects status.
pass_criteria:
Status changed AND operator recorded AND labor time accumulating
AND kanban moved.

negative_variants.title: Cannot start an operation already in progress on another operator
negative_variants.action:
As a second operator, scan the same operation that another
operator has already started.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked or warns; the operation cannot have two
simultaneous owners (or requires explicit handoff).
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Duplicate start is refused or routed through a handoff flow.

negative_variants.title: Cannot start an operation out of routing order
negative_variants.action:
Try to start the second routing operation while the first is
still released.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked with a clear "previous operation must
complete first" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Out-of-order start refused.

negative_variants.title: Cannot start without scanning operator badge
negative_variants.action:
Try to scan the WO and start without first identifying the
operator.
negative_variants.expected: The start action is blocked until an operator is identified.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Anonymous start refused.

title: Review and explain WO variance before closing the work order
goal:
Verify that when a WO closes, the system surfaces material and
labor variance vs. standard, and the user can attach a variance
reason before the close commits.
preconditions: A WO has completed all operations and is ready to close.

steps.action: Open a completed WO ready to close. Open the variance summary.
steps.expected:
Material variance (actual issue cost - standard BOM cost),
labor variance (actual hours × rate - standard hours × rate),
and total cost variance are all shown.

steps.action:
For non-zero variance, attach a reason code (e.g., "Setup
scrap higher than standard").
steps.expected: Reason saves on the WO.

steps.action: Close the WO.
steps.expected:
WO closes. Variance posts to the appropriate variance accounts
(material variance, labor variance) per the costing model.

expected_overall: WO variance is surfaced, explained, and posted to GL.
pass_criteria: Variance correct AND reason captured AND GL postings tie.

title: Configure declining-balance depreciation on a fixed asset
goal:
Verify the application supports depreciation methods beyond
straight-line — specifically declining balance (e.g., 200% DB) —
and that period postings match the method's math.
preconditions: At least one fixed asset is being created or can have its method changed (typically only at creation).

steps.action:
Create a new asset with method = 200% declining balance,
useful life 5 years, cost $10,000.
steps.expected:
Asset saves. Schedule shows year-1 depreciation ≈ $4,000
(40% of $10,000), year-2 ≈ $2,400 (40% of $6,000 remaining),
etc.

steps.action: Run period depreciation for the first period.
steps.expected: Posting equals (year-1 depreciation / 12) for a monthly close.

steps.action: Verify GL posting is correct.
steps.expected:
Depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation post per the
method.

expected_overall: Declining-balance depreciation works correctly.
pass_criteria:
Schedule matches method math AND period posting equals expected
amount.
notes:
Many ERPs ship straight-line only. If declining balance is not
available, that's a real limitation — flag rather than soften.

title: Retire a fixed asset (dispose, scrap, sell)
goal:
Verify retirement of a fixed asset stops further depreciation,
posts the appropriate gain or loss on disposal, and removes the
asset from operational lists (PM scheduling, work order references).
preconditions: At least one fixed asset has been depreciating (P5-CLOSE-003).

steps.action:
Open the asset. Use the retire / dispose action. Choose method:
"Sold" with proceeds = $5,000.
steps.expected:
Retirement is staged. The application computes net book value
vs. proceeds and shows the resulting gain or loss.

steps.action: Confirm.
steps.expected:
Asset transitions to Retired. GL impact: cost and accumulated
depreciation cleared, cash debited for proceeds, and gain or
loss posted to the right account.

steps.action: Run period depreciation again.
steps.expected: The retired asset is excluded — no further depreciation posts.

steps.action: Try to schedule a PM against the retired asset.
steps.expected: Operation is blocked with a clear "asset retired" message.

expected_overall:
Retirement stops depreciation, posts disposal gain/loss, and gates
operational use.
pass_criteria:
Asset retired AND gain/loss posted AND no further depreciation AND
PM scheduling blocked.

title: Transfer a fixed asset to another location with audit trail
goal:
Verify a fixed asset can be transferred to a different location and
/ or work center, the transfer is recorded with date and reason,
and depreciation continues uninterrupted.
preconditions: At least one fixed asset exists at a known location (P1-ASSET-001).
At least two locations exist (P1-LOC-002).

steps.action:
Open the asset Press 1. Use the transfer / move action. Set
destination location and effective date.
steps.expected: Transfer is accepted. Asset's current location updates.

steps.action: Open the asset's history.
steps.expected:
Transfer event recorded with date, prior location, new location,
reason, and actor.

steps.action:
Run period depreciation. Verify the asset still depreciates on
its existing schedule (no reset).
steps.expected: Depreciation continues per the same schedule.

expected_overall: Asset transfer changes location without disrupting financials.
pass_criteria:
Location updated AND history captures the transfer AND depreciation
schedule unchanged.

title: Reconcile a bank account against a statement
goal:
Verify the bank reconciliation workflow: import or enter the
bank statement, match each line to a system record (deposit,
payment, JE), identify unmatched items, post adjustments where
appropriate, and close the reconciliation with a balanced result.
preconditions: A cash GL account exists.
At least one customer payment and one vendor payment have posted in the period.

steps.action:
Find the bank reconciliation area. Start a new reconciliation
for the cash account, with a statement ending balance and date.
steps.expected:
Reconciliation form opens with system register on one side,
statement entries on the other.

steps.action:
Import or paste the bank statement (or enter manually). Match
each statement line to a system record.
steps.expected: Matched items clear. Unmatched items remain in their column.

steps.action:
For an unmatched bank line (e.g., bank fee), post an adjusting
JE.
steps.expected: JE posts. The line clears the reconciliation.

steps.action: Close the reconciliation.
steps.expected:
The system confirms reconciled balance equals statement ending
balance. The reconciliation is saved.

expected_overall:
Bank reconciliation closes balanced, capturing matches and
adjustments.
pass_criteria:
Reconciled balance = statement balance AND adjustments posted AND
reconciliation saved.

title: Inventory reconciliation as part of period close
goal:
Run an inventory reconciliation report comparing system-on-hand
to GL inventory. Investigate any variance.
preconditions: Phase 4 has run at least one full quote-to-cash cycle: a work order has consumed material, finished goods went to inventory, an order was shipped, an invoice was posted, and cash was applied. (Phase 4 outcomes.)

steps.action:
Find the inventory reconciliation report. Run as of the last
day of the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows total inventory value (system) vs. GL inventory
account balance.

steps.action:
Investigate any discrepancy. (If none — the case still passes;
that's the goal.)
steps.expected: Discrepancy explained or absent.

expected_overall: Inventory ties to GL. Any variance is identified and explainable.
pass_criteria: Reconciliation runs AND variance (if any) is explainable.

negative_variants.title: Reject reconciliation report run as of a future date
negative_variants.action: Try to run the reconciliation as of a date in the future.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked or warns; the report only meaningfully
runs at past or current period end.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Future-dated reconciliation refused.

title: Take AR and AP aging snapshots at period close
goal:
Snapshot AR and AP aging at the period boundary so the historical
position is recoverable later.
preconditions: AR and AP both have transaction history: at least one customer invoice exists in AR (from Phase 4) and at least one vendor invoice and payment exist in AP (from Phase 3). (Phase 3 + Phase 4 outcomes.)

steps.action: Run AR aging report as of the last day of the period.
steps.expected:
Report displays buckets (current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+) and
totals per customer.

steps.action: Run AP aging report similarly.
steps.expected: AP aging displays per vendor.

steps.action: Save / archive both reports for audit purposes.
steps.expected: Both reports are stored or downloadable.

expected_overall: Period-end aging is captured.
pass_criteria:
Both reports run AND saved AND totals tie to AR and AP control
accounts on the GL.

title: Run period depreciation on fixed assets
goal:
Calculate and post the period's depreciation across all fixed
assets.
preconditions: Fixed assets exist with depreciation schedules (P3-ASSET-COMM-001).

steps.action: Open the depreciation run area. Select the period.
steps.expected:
System calculates depreciation per asset based on each asset's
schedule.

steps.action: Review the calculation. Post.
steps.expected:
Depreciation entries post: depreciation expense debited,
accumulated depreciation credited, per asset and rolled up.

expected_overall: Period depreciation is posted. Fixed asset book values reduce.
pass_criteria:
Depreciation posted AND amounts match expected straight-line
calculations AND book values updated.

negative_variants.title: Cannot run depreciation twice for same period
negative_variants.action:
After posting depreciation for the period, attempt to run it
again for the same period.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked or surfaces a clear "already run for
this period" warning.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Double-run depreciation refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject depreciation for an asset retired before the period
negative_variants.action:
Run depreciation for a period that begins after an asset's
retirement date.
negative_variants.expected:
The retired asset is excluded from the run; depreciation does
not continue past retirement.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Retired assets do not depreciate.

title: Lock the period and prevent further postings
goal:
Once close is complete, lock the period to prevent backdated entries
that would invalidate already-published reports.
preconditions: All period adjustments and depreciation have posted.

steps.action: Find the period close action. Confirm closing.
steps.expected:
A confirmation requests the period close. After confirmation,
the period is locked.

steps.action: Try to post a journal entry dated within the closed period.
steps.expected:
System blocks the entry. The error explains the period is
closed and offers either an override (with permission) or a
route to the next open period.

steps.action:
Verify financial reports for the closed period are now
"snapshot" — running them produces the same numbers always.
steps.expected: Reports are stable.

expected_overall: Period is closed. Reports are stable. Backdated postings are blocked.
pass_criteria: Period locked AND backdated post blocked AND reports stable.
why_this_matters:
Without period close, every "month-end report" can change next time
it's run because someone backdated an entry. That destroys
accountability.

negative_variants.title: Reopen a closed period as controller
negative_variants.action:
As controller (with override authority), reopen the closed
period.
negative_variants.expected:
Reopen requires confirmation and is logged in an audit trail
(who reopened, when, why).
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Reopen is gated AND audit-logged.

title: Close the fiscal year and roll income-statement balances to retained earnings
goal:
Verify year-end close: all periods closed, year-end roll posts
closing entries that zero income-statement accounts and post net
income to retained earnings, and the new fiscal year opens with
balance-sheet carryforward only.
preconditions: All periods of the fiscal year are closed.
Net income for the year is computed (P&L).

steps.action: Find the year-end close action. Trigger the year-end roll.
steps.expected:
Application generates closing entries: revenue and expense
accounts zero out, net amount posts to Retained Earnings.

steps.action: Confirm and post.
steps.expected: Closing entries post. New fiscal year is open.

steps.action: Run the new year's opening trial balance.
steps.expected:
Asset, liability, and equity balances carry forward. Revenue
and expense accounts open at zero.

steps.action: Try to post a transaction dated in the closed prior year.
steps.expected:
Blocked unless the user has explicit re-open authority (per
P5-CLOSE-004 pattern).

expected_overall: Year closes, P&L resets, balance sheet carries forward.
pass_criteria:
Closing entries post AND new year opens with zeroed P&L AND
carried-forward BS AND prior year is locked.

title: Block a journal entry dated in a closed period
goal:
After a period is locked, a JE dated within that period is rejected
with a clear message — not silently rolled forward.
preconditions: A period has been closed and locked. (Established by P5-CLOSE-004.)

steps.action: Create a manual JE dated in the closed period.
steps.expected:
System blocks the entry on save with a clear "period closed"
message that names the period.

steps.action: Verify nothing posts to GL.
steps.expected: No GL impact. Trial balance for the closed period is unchanged.

expected_overall: Closed periods are protected from late JEs.
pass_criteria:
JE rejected AND no GL postings AND closed-period trial balance
unchanged.

title: Block a backdated vendor invoice into a closed period
goal:
An AP invoice with an invoice date inside the closed period must be
rejected, redirected to the next open period, or held for explicit
override — never silently posted into the closed period.
preconditions: A period has been closed. (Established by P5-CLOSE-004.) The vendor invoice received now is dated within that closed period.

steps.action:
Enter the AP invoice with the original invoice date inside the
closed period.
steps.expected:
System surfaces the closed-period conflict and offers explicit
paths: reject, redirect posting date to the next open period,
or controller override.

steps.action:
Choose the "post to next open period" path. Verify GL impact
lands in the next open period.
steps.expected:
Posting date is in the next open period; invoice date stays as
originally documented for audit.

expected_overall: Backdated AP invoices cannot silently breach period close.
pass_criteria:
Conflict surfaced AND user makes an explicit choice AND posting
date respects the closed period.

title: Block a backdated inventory adjustment into a closed period
goal:
An inventory adjustment with a transaction date inside the closed
period is blocked — otherwise inventory valuation in the closed
period would change after the books were closed.
preconditions: A period has been closed. (Established by P5-CLOSE-004.)

steps.action:
Try to post an inventory adjustment with an effective date
inside the closed period.
steps.expected: Action is blocked with a clear "period closed" message.

steps.action: Re-date the adjustment to the next open period and re-submit.
steps.expected:
Adjustment posts to the next open period. Closed-period
inventory valuation is unchanged.

expected_overall: Inventory valuation in closed periods is immutable.
pass_criteria:
Backdated adjustment blocked AND re-dated adjustment posts to next
open period AND closed-period valuation unchanged.

title: Block a cash receipt dated into a closed period
goal:
A customer payment with a deposit / receipt date inside the closed
period is rejected or redirected. Bank reconciliation for the
closed period stays settled.
preconditions: A period has been closed. (Established by P5-CLOSE-004.) Bank reconciliation for that period was completed. (Established by P5-BANK-001.)

steps.action:
Try to record a cash receipt with a deposit date inside the
closed period.
steps.expected:
System blocks the action and offers either redirection to the
next open period or controller override.

steps.action: Redirect to the next open period.
steps.expected:
Receipt posts to the next open period. Closed-period bank
reconciliation is unchanged.

expected_overall:
Cash receipts cannot retroactively destabilize closed-period bank
reconciliations.
pass_criteria:
Backdated receipt blocked AND posted into next open period AND
closed-period bank rec unchanged.

title: Controller-override late posting into closed period
goal:
When a true correction must hit a closed period (audit adjustment,
legal restatement), the controller can override — but the override
is gated, justification is captured, and an audit entry is logged.
preconditions: A period has been closed. (Established by P5-CLOSE-004.) A correcting JE must post into the closed period.

steps.action:
As controller, attempt the JE into the closed period and select
the override option.
steps.expected:
A justification field is required. Override requires
controller-level permission.

steps.action:
Enter justification ("audit adjustment AJE-2026-04-001").
Confirm.
steps.expected: JE posts into the closed period.

steps.action:
Open the audit log. Verify the override is recorded with
who / when / what / before / after / justification.
steps.expected: Audit entry is present and complete.

expected_overall:
Closed-period overrides are possible for legitimate correction but
always leave a trail.
pass_criteria:
Override gated by permission AND justification captured AND audit
entry complete.

negative_variants.title: Non-controller cannot override closed period
negative_variants.action:
As a user without controller-level permission, attempt the same
override.
negative_variants.expected:
Override option is unavailable or rejected with a clear
"insufficient permission" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Override gated by role.

negative_variants.title: Reject closed-period override without justification
negative_variants.action:
As controller, attempt override with the justification field
blank.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a "justification required" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Justification cannot be skipped.

title: Soft-close window allows reversing entries before hard close
goal:
Many businesses run a soft close where the period is provisionally
closed but a small set of users can still post adjusting / reversing
entries before the hard close locks it forever.
preconditions: The period has been soft-closed. (Established by P5-CLOSE-004 if soft / hard close are distinguished.)

steps.action:
As controller in the soft-close window, post an adjusting JE
dated in the soft-closed period.
steps.expected:
Posting succeeds with a flag indicating it landed during the
soft-close window.

steps.action: As a regular user, attempt the same posting.
steps.expected: Posting is blocked.

steps.action: Hard-close the period. Attempt the same posting as controller.
steps.expected:
Posting is now blocked even for controller (per P5-CLOSE-010
override path is the only remaining route).

expected_overall:
Soft / hard close distinction supports normal close workflow without
abandoning the lock.
pass_criteria:
Soft-close adjusting entries gated by role AND hard close eliminates
the soft-close path AND override path remains as last resort.

title: Run a routine cycle count
goal:
Perform a routine cycle count (e.g., ABC-classified parts) as part
of normal operations rather than year-end physical inventory.
preconditions: Inventory exists across multiple parts.

steps.action:
Generate a cycle count for parts due to be counted (system
should know which based on ABC class and last count).
steps.expected: Count list is generated automatically.

steps.action: Count physically. Enter results.
steps.expected: Quantities recorded. Variances flagged.

steps.action: Approve and post adjustments for variance.
steps.expected: Inventory adjusted. Last-count date updates per part.

expected_overall: Routine cycle count runs end-to-end. ABC schedule advances.
pass_criteria: Counts captured AND adjustments posted AND last-count dates updated.

title: Report damage to a piece of equipment
goal:
Floor operator notices damage on equipment (a press hydraulic leak,
a worn fixture) and logs it. A maintenance ticket is created.
preconditions: At least one fixed asset record (P1-ASSET-001).

steps.action:
At the press, scan the asset tag. Choose "Report damage" from
the asset's actions.
steps.expected: A damage report form opens, pre-filled with the asset.

steps.action:
Enter:
- Description: "Hydraulic fluid leak from main cylinder"
- Severity: Medium (operable but degraded)
- Photos (if supported): one or two
steps.expected: Form accepts. Severity drives downstream priority.

steps.action: Submit. Verify a maintenance ticket / work order is created.
steps.expected:
A maintenance WO appears in the maintenance queue, linked to
the asset.

expected_overall: Damage is logged, photographed, and dispatched to maintenance.
pass_criteria:
Maintenance WO created AND linked to the asset AND visible in the
maintenance queue.

negative_variants.title: Reject damage report on a retired asset
negative_variants.action:
Retire an asset (per P5-ASSET-RETIRE-001). Try to file a damage
report against it.
negative_variants.expected: Report is blocked with a clear "asset is retired" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: No report created against retired asset.

title: Maintenance triages a damage report and assigns a tech
goal:
The maintenance manager reviews the damage report, sets priority,
and assigns a tech. The kanban / scheduling board reflects this.
preconditions: A damage report exists against an asset, with description, severity, and (optionally) photos, and a maintenance ticket has been created in the maintenance queue. (Established by P5-DAMAGE-001.)

steps.action: Open the maintenance queue. Find the new ticket.
steps.expected:
Ticket visible with severity, asset, description, and
reporter.

steps.action:
Set priority: High (blocks production). Assign to a tech.
Schedule for today.
steps.expected:
Ticket updates with assignment. The tech sees it on their
dashboard.

expected_overall: Ticket triaged and dispatched.
pass_criteria: Priority set AND tech assigned AND tech sees the ticket.

negative_variants.title: Cannot assign ticket to a deactivated tech
negative_variants.action:
Try to assign the ticket to a maintenance tech who has been
deactivated.
negative_variants.expected:
The user is filtered out or the assignment is blocked with a
"user inactive" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Inactive tech cannot be assigned.

title: Maintenance tech executes the repair
goal:
Tech does the work, consumes any parts, records time, and closes
the ticket.
preconditions: A maintenance ticket has been triaged: priority is set, a tech is assigned, and the ticket is scheduled for execution. (Established by P5-DAMAGE-002.)

steps.action: Open the assigned ticket. Scan or click "Start work."
steps.expected: Time tracking begins.

steps.action:
Issue any required parts (from inventory, e.g., a hydraulic
fitting and a quart of fluid). Record consumed quantities.
steps.expected: Inventory decreases. Parts cost is recorded against the ticket.

steps.action:
Add a completion note: what was done, root cause if known,
recommended preventative action.
steps.expected: Note saves with timestamp.

steps.action: Close the ticket.
steps.expected:
Ticket closed. Asset returned to operable status. Total cost
(labor + parts) recorded against the asset's lifetime cost.

expected_overall: Repair is complete and costed. Asset is back to operable.
pass_criteria: Ticket closed AND parts and labor recorded AND asset back online.

negative_variants.title: Cannot close ticket without recording any labor or parts
negative_variants.action:
Try to close the maintenance ticket with no labor entries and
no parts consumed.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or warns; closure with empty work record is
almost always a data-entry omission.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Empty closure refused or surfaces an explicit warning.

negative_variants.title: Cannot consume parts not on hand
negative_variants.action: Try to issue 5 hydraulic fittings when only 2 are on hand.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "insufficient on-hand" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-issue refused.

title: Run period-end FX revaluation on open foreign-currency balances
goal:
Verify the application revalues open foreign-currency AR and AP
balances at the period-end rate, posting an unrealized gain or
loss to the appropriate GL account.
preconditions: At least one open AR or AP balance in a foreign currency exists.
A period-end FX rate that differs from the original transaction rate is loaded.

steps.action:
Find the FX revaluation area. Run revaluation for the period
end.
steps.expected:
Application identifies open foreign-currency balances and
computes the revaluation per balance.

steps.action: Review the revaluation summary.
steps.expected:
For each balance: foreign amount, original USD value, revalued
USD value, unrealized gain or loss.

steps.action: Post the revaluation.
steps.expected:
Adjusting JE posts to AR / AP control accounts and to
Unrealized FX Gain / Loss (NOT Realized — that's reserved for
settlement).

expected_overall: FX revaluation posts unrealized gain / loss correctly.
pass_criteria:
Revaluation amount matches hand math AND unrealized account is
used (not realized).

title: Approve pay-period time and produce a payroll-export feed
goal:
Verify the time-to-payroll workflow: time entries are approved by
the manager, the system computes regular vs. overtime hours per
classification, and a payroll feed (file or integration) is produced
matching the approved data.
preconditions: Multiple labor entries from at least 2 employees in the pay period exist (P4-LABOR).
At least one employee is non-exempt (overtime eligible).

steps.action:
As the manager, open the time-approval queue for the pay
period. Approve all pending entries.
steps.expected: Approvals save.

steps.action:
For a non-exempt employee with > 40 hrs in the week, verify
the system splits hours into regular and overtime per the
configured rule.
steps.expected: OT hours are computed correctly (e.g., FLSA 40-hour rule).

steps.action: Generate the payroll feed (file export or integration push).
steps.expected:
File / payload contains every approved employee, regular hours,
OT hours, pay rate, and gross pay.

steps.action: Spot-check one row against the source.
steps.expected: Match.

expected_overall: Time approves cleanly and payroll feed reflects approved data.
pass_criteria:
Approvals complete AND OT computed correctly AND feed matches
source.

title: Employee submits a PTO request; manager approves; balance updates
goal:
Verify employees can request PTO, managers approve / deny, the
balance updates correctly, and approved PTO appears on the
scheduling / capacity view so planners avoid over-booking.
preconditions: At least one employee with a configured PTO accrual policy exists.

steps.action:
As the employee, find the PTO area. Submit a request: 2 days
next month.
steps.expected:
Request saves in "Pending" status. Available PTO balance is
visible.

steps.action: As the manager, open the PTO approval queue. Approve.
steps.expected: Status changes to "Approved." The balance reservation updates.

steps.action: Open the production capacity view for the requested days.
steps.expected:
The employee's availability shows as "On PTO" and they're not
assignable on those days.

steps.action: Once the dates pass, balance is debited.
steps.expected: PTO balance reduces by 2 days.

expected_overall:
PTO request → approval → balance update → scheduling integration
works end-to-end.
pass_criteria:
Approval flow complete AND balance debited AND scheduling reflects
PTO.

title: Build a shift schedule and confirm employees see their shifts
goal:
Verify the shift management workflow: define shifts, assign
employees to shifts on a schedule, and have employees see their
upcoming shifts in their own view.
preconditions: At least 3 employees and at least 2 shift definitions exist (P1-CAL-001, P1-CAL-002).

steps.action:
Find the shift schedule area. Build a 1-week schedule covering
both first-shift and second-shift slots; assign 3 employees
across them.
steps.expected: Schedule saves. The view shows the shift assignments visually.

steps.action:
Sign in as one of the assigned employees. Open their schedule
view.
steps.expected: The employee sees their assigned shifts for the upcoming week.

steps.action: As the manager, swap two employees on a single shift.
steps.expected: Swap saves. Both employees see the updated assignment.

expected_overall: Shift scheduling propagates correctly to employee views.
pass_criteria:
Schedule saved AND employees see assignments AND swaps update
cleanly.

title: Terminate an employee with full offboarding workflow
goal:
Verify employee termination disables system access, retains
historical attribution (labor entries, audit log), produces final
pay and final-day documentation, and prevents new task / time
assignment.
preconditions: At least one employee with an active linked user account exists.

steps.action:
Open the employee record. Initiate termination. Capture
effective date, reason, and any final-pay items.
steps.expected: Termination form accepts.

steps.action: Confirm.
steps.expected:
Effective date applied. The linked user account is deactivated.
The employee record transitions to "Terminated" but is preserved.

steps.action: Try to sign in as the terminated user.
steps.expected: Sign-in is rejected.

steps.action: Try to assign a WO to the terminated employee.
steps.expected: Assignment is blocked with "employee terminated" message.

steps.action:
Run the labor distribution report covering the period before
termination.
steps.expected: The terminated employee's prior labor history is intact.

expected_overall:
Termination disables access, gates new assignment, preserves
history.
pass_criteria:
Sign-in blocked AND assignment blocked AND history preserved AND
audit recorded.

title: Record training and certifications with expiry on an employee
goal:
Verify training and certification records on an employee: capture
course / cert, completion date, expiry date, and ensure the
expiring-soon list surfaces upcoming renewals.
preconditions: At least one employee exists.

steps.action:
Open the employee. Add training records:
- "Forklift certification" — completed 2025-01-15, expires
  2027-01-15
- "Lockout / Tagout" — completed 2025-06-01, expires 2026-06-01
steps.expected: Both records save with their dates.

steps.action:
Run the "expiring within 60 days" report (or filter the
training list).
steps.expected:
The Lockout/Tagout cert appears (within 60 days of expiry as
of today's date if test date is in 2026-Q2).

steps.action:
Try to assign the employee to a work center that requires
forklift cert (if such a constraint is configured).
steps.expected: Assignment is allowed (cert valid).

steps.action:
Backdate the forklift cert to expired. Re-attempt the
assignment.
steps.expected: Assignment is blocked or warned.

expected_overall:
Training / cert records surface expirations and gate work
assignment where required.
pass_criteria:
Records saved AND expiring-soon report correct AND assignment
gating respects validity.

title: Manual inventory adjustment with a reason code
goal:
When inventory needs adjustment outside a cycle count (damage,
shrinkage, found stock), a manual adjustment with a reason code
posts to the right variance account.
preconditions: Inventory exists for at least one part.

steps.action:
Open the inventory adjustment area. Adjust 5 ft of steel out
of stock with reason: "Damaged in handling."
steps.expected: Adjustment is accepted. Reason is required.

steps.action:
Verify GL impact: inventory account decreased, scrap / handling
variance account increased.
steps.expected: Postings target the right variance account based on the reason.

expected_overall:
Inventory adjustments are reason-coded and post to the right
variance account.
pass_criteria:
Inventory adjusted AND reason captured AND GL postings reflect the
reason.

negative_variants.title: Reject adjustment that would drive on-hand negative
negative_variants.action: Try to adjust out 200 units when only 50 are on hand.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "would drive on-hand negative"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Negative on-hand prevention.

negative_variants.title: Reject adjustment without reason code
negative_variants.action: Try to save an adjustment with the reason field blank.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "reason is required" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Adjustment without reason refused.

negative_variants.title: Large adjustments require controller approval
negative_variants.action:
Try to adjust out a value above the documented self-approval
threshold.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked or routes for controller approval.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Large adjustments are gated by an approval workflow.

title: Post a balanced manual journal entry
goal:
Verify the manual JE workflow: enter a multi-line entry, ensure
debits = credits, post, and confirm GL impact.
preconditions: The chart of accounts is initialized.
An open period exists.

steps.action:
Find the JE entry area. Create a new JE with three lines:
- Debit Expense Account A: $300
- Debit Expense Account B: $200
- Credit Cash: $500
steps.expected: Entry accepts. Total debits = total credits = $500.

steps.action: Try to save with debits ≠ credits (e.g., change one line).
steps.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "must balance" message.

steps.action: Restore balance and post the entry.
steps.expected: JE posts. Each account's running balance updates.

steps.action: Open the audit log.
steps.expected: JE posting recorded with actor, timestamp, JE number, and total.

expected_overall:
Manual JEs post when balanced, are blocked when not, and are
audited.
pass_criteria: Entry posts when balanced AND blocked otherwise AND audit recorded.

title: Configure a recurring JE that posts each period
goal:
Verify a recurring template (e.g., monthly rent accrual) can be
defined and the system posts an instance each period — manually or
automatically.
preconditions: The chart of accounts is initialized.

steps.action:
Find the recurring JE area. Create a recurring entry: "Monthly
rent accrual" — Debit Rent Expense $5,000, Credit Accrued Rent
$5,000, monthly on the 1st, ending in 12 months.
steps.expected: Recurring template saves.

steps.action: Advance to the first scheduled date (or trigger manually).
steps.expected:
A JE is generated and posted (or queued, per workflow). It
references the recurring template.

steps.action: Open the recurring entry's history.
steps.expected: One generated JE so far, with timestamp.

expected_overall:
Recurring JEs generate per schedule with traceability to the
template.
pass_criteria:
Template saved AND first instance posted / queued AND traceability
back to template.

title: Configure a JE to auto-reverse in the next period
goal:
Verify a JE can be marked auto-reversing — useful for accruals.
When the next period opens, an offsetting reversal posts
automatically.
preconditions: At least two consecutive periods are configured.

steps.action:
Post a JE with the auto-reverse flag enabled, dated the last
day of the current period.
steps.expected:
JE posts. The reversal is scheduled for the first day of the
next period.

steps.action: Open the reversal queue (or advance to the next period).
steps.expected:
Reversal posts on the first day of the next period with
offsetting amounts. It references the original JE.

steps.action:
Verify GL impact: the original JE and the reversal net to zero
from the next period onward.
steps.expected: Net = 0.

expected_overall: Auto-reversing JEs work correctly.
pass_criteria: Original posts AND reversal posts on the right date AND net = 0.

title: Subcontract shipment lost in transit
goal:
A sub-assembly was shipped to a subcontractor but never arrived.
Carrier confirms loss. The system supports writing off the offsite
inventory and reopening the WO at the right operation.
preconditions: A sub-assembly was shipped to a subcontractor and is currently flagged offsite-with-vendor. (Established by P5-OFFSITE-SEND.)

steps.action: Open the offsite inventory report. Locate the lost shipment.
steps.expected:
The offsite line is visible with shipper number, vendor, and
quantity.

steps.action:
Mark the offsite material as lost-in-transit with reason and
carrier reference.
steps.expected:
A loss adjustment is created. Reason and carrier reference are
required fields.

steps.action:
Verify the WO is flagged so the subcontract operation can be
restarted with replacement material rather than waiting forever.
steps.expected:
WO is reopened at the subcontract operation, and the original
offsite quantity is removed from the offsite report.

expected_overall:
Lost subcontract shipments are reconcilable, not stuck offsite
forever.
pass_criteria: Loss recorded AND offsite report cleared AND WO flagged for restart.

negative_variants.title: Reject lost-in-transit without carrier reference
negative_variants.action:
Try to mark the shipment as lost without entering a carrier
claim or tracking reference.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "carrier reference required"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Reasonless loss claim refused.

title: Subcontractor returns only part of the shipment
goal:
Subcontractor sends back a partial quantity (some parts still in
process, some damaged at their facility, etc.). The system accepts
the partial receipt and keeps the remainder offsite.
preconditions: A sub-assembly is offsite at a subcontractor. (Established by P5-OFFSITE-SEND.)

steps.action:
At receiving, scan the inbound shipper. The expected quantity
from the original send is 100 units; the inbound is 60 units.
steps.expected:
System detects the underage and prompts whether this is a
partial return.

steps.action:
Confirm partial receipt of 60 units. Capture a reason for the
remaining 40 (still in process / damaged at vendor / scrapped at
vendor).
steps.expected:
60 units progress to the next routing operation. 40 units stay
flagged offsite with the captured reason.

steps.action:
Verify the offsite report still shows 40 units against the same
shipper and vendor.
steps.expected: Offsite balance equals 40. The original shipper is not closed.

expected_overall:
Partial subcontract returns are handled cleanly with the remainder
visibly outstanding.
pass_criteria:
Partial received AND remainder still offsite AND reason captured
for the gap.

negative_variants.title: Reject partial receipt without remainder disposition
negative_variants.action:
Try to confirm a partial receipt without selecting a reason for
the missing quantity.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a "remainder reason required" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Partial receipt without disposition refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject partial receipt exceeding original send quantity
negative_variants.action: Scan in 120 units against an original send of 100.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked or surfaces an explicit prompt to amend the
original shipper before accepting the overage.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-receipt against subcontract is gated.

title: Subcontractor reports parts scrapped during processing
goal:
Subcontractor damages or scraps some of the parts during their
operation. The system records the scrap, charges the right account,
and reflects the loss in the WO.
preconditions: A sub-assembly is offsite at a subcontractor and the vendor has reported scrap during processing. (Established by P5-OFFSITE-SEND.)

steps.action:
Open the offsite shipper. Record vendor-reported scrap of 5
units with reason "Plating bath contamination — vendor fault."
steps.expected:
Scrap is recorded against the shipper with reason and
responsibility (vendor).

steps.action:
Verify offsite balance reduces by 5. Verify the scrap variance
account is debited and a vendor-charge / debit-memo is queued
to recover cost from the subcontractor.
steps.expected: GL postings reflect the scrap. Vendor recovery is queued.

steps.action:
Receive the remaining good units back. Confirm the WO advances
with the reduced quantity.
steps.expected: WO continues with the surviving quantity.

expected_overall:
Scrap at vendor is captured with responsibility, GL postings, and
vendor cost recovery.
pass_criteria:
Scrap recorded AND responsibility flagged AND vendor recovery
queued AND WO quantity reduced.

negative_variants.title: Reject vendor-scrap entry without responsibility code
negative_variants.action:
Try to record vendor-reported scrap without selecting a
responsibility (vendor / our material defect / shared).
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a "responsibility required" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Scrap without responsibility refused.

negative_variants.title: Reject scrap quantity exceeding offsite balance
negative_variants.action: Try to record 200 units scrapped when only 100 are offsite.
negative_variants.expected: Save is blocked with a clear "exceeds offsite quantity" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Over-scrap refused.

title: Aging alert fires on overdue subcontract shipment
goal:
When a subcontract shipment passes its expected return date, the
system flags it so it doesn't sit forgotten offsite indefinitely.
preconditions: A subcontract shipment has been sent with a documented expected return date that has now passed. (Established by P5-OFFSITE-SEND.)

steps.action:
Run the offsite-aging report. Confirm overdue shipments are
surfaced separately or highlighted.
steps.expected: Overdue shipments are visible with days-overdue and vendor.

steps.action:
Verify a notification or alert was raised for the production
manager when the shipment first crossed overdue.
steps.expected:
An alert is recorded against the shipment, dated the first day
it became overdue.

expected_overall:
Overdue subcontract shipments are detectable and alerted, not
invisible.
pass_criteria:
Aging report flags overdue AND a one-time alert was raised when it
first went overdue.

title: Subcontractor returns wrong serial / lot
goal:
Subcontractor returns parts but the serials or lots do not match
what was sent (mix-up at their facility). The system catches the
mismatch on receipt rather than letting bad traceability propagate.
preconditions: A subcontract shipment with serial- or lot-tracked parts is offsite. (Established by P5-OFFSITE-SEND.)

steps.action:
At receiving, scan the inbound serials / lots. One or more do
not match what was sent.
steps.expected:
System flags the mismatch and refuses to silently accept the
wrong identifiers.

steps.action:
Open a discrepancy record against the vendor with the expected
and actual identifiers captured.
steps.expected:
Discrepancy is logged with both identifier sets. Vendor is
notified per the documented escalation path.

expected_overall:
Identifier mismatches at subcontract receipt are caught, not
rubber-stamped — protecting downstream traceability.
pass_criteria:
Mismatch detected AND receipt blocked or held AND discrepancy
logged with full identifier history.

negative_variants.title: Cannot override mismatch without recorded justification
negative_variants.action:
Attempt to force-accept a mismatched serial without entering a
justification note.
negative_variants.expected: Override is blocked with a "justification required" message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Silent override refused.

title: Receive material back from the subcontractor
goal:
Material returns from the subcontractor; the routing resumes from
the operation after the subcontract.
preconditions: A sub-assembly has been shipped out for subcontract processing: a shipper / pack list was generated and the material is flagged offsite-with-vendor (no longer in available inventory). (Established by P5-OFFSITE-SEND.)

steps.action: At receiving, scan the inbound shipper from the subcontractor.
steps.expected: System matches against the offsite WO. Material is reconciled.

steps.action:
Confirm receipt. Verify the WO automatically advances past the
subcontract operation.
steps.expected: WO continues at the next routing operation.

steps.action:
The subcontract cost is recorded against the WO (per the
routing's cost from P2-ROUTE-002).
steps.expected: WO actual cost increases by the subcontract cost.

expected_overall:
Subcontract round-trip is closed. WO continues with no manual
intervention beyond the receipt.
pass_criteria: Material received AND WO advanced AND subcontract cost recorded.

title: Ship a sub-assembly out for subcontract processing
goal:
When a routing has a subcontracted operation, the system handles
shipping out, holding while off-site, and receiving back.
preconditions: A WO is at a subcontract operation (from P2-ROUTE-002).

steps.action:
At the operation, scan to ship to the subcontractor. System
generates a shipper / pack list.
steps.expected:
Pack list and shipper are created. Material is marked as
offsite-with-vendor.

steps.action:
Verify that the material is no longer counted in available
inventory but appears in an "in-transit / offsite" report.
steps.expected: Material shows as offsite, not on-hand.

expected_overall:
Subcontracted material is properly tracked while away from the
facility.
pass_criteria:
Material flagged offsite AND shipper generated AND inventory
visibility maintained.

title: Configure a preventative maintenance schedule
goal:
Define a PM schedule on a critical asset — every N hours of run
time, or every N days, or both — so the system can trigger
preventative work before damage happens.
preconditions: At least one asset (P1-ASSET-001).

steps.action: Open the press asset. Find the PM section.
steps.expected: A way to define PM schedules is present.

steps.action:
Add a schedule:
- Type: Quarterly (every 90 days)
- Description: "Hydraulic fluid change + filter inspection"
- Required parts: hydraulic fluid, filter
- Estimated labor: 2 hours
steps.expected: Schedule saves.

steps.action:
Set the next due date and verify the system will create a PM WO
automatically when due.
steps.expected:
Next-due date is visible. A scheduled job will create the WO
when the date arrives.

expected_overall:
PM schedule is configured. The system will trigger work before
damage occurs.
pass_criteria:
Schedule exists AND next-due is visible AND scheduled trigger
is in place.

negative_variants.title: Reject PM schedule with zero or negative interval
negative_variants.action:
Try to configure a PM schedule with interval = 0 days or
runtime hours = -100.
negative_variants.expected: Submission is blocked with a clear error.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Zero / negative intervals rejected.

title: Trigger a PM work order manually and complete it
goal:
Verify the manual trigger path for PMs (e.g., advancing schedule
for a planned shutdown) and that execution mirrors damage repair.
preconditions: A preventative maintenance schedule is configured on an asset, with interval, description, required parts, estimated labor, and a next-due date that will trigger a PM work order. (Established by P5-PM-001.)

steps.action: From the press's PM section, manually trigger the next PM.
steps.expected:
A PM work order is created and assigned (or queued for
assignment).

steps.action:
As the assigned tech, execute the PM: parts, labor, completion
note.
steps.expected:
PM completes. Schedule advances — next due date moves forward
by the configured interval.

expected_overall: PM cycle is closed. Schedule advances automatically.
pass_criteria: PM WO closed AND parts/labor recorded AND next-due date advanced.

negative_variants.title: Cannot trigger duplicate PM while one is open
negative_variants.action:
Trigger a PM. Before it is closed, try to trigger the same PM
again.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked or warns; only one open PM per schedule
should be active.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Duplicate active PM refused.

title: PM execution exceeds estimated labor — variance captured
goal:
When PM execution runs over the estimated labor (e.g., 5 hours
actual vs 2 hours estimated), the system captures the labor
variance for reporting without blocking the work.
preconditions: A PM work order is in progress with documented estimated labor hours. (Established by P5-PM-002.)

steps.action:
Record actual labor hours that exceed the estimate by 100% or
more (e.g., 5 actual vs 2 estimated).
steps.expected:
Labor records normally. The system flags the variance but does
not block.

steps.action:
Open the PM variance report. Confirm the over-budget PM is
surfaced with estimated, actual, and variance fields.
steps.expected:
Report shows variance with the over-budget PM at the top or
flagged for review.

expected_overall: Labor variance on PM is captured and surfaced for review.
pass_criteria:
Actual hours recorded AND variance flagged AND visible in the PM
variance report.

negative_variants.title: Large variance triggers manager review
negative_variants.action:
Record actual hours that exceed estimate by more than the
documented review threshold.
negative_variants.expected: The PM closure routes through a manager review step.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Above-threshold variance is gated by review.

title: PM cannot start because required parts are not on hand
goal:
When a PM is due but the required parts (per the PM's parts list)
are not on hand, the system surfaces the shortage rather than
letting the tech begin and stall.
preconditions: A PM schedule defines required parts. (Established by P5-PM-001.) On-hand inventory of at least one required part is below the PM's required quantity.

steps.action: Trigger the PM. Attempt to start work.
steps.expected:
System surfaces the parts shortage and offers options: hold the
PM, generate a parts requisition, or proceed with override.

steps.action:
Choose "generate parts requisition." Verify the requisition
lists the missing parts and quantities.
steps.expected: Requisition is created and tied to the PM.

steps.action:
Verify the PM stays in a "waiting on parts" state until the
parts arrive.
steps.expected:
PM state reflects the wait. Tech dashboards do not show it as
ready to start until parts are available.

expected_overall: Parts shortages on PM are surfaced before work starts, not after.
pass_criteria:
Shortage surfaced AND requisition optional path works AND PM held
in waiting state.

negative_variants.title: Override start without parts requires approval
negative_variants.action: Try to override the parts shortage and start the PM anyway.
negative_variants.expected: Override requires manager approval and is recorded with reason.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Override gated by approval and audit-logged.

title: PM consumes more parts than the schedule estimated
goal:
When PM execution consumes more parts than the schedule estimated
(e.g., a second filter needed mid-job), the additional consumption
is recorded and the variance is reportable.
preconditions: A PM work order is in progress. (Established by P5-PM-002.)

steps.action: Issue 2 filters when the PM estimate was 1 filter.
steps.expected: Issuance is recorded. Inventory decreases for both filters.

steps.action: Verify the PM variance report flags the parts overage.
steps.expected: Report shows estimated vs actual parts cost with variance.

expected_overall: Parts overage on PM is recorded and reportable.
pass_criteria: Extra parts consumed AND inventory adjusted AND variance reported.

title: Tech discovers additional issue during PM
goal:
During PM, the tech discovers an unrelated issue (e.g., a worn belt
found during a hydraulic PM). The system supports either spawning
a new corrective WO or extending the current PM with the additional
work captured separately.
preconditions: A PM work order is in progress. (Established by P5-PM-002.)

steps.action:
During execution, log the additional finding ("worn drive belt")
with severity.
steps.expected:
Finding is captured. The system offers either to add it to this
PM or spawn a new corrective WO.

steps.action:
Choose "spawn corrective WO." Verify the new WO is linked to
the original PM and to the asset.
steps.expected:
New corrective WO exists and is traceable back to the PM that
surfaced it.

steps.action:
Close the original PM normally without the unrelated work
contaminating its variance numbers.
steps.expected:
Original PM closes with its own scope. Corrective WO is
independently tracked.

expected_overall:
Additional findings on PM execution are captured cleanly without
distorting PM variance reporting.
pass_criteria:
Finding logged AND corrective WO spawned AND linkage preserved AND
PM variance not distorted.

title: PM aborted mid-execution due to safety lockout
goal:
Tech starts a PM, then must abort because a safety lockout / tagout
condition prevents safe completion. The system handles the abort
gracefully — recording partial work and rescheduling the PM.
preconditions: A PM work order is in progress. (Established by P5-PM-002.)

steps.action:
As the tech, choose "abort PM" with reason "LOTO required —
production not stopping."
steps.expected:
Abort prompts for reason. Partial labor and parts already
consumed are recorded.

steps.action:
Verify the PM's next-due date is preserved or reset per policy
so the PM is not silently considered complete.
steps.expected:
PM is in an aborted / rescheduled state. Next-due date reflects
that the PM still needs to be done.

steps.action: Reschedule the PM. Confirm a new PM work order is created.
steps.expected: New PM WO references the aborted one for traceability.

expected_overall:
PM aborts are first-class — tracked, costed for partial work, and
rescheduled.
pass_criteria:
Abort recorded with reason AND partial cost captured AND PM
rescheduled AND traceability preserved.

title: PM cost roll-up to asset lifetime maintenance cost
goal:
PM costs (parts + labor) accumulate to the asset's lifetime
maintenance cost so capital decisions (repair vs replace) have
defensible numbers.
preconditions: Multiple PM cycles have completed against an asset. (Established by P5-PM-002 plus P5-PM-003 / P5-PM-005 if those ran.)

steps.action: Open the asset record. Find lifetime maintenance cost.
steps.expected:
A lifetime cost figure is shown, broken down by PM, corrective,
and damage repairs.

steps.action:
Drill down. Verify each completed PM contributes its parts +
labor cost to the rollup.
steps.expected: Drill-down ties to the individual PM WOs.

steps.action:
Compare lifetime maintenance cost to the asset's net book value
from depreciation (P5-CLOSE-003).
steps.expected:
Both numbers are visible side by side, supporting repair-vs-
replace analysis.

expected_overall:
Asset lifetime maintenance cost rolls up cleanly and is comparable
to depreciation-based book value.
pass_criteria:
Lifetime cost shown AND drill-down ties to PM WOs AND comparable
to net book value.

title: Open a CAPA from an NCR and track to closure
goal:
Verify the CAPA workflow: from a recurring NCR pattern, open a
CAPA, assign owner, capture root cause and corrective action,
define preventive action, and close with verification.
preconditions: At least one NCR exists (P5-QC-NCR-001).

steps.action:
From an NCR (or independently), open a CAPA. Set:
- Description: short summary of the issue
- Owner: assigned engineer
- Due date: 30 days from today
steps.expected: CAPA saves with a stable ID, in "Open" status.

steps.action:
As the owner, document root cause analysis (e.g., "Vendor's
lot heat-treatment was off-spec") and corrective action ("Reject
lot, request vendor re-test"). Save.
steps.expected: RCA and CA saved.

steps.action:
Document preventive action ("Add incoming hardness check to
inspection plan for steel from this vendor").
steps.expected: PA saved.

steps.action:
Close the CAPA with a verification step (e.g., "Updated plan
P5-QC-INSPECT-001 with hardness check, verified active 2026-04-25").
steps.expected:
CAPA transitions to Closed. Closure includes verification text
and date.

expected_overall: CAPA captures the full Plan-Do-Check-Act loop with audit trail.
pass_criteria: CAPA opened AND RCA / CA / PA all captured AND closure verified.
why_this_matters:
CAPAs that don't track to verified closure are theater. Auditors
for ISO / AS9100 specifically check that closures have evidence.

title: Generate a certificate of analysis (COA) for a shipped lot
goal:
Verify the system can generate a COA for a customer shipment that
ties to the actual lot's inspection results — not a generic
template.
preconditions: At least one lot-tracked finished good was inspected and shipped on a customer SO.

steps.action:
Open a shipped SO that contained a lot-tracked product. Find
the COA generation action.
steps.expected: COA generation is available, scoped to the actual lot(s) shipped.

steps.action: Generate the COA.
steps.expected:
COA document includes: customer, SO, lot number(s), inspection
results from the lot's QC record (not a generic boilerplate),
authorized signature line.

expected_overall: COA reflects the actual shipped lot's inspection data.
pass_criteria:
COA generated AND lot-specific data present AND no generic / placeholder
values in inspection sections.

title: A QC inspection fails — initiate rework
goal:
At a QC step in a production routing, parts fail inspection. They
loop back through the routing for rework rather than going to
finished goods.
preconditions: A WO is at an inspection operation.

steps.action:
At inspection, scan the parts. Mark 3 of the 100 as failed.
Reason: "Weld porosity."
steps.expected:
Failure is recorded. The 3 parts move to a rework status, not
to finished goods.

steps.action: Choose the rework path: send back to Weld for repair.
steps.expected:
System creates a rework operation back at Weld for the 3 units.
The original WO continues with 97 units still moving forward.

steps.action: Verify the rework operation is visible to the Weld work center.
steps.expected: Rework is on Weld's queue.

expected_overall:
QC failures route to rework with full traceability of which units
were reworked at which operations.
pass_criteria:
Failed units flagged AND routed to rework AND traceable AND main
WO continues for the good units.

negative_variants.title: Reject QC fail without reason code
negative_variants.action: Mark units failed with the reason code blank.
negative_variants.expected:
Save is blocked with a clear "QC fail reason is required"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Failure without reason refused.

negative_variants.title: Failed units cannot move to finished goods
negative_variants.action:
After marking units failed, try to manually move them into
finished goods inventory.
negative_variants.expected:
The action is blocked with a "failed units cannot enter FG"
message.
negative_variants.pass_criteria: Failed units stay isolated from sellable inventory.

title: Track gage calibration; out-of-cal gages cannot be used for inspection
goal:
Verify that measurement devices (calipers, micrometers, CMMs, etc.)
have calibration records; using an out-of-cal gage for inspection
is blocked or flagged.
preconditions: The inspection plan from P5-QC-INSPECT-001 references measurement tools.

steps.action:
Find the gage / device area. Create a gage record:
- ID: GAGE-CAL-001
- Type: digital caliper
- Last cal: 6 months ago
- Next cal due: 6 months from last cal
steps.expected: Gage saves.

steps.action:
Verify the gage's status shows "Calibrated" with the next-due
date.
steps.expected: Status correct.

steps.action:
Backdate the next-cal-due to yesterday (so the gage is now
out-of-cal). Try to use the gage on an inspection.
steps.expected:
The application blocks or warns clearly that the gage is
out-of-calibration. The user cannot silently use it.

steps.action: Record a calibration event with current date and certificate.
steps.expected: Status returns to "Calibrated." Next-due rolls forward.

expected_overall: Gage calibration is tracked and out-of-cal usage is gated.
pass_criteria:
Gage status accurate AND out-of-cal blocks / warns AND new cal
event resets status.

title: Create an inspection plan and apply it to vendor receipts
goal:
Define an inspection plan for a part (sample size, tests, accept
criteria) and verify that incoming receipts of the part trigger an
inspection task before stock becomes available.
preconditions: At least one purchased raw material exists.

steps.action:
Find the inspection plans area. Create a plan for
RM-STEEL-1018-3X3:
- Sample: 5% of receipt, minimum 5
- Tests: dimensional check, visual surface check
- Accept criteria: zero rejections in sample
steps.expected: Plan saves and is associated with the part.

steps.action: Receive a PO for the part (per P3-RECV-001 flow).
steps.expected:
Stock arrives in a "pending inspection" status, NOT directly
available.

steps.action:
Open the QC inspection queue. Pick up the inspection task.
Record the dimensional check and visual check as pass.
steps.expected: Inspection completes. Stock moves from pending to available.

steps.action: Repeat with a sample failure.
steps.expected: Stock either stays in quarantine or routes to NCR (P5-QC-NCR-001).

expected_overall: Inspection plans gate stock availability based on real QC outcomes.
pass_criteria:
Plan applied AND stock gated AND pass / fail outcomes drive
disposition.

title: Open an NCR on rejected material and route for disposition
goal:
Verify that material failing inspection generates a non-conformance
report capturing the defect, that the NCR routes for disposition
(rework / scrap / use-as-is / RTV), and that the chosen disposition
drives the inventory and GL outcome.
preconditions: At least one inspection has failed (P5-QC-INSPECT-001).

steps.action:
From a failed inspection, create an NCR. Capture: part, lot,
quantity, defect description, photos.
steps.expected: NCR saves with a stable ID. Affected stock is in quarantine.

steps.action:
Route to MRB (Material Review Board) or equivalent. Record a
disposition: "Use as-is" with engineer signoff.
steps.expected: Disposition saves with the engineer's name and timestamp.

steps.action:
Verify the disposition is acted on: stock moves out of
quarantine to available (use-as-is) or to scrap, return-to-
vendor, etc.
steps.expected:
Inventory state matches disposition. GL impact (if scrap or
RTV) posts to the right account.

expected_overall:
NCR captures the issue, drives a disposition, and the disposition
is enacted.
pass_criteria:
NCR exists AND disposition recorded with signoff AND inventory /
GL match disposition.

title: Floor operator records feedback against an engineering revision
goal:
Floor operator notices an issue with a part or assembly during
production. They log feedback against the engineering revision so
R&D sees it.
preconditions: At least one BOM with a revision (P2-BOM-003).

steps.action:
At the work center, while running a WO, open the part being
