title: Sales / Account Manager is allowed to override unit price on a quote line
goal:
Verify a Sales user can override the default price on a quote line
and that the override is captured for management visibility.
preconditions: A Sales user exists.
At least one customer with default pricing exists.
At least one finished part with a default price exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Sales. Create a draft quote for the customer with a
line for the part. Note the default price.
steps.expected: Default unit price populates from the customer's price list.

steps.action: Override the unit price downward by 10%. Save.
steps.expected:
Override is accepted. The quote line shows the overridden price
with a visible indicator that it's an override.

steps.action: Open the quote audit log or the line's history.
steps.expected:
The override is recorded with the user, timestamp, prior price,
and new price.

expected_overall: Sales overrides a price; the override is captured for review.
pass_criteria: Override accepted AND visibly flagged AND captured in audit.

title: Sales / Account Manager is denied posting a journal entry
goal:
Verify a Sales user — who creates customer-facing financial
documents like quotes and draft invoices but has no GL authority —
cannot post manual journal entries.
preconditions: A Sales user exists with no other roles attached.
The chart of accounts is initialized.

steps.action:
Sign in as Sales. Look in the navigation for any
journal-entries / accounting surface.
steps.expected: No JE creation surface is visible or reachable.

steps.action: Type the journal-entries URL directly.
steps.expected: The JE form does not render. Permission denial or redirect.

steps.action: If an API is exposed, attempt the create-JE call.
steps.expected: The request is rejected with an authorization error.

expected_overall: Sales cannot post a JE through any path.
pass_criteria:
No JE created AND UI denies access AND direct URL blocked AND API
rejects the request.

title: Sales / Account Manager is denied posting a payroll run
goal: Verify a Sales user cannot post payroll.
preconditions: A Sales user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one prepared payroll run exists.

steps.action: Sign in as Sales. Look for any payroll surface.
steps.expected: The payroll surface is not reachable.

steps.action: Attempt the post-payroll endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Sales cannot post payroll.
pass_criteria: Run not posted AND attempt rejected.

title: Sales / Account Manager is denied releasing a work order
goal:
Verify a Sales user cannot release work orders. They sell against
capacity but the production schedule belongs to Production.
preconditions: A Sales user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one planned (un-released) work order exists.

steps.action: Sign in as Sales. Look for any WO release surface.
steps.expected: No WO release surface is reachable for this role.

steps.action: Type the WO release URL directly into the address bar.
steps.expected: The release action does not render. Permission denial or redirect.

steps.action: If an API is exposed, attempt the release-WO call.
steps.expected: The request is rejected.

expected_overall: Sales cannot release a WO.
pass_criteria:
No WO released AND UI denies access AND direct URL blocked AND API
rejects the request.

title: Sales / Account Manager is denied voiding a posted invoice
goal:
Verify a Sales user, who can create draft invoices, cannot void a
posted one — that's a financial action belonging to the Controller.
preconditions: A Sales user exists.
At least one posted customer invoice exists.

steps.action: Sign in as Sales. Open the posted invoice.
steps.expected:
Void / cancel action is hidden, disabled, or replaced with a
"request void" handoff.

steps.action: Attempt the void via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: Action is rejected with a clear authorization error.

expected_overall: Sales cannot void posted invoices.
pass_criteria:
Invoice not voided AND no GL reversal posted AND attempt was
rejected at every accessible surface.

title: Warehouse / Logistics is denied approving an engineering change request
goal: Verify a Warehouse user cannot approve ECRs.
preconditions: A Warehouse user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one open ECR exists.

steps.action: Sign in as Warehouse. Look for any ECR area.
steps.expected: ECR admin is not reachable.

steps.action: Attempt the approve endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Warehouse cannot approve an ECR.
pass_criteria: ECR not approved AND attempt rejected.

title: Warehouse / Logistics is denied approving a PO
goal:
Verify a user with only the Warehouse / Logistics role cannot
approve POs (they receive against POs, but do not authorize spend).
preconditions: A Warehouse user exists.
A draft PO awaiting approval exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Warehouse. Open the draft PO (read-only access for
receiving prep is acceptable).
steps.expected: The Approve action is hidden, disabled, or not available.

steps.action: Attempt to reach the approve endpoint via direct URL.
steps.expected: Action is rejected. PO remains unapproved.

expected_overall: Warehouse cannot approve POs through any path.
pass_criteria: No approval recorded AND PO state unchanged.

title: Warehouse / Logistics is denied deleting a customer record
goal: Verify a Warehouse user cannot delete customer records.
preconditions: A Warehouse user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one customer record exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Warehouse. Open a customer record if visible from
a shipment view.
steps.expected: No delete action is available.

steps.action: Attempt the delete endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Warehouse cannot delete a customer record.
pass_criteria: Customer record unchanged AND endpoint rejected.

title: Warehouse / Logistics is denied issuing a customer credit memo
goal:
Verify a Warehouse user cannot issue credit memos. They process
RMAs and return receipts but the financial credit issuance belongs
to Controller.
preconditions: A Warehouse user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one customer with an open AR balance exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Warehouse. Look for any credit-memo issuance
surface.
steps.expected: The issuance surface is not visible.

steps.action: Attempt the issue-credit-memo endpoint via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

expected_overall: Warehouse cannot issue a credit memo.
pass_criteria: No credit memo posted AND endpoint rejected.

title: Warehouse / Logistics is denied modifying a released BOM
goal: Verify a Warehouse user cannot modify BOMs.
preconditions: A Warehouse user exists with no other roles attached.
A finished part has a released BOM.

steps.action:
Sign in as Warehouse. Open a BOM (read-only access for picking
visibility is acceptable).
steps.expected: The BOM is read-only.

steps.action: Attempt the BOM modify endpoint via direct URL.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

steps.action: If an API is exposed, attempt the modify-BOM call.
steps.expected: The request is rejected.

expected_overall: Warehouse cannot modify a BOM.
pass_criteria: BOM unchanged AND no new revision created AND attempt rejected.

title: Warehouse / Logistics is denied changing a customer credit limit
goal: Verify a Warehouse user cannot change customer credit limits.
preconditions: A Warehouse user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one customer with an existing credit limit exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Warehouse. Open a customer record if visible from
a shipment view.
steps.expected: Credit limit field is read-only or hidden.

steps.action: Try to PATCH a credit-limit update via direct URL or API.
steps.expected: The update is rejected.

expected_overall: Warehouse cannot change credit limits.
pass_criteria: Limit unchanged AND endpoint rejected.

title: Warehouse / Logistics is denied overriding pricing
goal: Verify a Warehouse user cannot override quote / order pricing.
preconditions: A Warehouse user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one open draft quote exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Warehouse. Look for any quote / sales-order pricing
area.
steps.expected: No editable pricing surface is reachable.

steps.action: Try the quote URL directly.
steps.expected: The quote does not render an editable pricing surface.

steps.action: If an API is exposed, attempt the price-override call.
steps.expected: The request is rejected.

expected_overall: Warehouse cannot override pricing.
pass_criteria: No override saved AND attempts rejected.

title: Warehouse / Logistics is denied voiding a posted customer invoice
goal:
Verify a Warehouse user cannot void invoices. They confirm
shipments — which trigger invoice creation — but do not adjust
invoices after the fact.
preconditions: A Warehouse user exists with no other roles attached.
At least one posted customer invoice exists.

steps.action:
Sign in as Warehouse. Open the invoice if visible from the
shipment view.
steps.expected: Void / cancel action is hidden or disabled.

steps.action: Attempt the void endpoint via direct URL.
steps.expected: The action is rejected.

steps.action: If an API is exposed, attempt the void-invoice call.
steps.expected: The request is rejected.

expected_overall: Warehouse cannot void an invoice.

title: AP aging detail buckets reconcile to open vendor-invoice balances
goal:
Run the AP aging detail (current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+).
Verify each bucket equals the sum of open vendor invoices whose
age (as of the report date) falls in that bucket, and the grand
total equals the AP control balance.
preconditions: At least one open vendor invoice exists in each of three buckets (current, 1-30, 60+) so bucket cuts are exercised.
The AP control account balance is visible from the trial balance.

steps.action: Run the AP aging detail report as of the close date.
steps.expected:
Report renders with per-vendor rows, per-invoice detail, and
bucket columns.

steps.action:
Pull the open vendor-invoice register as of the same date.
Compute age = (report date - due date) per invoice and bucket.
steps.expected: Per-bucket totals computed.

steps.action: Compare to the report's bucket totals.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01 per bucket.

steps.action: Sum all buckets. Compare to AP control balance.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Spot-check an invoice on a bucket boundary (e.g., due 60 days
ago). Confirm correct bucket placement per the documented
rule.
steps.expected: Boundary placement correct.

expected_overall:
Aging buckets reconcile to invoice ages and grand total equals
AP control.
pass_criteria:
Per-bucket totals match within $0.01 AND grand total = AP control
within $0.01 AND boundary invoice placed correctly.
why_this_matters:
Cash-flow forecasting starts from AP aging. Mis-bucketed invoices
distort which payments are due when, leading to either late fees
or unnecessary borrowing.

title: AP aging drill-through opens the underlying vendor invoice with matching balance
goal:
From AP aging detail, drill into one vendor invoice. Verify it
opens with matching vendor, original amount, applied payments
and credit memos, and remaining balance.
preconditions: At least one partially paid vendor invoice exists or a credit memo has been applied (P3-AP-CM-001).

steps.action: Run AP aging detail.
steps.expected: Renders.

steps.action: Drill into a vendor invoice with a credit memo applied.
steps.expected: Vendor invoice opens.

steps.action:
Compare original - payments - credit memos to the aging row's
balance.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Confirm a fully paid vendor invoice does NOT appear on aging.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
Drill opens the right vendor invoice and aging reflects credit
memos and payments correctly.
pass_criteria:
Aging balance = original - payments - credit memos within $0.01
AND fully paid invoices are absent.

title: AP aging grouped by GL expense category reconciles per category
goal:
Run the AP aging grouped by GL expense / cost category. Verify
each category subtotal equals the sum of open vendor invoices
coded to GL accounts in that category, and category sums roll up
to the AP control balance.
preconditions: Open vendor invoices span at least two distinct GL expense categories (e.g., "Materials" and "Operating Expenses").

steps.action: Run AP aging grouped by GL category.
steps.expected: Report shows per-category subtotals.

steps.action:
For one category, sum the open vendor-invoice balances coded
to GL accounts in it. Compare to the subtotal.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Sum all category subtotals. Compare to AP control balance.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall: Per-category aging reconciles and rolls up to AP.
pass_criteria:
Category subtotal matches hand sum within $0.01 AND sum of
categories equals AP control within $0.01.

title: AR aging detail buckets reconcile to open-invoice balances and bucket-level cuts
goal:
Run the AR aging detail report (current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+).
Verify each bucket equals the sum of open invoices whose age (as
of the report date) falls in that bucket, and the grand total
equals the AR control balance.
preconditions: At least one open invoice exists in each of three buckets (current, 1-30, 90+) so bucket cuts are exercised.
The AR control account balance is visible from the trial balance.

steps.action: Run the AR aging detail report as of the close date.
steps.expected:
Report renders with per-customer rows, per-invoice detail, and
bucket columns.

steps.action:
Pull the open-invoice register as of the same date. For each
open invoice, compute age = (report date - invoice due date).
Place each invoice in its bucket and sum.
steps.expected: Per-bucket totals computed.

steps.action: Compare to the report's bucket totals.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01 per bucket.

steps.action:
Sum all bucket totals. Compare to the AR control balance from
the trial balance.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Spot-check an invoice exactly on a bucket boundary (e.g., due
date = 30 days before report date). Confirm it falls in the
correct bucket per the documented rule (1-30 vs. 31-60).
steps.expected: Boundary placement matches the documented bucket rule.

expected_overall:
Aging buckets reconcile to invoice ages and grand total equals
the AR control balance.
pass_criteria:
Per-bucket totals match within $0.01 AND grand total = AR control
within $0.01 AND boundary invoice placed per the documented rule.
why_this_matters:
Aging is the basis of collections priority and bad-debt reserves.
Mis-bucketed invoices distort both. The boundary check catches
off-by-one bugs that hide in the middle of a normal aging.

title: AR aging drill-through opens the underlying invoice with matching balance
goal:
From the AR aging detail report, drill into one invoice line.
Verify the invoice opens with matching customer, original amount,
applied payments, and remaining balance.
preconditions: At least one partially paid invoice exists (P4-CASH-PARTIAL).

steps.action: Run the AR aging detail.
steps.expected: Renders.

steps.action: Drill into a partially paid invoice's row.
steps.expected: The invoice record opens.

steps.action:
Compare original amount, payments applied, and remaining
balance on the invoice to the aging row's balance.
steps.expected: Aging row balance = original - payments applied within $0.01.

steps.action:
Drill into a fully paid invoice (should NOT appear on the
aging report). Confirm it doesn't show.
steps.expected: Excluded from aging.

expected_overall:
Drill-through opens the right invoice and aging balance reflects
payments correctly.
pass_criteria:
Aging balance = invoice original - payments within $0.01 AND
fully paid invoices are absent from aging.

title: AR aging filtered by sales rep ties to that rep's customer balances
goal:
Run the AR aging filtered to one sales rep / collector. Verify the
total equals the sum of open invoices for customers assigned to
that rep, and per-rep totals roll up to the consolidated AR.
preconditions: Customers with open invoices are assigned to at least two distinct sales reps.

steps.action: Run AR aging filtered to rep A.
steps.expected: Report shows only rep A's customers with open balances.

steps.action:
For one rep-A customer, sum their open invoices. Compare to
their row total.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Run filtered to rep B. Sum all per-rep totals (A + B + ...
unassigned). Compare to the unfiltered AR aging grand total.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Confirm a rep-B customer does NOT appear in rep A's filter.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall: Rep filter scopes correctly and per-rep sum equals consolidated.
pass_criteria:
Per-rep total reconciles within $0.01 AND per-rep sum equals
consolidated within $0.01.

title: Balance sheet ties to AR, AP, inventory, and fixed-asset subledgers
goal:
Run the balance sheet as of the close date and verify that AR, AP,
inventory, and fixed-asset totals match their respective subledger
reports — and that the sheet itself balances.
preconditions: The period has been closed (P5-CLOSE-004).
AR aging, AP aging, inventory valuation, and a depreciation schedule are runnable.

steps.action: Run the balance sheet as of the close date.
steps.expected:
Report renders. Total assets and total liabilities + equity are
both displayed.

steps.action: Verify total assets equal total liabilities + equity.
steps.expected: Equal to the cent.

steps.action: AR control = total of AR aging report. Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: AP control = total of AP aging report. Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Inventory control = total of inventory valuation report. Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Net fixed assets = depreciation schedule's net book value total.
Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Balance sheet balances and every control account ties to its
subledger.
pass_criteria:
All four subledger ties match within $0.01 AND assets = liabilities
+ equity.

title: Comparative balance sheet reconciles each period column to its trial balance
goal:
Run the balance sheet in comparative mode (current close date vs.
prior close date). Verify each column balances and ties to its
respective trial balance.
preconditions: Two sequential periods have been closed (P5-CLOSE-004 run twice).
Trial balance is runnable for both close dates.

steps.action:
Run the comparative balance sheet showing current period close
date and prior period close date.
steps.expected:
Report renders. Both columns show assets, liabilities, equity,
with a totals row and (typically) a variance column.

steps.action:
For the current column: total assets equal total liabilities +
equity.
steps.expected: Equal to the cent.

steps.action:
For the prior column: total assets equal total liabilities +
equity.
steps.expected: Equal to the cent.

steps.action:
Run the trial balance as of the current close date. Each
account on the balance sheet ties to the trial balance net.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Run the trial balance as of the prior close date. Each account
on the prior column ties to that trial balance net.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Both columns balance and reconcile to the trial balance for their
respective close dates.
pass_criteria:
Both columns balance to the cent AND both columns reconcile to
their trial balances within $0.01.

title: Balance sheet drill-through lands on subledger detail
goal:
From the balance sheet, drill into the AR control account, the
inventory account, and the fixed-asset account. Verify each
drill-through opens the corresponding subledger detail with a
matching total.
preconditions: Balance sheet runs cleanly (RPT-BALSHEET-001 has passed).

steps.action: Run the balance sheet as of the close date.
steps.expected: Report renders.

steps.action:
Drill into the AR control account. The drill-through should
land on AR aging detail or an AR transaction list.
steps.expected:
Subledger opens. Total at the bottom equals the AR control
balance within $0.01.

steps.action:
Drill into the inventory account. Should land on the inventory
valuation report.
steps.expected:
Inventory valuation grand total equals the inventory account
balance within $0.01.

steps.action:
Drill into net fixed assets. Should land on the depreciation
schedule or fixed-asset register.
steps.expected:
Net book value total equals the net fixed-asset balance within
$0.01.

expected_overall:
Every subledger drill-through lands on the correct detail and the
detail total matches the control account.
pass_criteria:
All three drill-throughs land on the right subledger AND each
subledger total ties to its control balance within $0.01.
why_this_matters:
Subledger ties are the auditor's first checkpoint. A drill-through
that opens the wrong screen — or one with stale totals — burns
audit hours fast.

title: Per-entity (or per-segment) balance sheet rolls up to consolidated
goal:
Run the balance sheet filtered by entity or segment dimension.
Verify each entity's balance sheet balances independently AND the
per-entity totals roll up to the consolidated balance sheet.
preconditions: Postings carry an entity / segment dimension and at least two distinct values exist.
Each entity has its own AR, inventory, and equity activity.

steps.action: Run the balance sheet filtered to entity A.
steps.expected:
Report renders. Total assets = total liabilities + equity for
that entity to the cent.

steps.action: Run the balance sheet filtered to entity B.
steps.expected: Same — balances to the cent for that entity.

steps.action:
Sum (entity A assets + entity B assets + any others). Compare
to the consolidated balance sheet's total assets.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Repeat for total liabilities + equity.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Each entity's balance sheet balances and per-entity totals roll up
cleanly to the consolidated.
pass_criteria:
Every per-entity sheet balances AND the per-entity sum equals the
consolidated within $0.01.

title: Bookings, billings, and backlog reconcile to SO confirmations, invoices, and open SOs
goal:
Run the bookings / billings / backlog (BBB) report for the
period. Verify bookings = sum of SOs confirmed in the period,
billings = sum of invoices issued in the period, and backlog =
open SO value as of the report date.
preconditions: At least one SO was confirmed in the period (P4-QUOTE-005 or SO confirmation pathway).
At least one invoice was issued in the period (P4-INV-001).
At least one SO remains open (not fully shipped/invoiced) as of the report date.

steps.action: Run the BBB report for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows three numbers (or trend): bookings, billings,
backlog.

steps.action:
Bookings: sum SO total of all SOs confirmed within the
period. Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Billings: sum invoice totals issued in the period. Compare.
steps.expected:
Match within $0.01 (and equals revenue from sales-by-customer
grand total within $0.01).

steps.action:
Backlog: open SO value as of the report date = sum of (SO
total - billed amount) per open SO. Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Roll-forward identity: opening backlog + bookings - billings
= ending backlog. Verify (within $0.01).
steps.expected: Identity holds.

expected_overall:
All three numbers reconcile to source registers and the BBB
identity holds.
pass_criteria:
Bookings, billings, backlog each reconcile within $0.01 AND
the BBB roll-forward identity holds within $0.01.
why_this_matters:
BBB is the headline sales-pipeline KPI. A wrong backlog leads
to wrong forecasting, wrong capacity planning, and wrong board
guidance.

title: Backlog trend over consecutive periods follows opening + bookings - billings = ending
goal:
Run the BBB report in trend mode for 3+ consecutive periods.
Verify each period's ending backlog equals the next period's
opening, and the period-over-period roll-forward identity holds
for every period.
preconditions: SO and invoice activity span at least 3 consecutive periods.

steps.action:
Run the BBB report in trend / multi-period mode for 3
consecutive periods.
steps.expected:
Report shows opening backlog, bookings, billings, ending
backlog per period.

steps.action:
For each period, verify opening + bookings - billings =
ending.
steps.expected: Identity holds within $0.01 per period.

steps.action:
Verify period N's ending backlog equals period N+1's opening
backlog (continuity).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Backlog trend is continuous and roll-forward holds period by
period.
pass_criteria:
Identity holds within $0.01 every period AND period-to-period
continuity holds within $0.01.

title: Budget-vs-actual P&L reconciles actuals to postings and variance is computed correctly
goal:
Run the budget vs. actual P&L for the closed period. Verify the
actual column ties to the regular P&L (RPT-PNL-001), the budget
column ties to the loaded budget, and the variance column equals
(actual - budget) line by line.
preconditions: A budget has been loaded for the period across all P&L lines.
The period has been closed and the regular P&L is runnable.

steps.action: Run budget vs. actual P&L for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows budget, actual, variance, and (often) variance %
per P&L line.

steps.action: Compare actual revenue and expense lines to RPT-PNL-001.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Compare budget revenue and expense lines to the loaded
budget detail.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
For each line, compute (actual - budget). Compare to the
variance column.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01 line-by-line.

steps.action:
Variance % = variance / budget × 100 (per the documented
formula, handling budget = 0). Spot-check one row.
steps.expected: Match within 0.1 pp.

expected_overall:
Actual ties to P&L, budget ties to loaded budget, variance is
arithmetically correct.
pass_criteria:
Actual column ties to RPT-PNL-001 within $0.01 AND budget column
ties to loaded budget within $0.01 AND variance = actual - budget
within $0.01 line-by-line.
why_this_matters:
Department managers and the CFO live in this report. Reconciling
actuals to the P&L every period catches mapping errors before
they propagate to the board pack.

title: Capacity utilization report reconciles to scheduled WO load and calendar
goal:
Run the capacity utilization report by work center and verify the
load-vs-available numbers reconcile to scheduled WO operation
hours and the calendar's available hours.
preconditions: At least one WO is scheduled (released and dated) at a known work center.
The work center's calendar is configured (P1-CAL-001 or its override per P1-WC-003).

steps.action:
Run the capacity utilization report for the next 7 days, by
work center.
steps.expected:
Report shows available hours and load hours per work center
per day.

steps.action:
For one day, sum the routing-operation hours for every scheduled
WO at that work center. This is the load.
steps.expected: Compute by hand or via a query.

steps.action:
For the same day, the available hours = calendar shift duration
minus any planned-downtime block (P1-CAL-003).
steps.expected: Compute.

steps.action: Compare to the report.
steps.expected: Both load and available match within 0.01 hours.

expected_overall: Capacity report reconciles to scheduled load and calendar.
pass_criteria: Load and available hours both match the hand computation.

title: Multi-week capacity utilization aggregates per-day load to per-week and per-range
goal:
Run the capacity utilization report for a 4-week range. Verify
per-week aggregates equal the sum of their per-day load and that
the range total equals the sum of weeks.
preconditions: At least one work center has scheduled WO load on multiple days across a 4-week span.

steps.action: Run capacity utilization for the next 4 weeks, weekly buckets.
steps.expected: Report shows per-week load and available hours per work center.

steps.action:
For one work center / one week, sum the daily load. Compare
to the weekly bucket.
steps.expected: Match within 0.01 hours.

steps.action:
For the same work center, sum the four weekly buckets. Compare
to the report's range total.
steps.expected: Match within 0.01 hours.

steps.action:
Verify weeks containing planned-downtime blocks (e.g., a
holiday) show reduced available hours.
steps.expected:
Available hours dip on the holiday week consistent with the
calendar.

expected_overall:
Multi-week roll-up is correct and calendar overrides flow into the
available-hours total.
pass_criteria:
Daily-to-weekly and weekly-to-range sums match within 0.01 hours
AND holiday weeks show reduced available hours.

title: Capacity utilization filtered to a single work center reflects only that center's load
goal:
Run the capacity utilization report filtered to one work center.
Verify the load comes only from operations at that work center —
no leakage from operations at other centers in the same routing.
preconditions: At least one routing has operations on multiple work centers (P2-ROUTE-001).
At least one WO using such a routing is scheduled (P4-WO-001).

steps.action: Run capacity utilization filtered to work center A.
steps.expected: Report shows load only at WC A.

steps.action:
Pick one WO whose routing has operations at both WC A and WC
B. The load on WC A's report should reflect only the WC A
operation's hours, not the WC B operation.
steps.expected:
Hours on the WC A report match the WC A operation's hours,
not the full routing.

steps.action:
Run the report filtered to WC B for the same WO. The hours
shown should be the WC B operation's hours.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action:
Sum (WC A operation hours + WC B operation hours) and confirm
it equals the routing's total scheduled hours for that WO.
steps.expected: Match.

expected_overall:
Work-center filter splits multi-WC routings correctly per
operation, with no double-counting and no missed operations.
pass_criteria:
Filtered hours per work center match the operation's hours within
0.01 AND the per-WC sum equals the routing's total.

title: Cash flow ties to net income and bank-account movement
goal:
Run the cash flow statement (indirect method) and verify it
reconciles to net income and to the actual change in cash on the
balance sheet.
preconditions: The period is closed and a P&L and balance sheet have been run for it.
Beginning and ending cash balances are visible from the balance sheets.

steps.action: Run the cash flow statement for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows operating, investing, and financing sections plus
net change in cash.

steps.action:
Net income reported on the cash flow's first line equals the
P&L's net income.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action:
Net change in cash equals (ending cash on the balance sheet -
beginning cash on the prior balance sheet).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Spot-check one investing line (e.g., the asset purchase from
P3-ASSET-COMM-001 should appear under investing).
steps.expected:
Asset purchase shows in investing activities for the correct
amount.

expected_overall:
Cash flow ties to net income and to the balance sheet's cash
movement.
pass_criteria:
All three reconciliations match within $0.01 AND a known investing
transaction is in the right section.

title: Direct-method cash flow reconciles to cash-account postings
goal:
Run the cash flow statement in direct-method mode (cash receipts
from customers, cash paid to suppliers, etc.). Verify each line
equals the actual cash-account postings classified to that source.
preconditions: The application supports direct-method cash flow (or both methods).
The period has been closed and a bank rec is complete.

steps.action: Run the cash flow statement in direct-method mode.
steps.expected:
Report shows operating cash inflows / outflows by category
(receipts from customers, payments to suppliers, payroll,
etc.) instead of the indirect adjustments.

steps.action:
Cash receipts from customers: from the bank register, sum
deposits coded as customer payments in the period. Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Cash paid to suppliers: sum bank disbursements coded to
vendor payments in the period. Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Net change in cash = total inflows - total outflows. Compare
to (ending cash - beginning cash) on the balance sheet.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Direct-method cash flow ties to actual bank-account postings and
the net change reconciles to balance-sheet cash movement.
pass_criteria:
Customer receipts, supplier payments, and net change in cash all
reconcile within $0.01.
notes:
Skip this case if the application only supports indirect-method
cash flow; record as N/A rather than a fail.

title: Cash flow drill-through opens the underlying cash transaction
goal:
From the cash flow statement, drill into one investing line (e.g.,
a fixed-asset purchase) and one financing line (e.g., a loan
proceed or repayment). Verify the underlying source transaction
opens with a matching amount.
preconditions: At least one fixed-asset purchase exists in the period (P3-ASSET-COMM-001).
At least one loan, owner contribution, or other financing transaction exists.

steps.action: Run the cash flow statement.
steps.expected:
Report renders with operating, investing, and financing
sections.

steps.action: Drill into the investing-section line for the asset purchase.
steps.expected:
Source transaction (the asset commissioning / capex bill)
opens.

steps.action: Compare its amount to the cash flow line.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Drill into one financing line. Confirm the source loan / equity
transaction opens.
steps.expected: Source opens with a matching amount.

expected_overall:
Both drill-throughs land on real source transactions with matching
amounts.
pass_criteria:
Both drill-throughs open the correct source AND amounts match
within $0.01.
notes:
If the application has no financing activity in the period, sub
in a second investing line.

title: Customer statement reconciles invoices, payments, credit memos, and ending balance
goal:
Generate a customer statement for one customer for a defined
period. Verify the statement lists every invoice, payment, and
credit memo for that customer in the period and that the ending
balance equals the customer's open AR.
preconditions: At least one customer has period activity — an invoice, a partial payment, and (ideally) a credit memo (P4-CM-001).

steps.action:
Generate the customer statement for one customer covering
the period (e.g., the prior month).
steps.expected:
Statement shows opening balance, transactions in date order,
and ending balance.

steps.action:
Compare each line on the statement to the customer's AR
ledger entries for the same dates.
steps.expected: Every entry matches and there are no missing or extra lines.

steps.action:
Verify ending balance = opening + (invoices - payments -
credit memos applied).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Compare the statement's ending balance to the customer's row
on the AR aging detail (RPT-ARAGE-001).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Statement lists complete activity and the ending balance ties
to AR aging.
pass_criteria:
Every transaction in the period appears on the statement AND
ending balance ties to AR aging within $0.01.
why_this_matters:
Customer statements are sent externally. A wrong statement
triggers a customer dispute and erodes confidence in every
invoice that follows.

title: Cycle count history reconciles count entries to inventory adjustments
goal:
Run the cycle count history report for the period. Verify each
count entry's variance equals (counted qty - system qty) at
count time, and that approved variances tie to inventory
adjustment postings.
preconditions: At least one cycle count has been performed in the period (P3-COUNT-001 or P5-CYCLE-001) including one with a non-zero variance.

steps.action: Run the cycle count history report for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows per-count entry: part, location, system qty,
counted qty, variance qty, variance value.

steps.action:
For one count with variance, hand-compute (counted - system)
and (variance × unit cost). Compare.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action:
Sum variance value across approved variances. Compare to
the period's inventory adjustment postings (P5-INV-ADJ).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Confirm a count entry with zero variance does NOT generate
an inventory adjustment posting.
steps.expected: Zero-variance count: no adjustment.

expected_overall:
Cycle count history ties to per-count variance and approved
variances tie to GL adjustments.
pass_criteria:
Variance computation matches per row AND approved variance sum
ties to inventory adjustment postings within $0.01 AND zero-
variance counts produce no adjustment.
why_this_matters:
Cycle counting drives inventory accuracy. If the report doesn't
show real variances or approved variances skip the GL,
inventory accuracy degrades silently.

title: Depreciation schedule reconciles to fixed-asset register and GL postings
goal:
Run the depreciation schedule report and verify it ties to the
fixed-asset register and to the period's GL depreciation postings.
preconditions: At least one fixed asset has been commissioned and depreciation has run (P5-CLOSE-003).

steps.action: Run the depreciation schedule report.
steps.expected:
Report lists each asset with cost, accumulated depreciation,
net book value, and current-period depreciation.

steps.action:
For one asset, hand-compute current-period depreciation per its
method (e.g., straight-line: cost / useful life × period).
steps.expected: Compute and compare to the report row.

steps.action:
Sum current-period depreciation across all assets. Compare to
the GL's depreciation expense postings for the period.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Sum accumulated depreciation. Compare to the accumulated
depreciation contra-asset balance on the trial balance.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Depreciation schedule ties to per-asset method, GL expense, and
accumulated balance.
pass_criteria: All three reconciliations match within $0.01.

title: Depreciation forecast projects future periods consistent with each asset's method
goal:
Run the depreciation schedule's forecast / projection mode (next
12 months or full remaining life). Verify projected per-period
depreciation per asset matches its method and that cumulative
forecast equals (cost - salvage) for assets with predictable
methods (e.g., straight-line).
preconditions: At least one straight-line asset has been commissioned and has remaining useful life.

steps.action: Run the depreciation forecast for the next 12 months.
steps.expected: Report shows per-asset projected depreciation by period.

steps.action:
For one straight-line asset, hand-compute monthly =
(cost - salvage) / useful-life-months. Compare to each
forecast row.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01 per row.

steps.action:
Sum all projected depreciation across the asset's remaining
life. Compare to (cost - salvage - already-accumulated).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
For a declining-balance asset, confirm projected depreciation
drops period-over-period at the configured rate.
steps.expected: Pattern matches the method.

expected_overall:
Forecast respects each asset's method and cumulative forecast
equals (cost - salvage - accumulated) per asset.
pass_criteria:
Straight-line per-period match within $0.01 AND cumulative
matches (cost - salvage - accumulated) within $0.01.
why_this_matters:
Capex planning and budgeting depend on this forecast. A wrong
projection drives wrong cash and tax planning.

title: Depreciation schedule drill-through opens the asset record
goal:
From the depreciation schedule, drill into one asset row. Verify
the underlying asset record opens with matching cost, useful life,
method, and accumulated depreciation.
preconditions: At least one fixed asset has been commissioned and depreciated in at least one period.

steps.action: Run the depreciation schedule.
steps.expected: Renders.

steps.action: Drill into one asset row.
steps.expected:
The asset record opens with cost, useful life, method, salvage
value, and accumulated depreciation visible.

steps.action:
Compare cost, useful life, method, and accumulated to the
schedule row.
steps.expected: All four match.

steps.action:
From the asset record, view depreciation history. Sum the
period postings. Compare to the schedule's accumulated.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Drill exposes the correct asset and historical postings sum to
accumulated.
pass_criteria:
All four key fields match between schedule and asset record AND
history sum equals accumulated within $0.01.

title: Excess and obsolete (E&O) inventory report reconciles flagged parts to last-activity dates
goal:
Run the E&O inventory report. Verify each flagged part's last-
movement date and on-hand value match the inventory and
transaction history, and aging buckets (e.g., 0-90, 91-180,
181-365, 365+ days) are populated by last-activity date.
preconditions: At least one part has had no issue / consumption activity for 180+ days (a slow mover) AND at least one part is actively moving (should NOT be flagged).

steps.action: Run the E&O inventory report.
steps.expected:
Report shows per-part: on-hand qty, on-hand value, last
movement date, age in days, aging bucket.

steps.action:
For one slow-mover, pull the part's transaction history.
Confirm last issue / consumption matches the report's last-
movement date.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action:
Verify the part is bucketed by (report date - last movement)
into the correct aging bucket per the documented rule.
steps.expected: Correct bucket.

steps.action:
Sum on-hand value for the 365+ bucket. This is the headline
"obsolete inventory" exposure.
steps.expected: Subtotal available.

steps.action:
Confirm an actively moving part (last issue ≤ 30 days ago)
is NOT in the report (or only in the youngest bucket).
steps.expected: Either excluded or in the freshest bucket.

expected_overall:
E&O report ties to last-activity dates and aging buckets are
correct.
pass_criteria:
Slow-mover last-movement date matches transaction history AND
bucket placement matches documented rule AND active part not
flagged as old.
why_this_matters:
E&O drives reserve provisions and write-down decisions. A wrong
age makes the company either over-reserve (hitting earnings) or
under-reserve (delaying losses).

title: Employee labor distribution reconciles to labor postings on WOs
goal:
Run the employee labor distribution report and verify each
employee's total hours equal the sum of their WO labor entries
for the period.
preconditions: At least one employee has recorded labor against WOs in the period (P4-LABOR).

steps.action: Run the employee labor distribution report for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows per employee: direct hours, indirect hours, total
hours, possibly broken down by work center or WO.

steps.action:
Pick one employee. Pull their labor entries for the period.
Sum the hours.
steps.expected: Total available.

steps.action: Compare to the report's row for that employee.
steps.expected: Match within 0.01 hours.

steps.action:
Spot-check the breakdown: at least one direct-labor entry on a
WO and (if any) one indirect-labor / stoppage entry should both
appear in the right buckets.
steps.expected: Categorization is correct.

expected_overall: Employee labor distribution ties to underlying labor entries.
pass_criteria:
Per-employee totals match labor entry sum within 0.01 hours AND
direct/indirect categorization is correct.

title: Employee labor distribution grouped by department aggregates correctly
goal:
Run the employee labor distribution report grouped by department.
Verify each department's hours equal the sum of its employees'
hours, and per-department totals roll up to the consolidated.
preconditions: Employees in at least two departments have recorded labor in the period.

steps.action: Run the labor distribution report grouped by department.
steps.expected: Report shows department subtotals.

steps.action:
For one department, sum each member employee's total hours
from the per-employee detail. Compare to the department
subtotal.
steps.expected: Match within 0.01 hours.

steps.action:
Sum all department subtotals. Compare to the unfiltered
consolidated.
steps.expected: Match within 0.01 hours.

steps.action:
Confirm an employee assigned to a different department does
NOT appear under the first department.
steps.expected: Correct attribution.

expected_overall:
Department grouping reflects employee-to-department mapping and
totals reconcile.
pass_criteria:
Per-department subtotal matches employee-detail sum within 0.01
hours AND consolidated equals sum of departments within 0.01.

title: Employee labor distribution broken down by work center reconciles to per-WC clocking
goal:
Run the employee labor distribution report broken down by work
center. Verify each (employee, work center) cell equals the sum
of that employee's labor entries at that work center.
preconditions: At least one employee has logged labor at two or more distinct work centers (P4-LABOR with multiple WO/op entries).

steps.action: Run the labor distribution by work center.
steps.expected: Report shows employee × work center matrix or grouped detail.

steps.action:
Pick one employee. For each work center where they logged
time, sum the hours. Compare to the cell.
steps.expected: Match within 0.01 hours per cell.

steps.action:
Sum that employee's row across all work centers. Compare to
their total hours (from RPT-EMPLABOR-001).
steps.expected: Match within 0.01 hours.

steps.action:
Confirm a work center the employee never clocked at shows
zero (or no row), not phantom hours.
steps.expected: No phantom hours.

expected_overall:
Per-WC employee labor reconciles cell-by-cell and rows sum to
total hours.
pass_criteria:
Each cell matches hand-summed labor entries within 0.01 hours
AND row sum matches total hours within 0.01.

title: Fixed-asset roll-forward reconciles opening, additions, disposals, depreciation, ending
goal:
Run the fixed-asset roll-forward report (opening cost + additions
- disposals = ending cost; opening accumulated + depreciation -
disposals = ending accumulated). Verify each column ties to the
underlying activity in the period.
preconditions: The period had at least one asset addition (P3-ASSET-COMM-001), one disposal/retirement (P5-ASSET-RETIRE-001), and one period of depreciation (P5-CLOSE-003).

steps.action: Run the fixed-asset roll-forward for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows by category (or asset class):
cost: opening, additions, disposals, ending
accumulated: opening, depreciation, disposals, ending
net book value: opening, ending

steps.action:
Cost roll: opening cost + period additions - period disposals
= ending cost. Verify the math row-by-row.
steps.expected: Each category row balances to the cent.

steps.action:
Sum period additions. Compare to capex activity (P3-ASSET-COMM
events) for the period.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Period depreciation column equals current-period depreciation
from RPT-DEPSCH-001.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Ending net book value = opening NBV + additions - disposals -
depreciation. Confirm.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Roll-forward arithmetic holds and each activity column ties to
underlying transactions.
pass_criteria:
All three columns (cost, accumulated, NBV) reconcile within
$0.01 AND additions and depreciation tie to source activity.
why_this_matters:
This report is the auditor's fixed-asset workpaper. Reconciliation
failures here cause restatements; getting it right monthly avoids
year-end fire drills.

title: GR/IR aging report ties to receipts not yet invoiced and invoices not yet received
goal:
Run the GR/IR (Goods Received / Invoice Received) aging report.
Verify the open balances equal the sum of (receipts not matched
to a vendor invoice) and (vendor invoices not matched to a
receipt), bucketed by age. The GR/IR control account on the
trial balance should equal the report grand total.
preconditions: At least one PO has been received but not yet invoiced (P3-RECV-001 without follow-up P3-AP-001 for that PO).
The GR/IR clearing account is configured and visible on the trial balance (P1-GL-001).

steps.action: Run the GR/IR aging report as of the close date.
steps.expected:
Report shows per-PO open balance with receipt and invoice
side and aging bucket.

steps.action:
For one PO, identify receipt qty × unit price (received-not-
invoiced) and any partial vendor-invoice qty already matched.
Compute open GR/IR = (received × cost) - (invoiced × cost).
steps.expected: Computed.

steps.action: Compare to the report's row.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Sum the report's grand total. Compare to the GR/IR clearing
account balance on the trial balance.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Confirm a fully matched PO (receipt = invoice) does NOT
appear on the report.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
GR/IR aging ties to per-PO open mismatch and totals reconcile to
the clearing account.
pass_criteria:
Per-PO open matches hand computation within $0.01 AND grand
total equals GR/IR clearing balance within $0.01 AND fully
matched POs are excluded.
why_this_matters:
GR/IR is one of the most common period-close stuck items. A
silently growing clearing account hides duplicate payments,
missing invoices, or pricing mismatches.

title: Headcount and turnover report reconciles opening, hires, terms, and ending headcount
goal:
Run the headcount and turnover report for the period. Verify
opening + hires - terminations = ending headcount, and turnover
% = terminations / average headcount over the period.
preconditions: At least one hire (P4-HIRE-001) and one termination (P5-HR-TERM-001) occurred in the period.

steps.action: Run the headcount and turnover report for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows opening, hires, terminations, ending, and
turnover %.

steps.action:
Pull the employee register: count active as of opening date
and as of ending date.
steps.expected: Compare to opening and ending. Match.

steps.action:
Count hires (status change to active) in the period.
Compare.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action:
Count terminations (status change from active) in the
period. Compare.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action: Verify opening + hires - terminations = ending.
steps.expected: Identity holds exactly.

steps.action:
Turnover % = terminations / average headcount × 100, where
average = (opening + ending) / 2 (or per the documented
formula). Compute and compare.
steps.expected: Match within 0.1 pp.

expected_overall:
Headcount roll-forward holds and turnover % reflects employee
status changes.
pass_criteria:
Headcount identity holds exactly AND turnover % matches within
0.1 pp.
why_this_matters:
HR uses this for board reporting and budgeting. A miscount
hides retention problems and budget pressures.

title: Inventory turnover reconciles annualized COGS and average inventory per part
goal:
Run the inventory turnover report for the period. Verify per-
part turnover = annualized COGS / average inventory value, and
days-on-hand = 365 / turnover. Spot-check inputs against the
P&L COGS and inventory valuation.
preconditions: The closed period has COGS activity and the inventory valuation has been run (RPT-INVVAL-001).

steps.action: Run the inventory turnover report for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows per-part: COGS, average inventory, turnover,
days on hand.

steps.action:
For one part, identify period COGS (from completion or
shipping postings) and annualize: COGS × (12 / period
months). Compute average inventory = (opening + ending) / 2
from inventory valuation.
steps.expected: Computed.

steps.action:
Compute turnover = annualized COGS / average inventory.
Compare to the report.
steps.expected: Match within 0.1 turns.

steps.action: Days on hand = 365 / turnover. Compare.
steps.expected: Match within 1 day.

steps.action:
Sum per-part annualized COGS. Compare to (P&L COGS × 12 /
period months) total.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01 (or document a known reconciling item).

expected_overall:
Per-part turnover and DOH reconcile to source COGS and
inventory valuation.
pass_criteria:
Spot-checked turnover matches within 0.1 turns AND DOH within
1 day AND total annualized COGS reconciles to P&L within
$0.01.
why_this_matters:
Turnover and DOH are the headline working-capital KPIs. A wrong
number drives wrong inventory targets and tied-up cash.

title: Inventory valuation report ties to the GL inventory account
goal:
Run the inventory valuation report and verify it equals the GL
inventory control account balance to the cent. This is the report
P5-CLOSE-001 leans on.
preconditions: Inventory exists for at least one part.
At least one period has been through the close cycle.

steps.action:
Run the inventory valuation report (system on-hand × cost) as
of the close date.
steps.expected:
Report displays per-part on-hand × unit cost = extended value,
with a grand total.

steps.action:
Run the GL trial balance and read the inventory account net
balance as of the same date.
steps.expected: The balance is visible.

steps.action: Compare grand total to GL inventory balance.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Spot-check one lot-tracked part: sum the open lots' values from
the lot detail. Compare to the part's row total in the report.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall: Inventory valuation ties to GL and lot detail rolls up correctly.
pass_criteria:
Grand total = GL inventory balance within $0.01 AND the spot-checked
lot rollup matches.

title: Inventory valuation filtered by location reconciles to bin-level inventory
goal:
Run the inventory valuation report filtered to a single location
(warehouse or plant). Verify the per-location total equals the sum
of bin-level on-hand × cost at that location, and that all
per-location totals roll up to the consolidated valuation.
preconditions: Inventory exists at two or more distinct locations / warehouses.
At least one part is stocked at multiple locations to verify the location filter splits the part correctly.

steps.action: Run the inventory valuation filtered to location A.
steps.expected: Report shows only parts/qty at location A with extended values.

steps.action:
For one bin in location A, sum (bin qty × unit cost). Compare
to the report's part row at location A.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Run the report filtered to location B. Sum its grand total.
steps.expected: Available.

steps.action:
Sum (location A grand total + location B grand total + any
other locations). Compare to the unfiltered consolidated
valuation.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Pick a part stocked at multiple locations. Confirm the per-
location quantities sum to the consolidated quantity.
steps.expected: Match exactly (no rounding on quantity).

expected_overall:
Per-location valuation reconciles to bin-level detail and rolls
up cleanly to the consolidated total.
pass_criteria:
Bin-level spot check matches within $0.01 AND per-location sum
equals consolidated within $0.01 AND multi-location part qty
reconciles exactly.

title: Inventory valuation reflects the configured cost method per part
goal:
For a part costed FIFO (or LIFO, weighted average), verify the
unit cost on the valuation report equals the cost computed from
the open-receipt layers (FIFO/LIFO) or the rolling weighted-
average cost.
preconditions: At least one part is configured for FIFO or weighted-average costing AND has at least two receipts at different unit costs (P3-RECV-001 run twice with different prices).

steps.action:
Run the inventory valuation report. Note the unit cost shown
for the part in question.
steps.expected: Unit cost is displayed.

steps.action:
Pull the part's receipt history. For FIFO, the open layers
are the most recent receipts whose cumulative qty equals
on-hand; compute extended value = sum(layer qty × layer cost).
steps.expected: Compute and divide by on-hand to get the implied unit cost.

steps.action:
For weighted average: weighted avg = sum(receipt qty × receipt
cost) / sum(receipt qty), adjusted for issues.
steps.expected: Compute.

steps.action: Compare hand-computed unit cost to the report's unit cost.
steps.expected: Match within $0.0001 per unit (or $0.01 on the extended).

expected_overall:
Cost method is being applied correctly: report's unit cost
matches the hand-computed FIFO / weighted-average cost.
pass_criteria:
Hand-computed unit cost matches the report's unit cost within
$0.0001 per unit AND extended value matches within $0.01.
why_this_matters:
A wrong cost method silently mis-states inventory and COGS
every period. Spot-checking one part per method on every release
is the cheapest insurance against this class of bug.

title: Inventory valuation drill-through opens lot or serial detail
goal:
From the inventory valuation report, drill into a lot-tracked or
serial-tracked part. Verify the drill lists the open lots / serials
and their values sum to the part's row total.
preconditions: At least one lot- or serial-tracked part has on-hand stock spanning two or more lots / serials.

steps.action: Run the inventory valuation report.
steps.expected: Renders.

steps.action: Drill into a lot-tracked part's row.
steps.expected:
A list of open lots opens, each with qty, unit cost, and
extended value.

steps.action:
Sum the extended values across the listed lots. Compare to
the part's row in the parent report.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Drill into one specific lot. Confirm its expiry date, source
receipt PO, and on-hand quantity match the lot detail screen.
steps.expected: Lot record opens with matching attributes.

expected_overall:
Drill-through opens correct lot / serial detail and sums roll up
to the part row.
pass_criteria:
Lot list sum matches the part row within $0.01 AND the drilled-in
lot detail shows correct attributes.

title: Kitting variance reconciles per-kit component-issue variance to BOM standard
goal:
Run the kitting / sub-assembly variance report. Verify each kit
WO's variance equals (actual components issued - BOM standard
qty) × component cost.
preconditions: At least one kit / sub-assembly WO has been completed (P4-WO-BACKFLUSH-001 or a WO with kit/phantom BOM in P2-BOM-002).

steps.action:
Run the kitting / sub-assembly variance report for the
period.
steps.expected:
Report shows per-kit WO: BOM standard qty per component,
actual issued qty, variance qty, variance value.

steps.action:
For one closed kit WO, look up the BOM standard quantities
and the actual issue records. Compute (actual - standard) ×
cost per component.
steps.expected: Computed.

steps.action: Compare to the report's WO row.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01 per component AND total variance.

steps.action:
Confirm a finished kit produced exactly to BOM has zero
variance.
steps.expected: Zero-variance kit shows zero.

expected_overall:
Kit variance reflects component-level deviation from BOM
standard with correct sign.
pass_criteria:
Per-component variance matches hand computation within $0.01
AND zero-variance kits show zero.

title: Lead funnel reconciles per-stage lead counts to underlying lead and opportunity records
goal:
Run the lead funnel report for the period. Verify each stage
count (new lead → qualified → opportunity → quote → SO) equals
the count of records currently in or converted from that stage,
and stage-to-stage conversion rates compute correctly.
preconditions: At least 5 leads exist with stage history spanning at least 3 funnel stages.

steps.action: Run the lead funnel report for the period.
steps.expected: Report shows per-stage counts and stage-to-stage conversion %.

steps.action:
Filter the lead register by stage = "qualified" entered in
the period. Count.
steps.expected: Compare to the report's qualified count. Match.

steps.action: Repeat for "opportunity" and "quote" stages.
steps.expected: Match per stage.

steps.action:
Verify stage-to-stage conversion = (next stage count) /
(prior stage count). Compute and compare.
steps.expected: Match within 0.1 pp.

steps.action:
Confirm a lead marked "lost" is excluded from the active
funnel counts.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
Funnel counts and conversion rates reflect actual lead stage
history.
pass_criteria:
Per-stage count matches lead-register filter AND stage-to-stage
conversion matches within 0.1 pp AND lost leads excluded.

title: Forward lot trace shows full path from raw-material lot to finished goods and customer
goal:
Run the forward lot trace report starting from a single raw-
material lot. Verify the report lists every WO that consumed
that lot, every finished-good lot/serial produced from those
WOs, and every shipment that included those finished goods.
preconditions: A raw-material lot has been received (P3-RECV-001 with lot enabled) and consumed in at least one WO (P4-MATL-ISSUE).
That WO has completed and the finished goods have shipped (P4-COMP-FINAL, P4-SHIP).

steps.action:
Open the lot trace report and select forward direction
starting from the raw-material lot.
steps.expected:
Report displays a tree or chain: raw lot → WOs that consumed
it → finished lots → shipments → customers.

steps.action:
Pull the raw lot's transaction history. List the WOs that
issued from this lot. Compare to the report's WO list.
steps.expected: Same WOs appear in the report.

steps.action:
For one WO in the chain, list the finished lots / serials
produced. Compare to the report's downstream nodes.
steps.expected: Same finished lots appear.

steps.action:
For one finished lot, identify the shipment(s) and customer.
Compare to the report's leaf nodes.
steps.expected: Same customer / shipment appears.

steps.action: Confirm a WO that did NOT consume this raw lot is absent.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
Forward trace correctly chains raw lot → WOs → finished lots →
shipments with no missing or extraneous nodes.
pass_criteria:
Every node in the trace matches manual trace through transaction
history AND no unrelated WO/shipment appears.
why_this_matters:
Forward trace drives recall scope. Missing a downstream WO means
customers eat the recall risk; including unrelated WOs creates
unnecessary recall cost.

title: Backward lot trace from finished serial walks back to all source raw lots
goal:
Run the backward (genealogy) lot trace report from a finished
serial number. Verify the trace lists every raw-material lot
consumed in the WO that produced it, with quantities matching
the issue records.
preconditions: A finished serial has been produced from a WO that consumed multiple raw lots (P4-COMP-FINAL with multi-component BOM).

steps.action:
Open the lot trace report and select backward direction from
the finished serial.
steps.expected:
Report shows the producing WO, then the raw-material lots
consumed by that WO with quantities.

steps.action:
Compare the listed raw lots to the WO's material issue
records (P4-MATL-ISSUE).
steps.expected: Same lots, same issue quantities.

steps.action:
Pick one raw lot in the trace. Confirm its receipt PO and
vendor are correct (drill-through if available).
steps.expected: Receipt info matches the lot record.

steps.action:
Confirm a raw lot that was NOT consumed by this WO does NOT
appear in the trace.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
Backward trace correctly identifies every source lot for the
finished serial with matching quantities.
pass_criteria:
Trace's source lot list matches WO issue records exactly AND
unrelated lots are excluded.
why_this_matters:
Quality root-cause investigations start here. A backward trace
that misses a contaminated source lot wastes investigation time
and risks more incidents.

title: Gross margin by product reconciles per-part revenue, COGS, and margin to source records
goal:
Run the gross margin by product report. Verify per-part revenue
equals invoice line revenue (RPT-SALESPROD-001), per-part COGS
equals the cost component of those invoice lines (or the WO
completion COGS posting), and gross margin = revenue - COGS.
preconditions: At least one part has invoice line activity in the period AND a corresponding WO completion / standard cost.

steps.action: Run gross margin by product for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows per-part: units sold, revenue, COGS, gross
margin $, gross margin %.

steps.action:
For one part, compare revenue to its row on
RPT-SALESPROD-001.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
For the same part, sum the COGS posted at invoicing (or WO
completion) for those invoice lines. Compare to the report's
COGS column.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Verify gross margin $ = revenue - COGS and gross margin % =
margin $ / revenue × 100 per row.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01 and 0.1 pp.

steps.action:
Sum total revenue and total COGS across the report.
Compare to the P&L's revenue and COGS.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Per-part revenue, COGS, and margin reconcile to source records
and roll up to the P&L.
pass_criteria:
Per-part revenue and COGS each reconcile within $0.01 AND
margin computation is correct AND grand totals tie to P&L.
why_this_matters:
Product-level margin drives pricing and rationalization. A
wrong margin steers the company toward unprofitable products
and away from profitable ones.

title: MRP exception report identifies real material shortfalls
goal:
Run the MRP exception report and verify it flags only the parts
whose projected supply is short of demand within the planning
horizon, with no false positives or false negatives.
preconditions: At least one open SO with demand exists.
At least one part has demand that exceeds on-hand + on-order minus safety stock.
At least one part has supply ≥ demand (should NOT appear in the exception report).

steps.action: Run the MRP exception report.
steps.expected:
Report lists parts with projected shortage, including: required
date, shortage quantity, suggested action (PO, expedite,
transfer).

steps.action:
Verify the part you know is short appears in the report with
the right shortage quantity.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action: Verify the part you know is fine does NOT appear.
steps.expected: Not present.

steps.action:
Pick one entry. Hand-verify: demand per SO + safety stock vs.
on-hand + on-order. Compute the shortage.
steps.expected: Hand-computed shortage matches the report.

expected_overall:
MRP exception report correctly identifies real shortfalls and skips
non-shortfalls.
pass_criteria:
Known-short part is present AND known-fine part is absent AND
spot-checked shortage matches hand computation.
why_this_matters:
An MRP exception report with false positives gets ignored. One with
false negatives causes stockouts. Both are common bugs and both
silently destroy trust in the planning function.

title: MRP exception filtered by buyer ties to buyer-assigned parts only
goal:
Run the MRP exception report filtered to a specific buyer or
planner. Verify the report shows only parts whose default buyer
matches and that all listed shortages reconcile to source demand.
preconditions: At least two parts have different default buyers configured (P2-PART-001 with buyer assignment).
Both buyers have at least one part in shortage and one part not in shortage.

steps.action: Run the MRP exception report filtered to buyer A.
steps.expected: Report lists only buyer-A parts that are short.

steps.action:
Pick one buyer-A short part. Hand-compute the shortage
(demand + safety stock - on-hand - on-order). Compare.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action:
Confirm a short part assigned to buyer B does NOT appear in
the buyer-A filter.
steps.expected: Excluded.

steps.action:
Confirm a buyer-A part that is fully covered does NOT appear
in the filter.
steps.expected: Excluded (the filter doesn't make non-shortages appear).

expected_overall:
Buyer filter correctly scopes the exception list and shortage
computation remains accurate.
pass_criteria:
Buyer-A short part shortage matches hand-computation AND
buyer-B parts and non-shortages are correctly excluded.
why_this_matters:
Buyers run their daily list off this filter. A buyer who sees
parts they don't own — or misses parts they do — manages the
wrong queue.

title: MRP exception report with longer planning horizon flags later-period shortages
goal:
Run the MRP exception with a 30-day horizon, then again with a
90-day horizon. Verify a part that is fine in 30 days but short in
60 days appears only in the 90-day run, with the right required
date.
preconditions: At least one part has demand 60-90 days out that exceeds on-hand + on-order through that date but is fine within 30 days (P3-FCST-001 has loaded forward demand).

steps.action: Run the MRP exception report with horizon = 30 days.
steps.expected: Report does NOT include the late-shortage part.

steps.action: Run the same report with horizon = 90 days.
steps.expected:
Report DOES include the late-shortage part with a required
date in the 60-90 day window.

steps.action:
Hand-compute: cumulative demand through 90 days minus on-hand
minus on-order arriving in that window. Confirm shortage qty
and required date.
steps.expected: Match.

expected_overall:
Horizon parameter correctly extends the look-ahead and the late-
period shortage is identified at the right date.
pass_criteria:
Late shortage is absent at 30-day horizon AND present at 90-day
horizon AND required date matches hand-computation.

title: OEE report reconciles to availability, performance, and quality records
goal:
Run the OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) report for a work
center and verify the three component metrics reconcile to source
data: availability from stoppage records, performance from cycle
times, and quality from QC pass / scrap records.
preconditions: At least one work center ran a complete WO with labor, completion, and any QC outcomes recorded.
Stoppage data has been recorded (P5-STOPPAGE) at least once.

steps.action: Run the OEE report for the work center for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows availability %, performance %, quality %, and
composite OEE %.

steps.action:
Availability: (planned production time - stoppage time) /
planned production time. Compute by pulling calendar planned
hours and subtracting recorded stoppage minutes.
steps.expected: Compute. Compare to the report's availability.

steps.action:
Performance: actual cycle output / theoretical output at routing
standard.
steps.expected: Compute. Compare to the report.

steps.action: Quality: good units completed / total units completed.
steps.expected: Compute. Compare to the report.

expected_overall: All three OEE components reconcile to source records.
pass_criteria: Each component matches the report within 0.5 percentage points.
why_this_matters:
OEE is the headline manufacturing metric. If it's wrong, plant
managers either chase ghosts or miss real problems.

title: OEE per shift reconciles to that shift's stoppage and completion records
goal:
Run OEE per shift (e.g., 1st shift vs. 2nd shift) for one work
center. Verify each shift's availability, performance, and quality
reconcile to that shift's stoppage and completion records.
preconditions: At least two shifts have run the same work center on the same day with distinct stoppage and completion records.

steps.action: Run OEE per shift for the work center.
steps.expected:
Report shows availability %, performance %, quality %, and
composite OEE % per shift.

steps.action:
For shift 1: planned production time minus shift-1 stoppage
time, divided by planned. Compute and compare to shift 1's
availability.
steps.expected: Match within 0.5 percentage points.

steps.action:
Repeat performance and quality for shift 1 from shift-1
records.
steps.expected: Match within 0.5 pp.

steps.action: Repeat for shift 2.
steps.expected: Match within 0.5 pp.

steps.action:
Confirm a stoppage event tagged to shift 1 does NOT count
against shift 2.
steps.expected: No cross-shift leakage.

expected_overall: Per-shift OEE reflects only that shift's records.
pass_criteria:
All three components per shift match hand computation within
0.5 pp AND no cross-shift leakage.

title: OEE across a multi-day range respects the date filter and rolls up
goal:
Run OEE for a 7-day range (or longer) and verify the daily OEE
values compose correctly into the range OEE — and that
out-of-range stoppages don't leak in.
preconditions: Stoppage and completion records span 7+ days at one work center.

steps.action: Run OEE for the 7-day range with a daily breakdown.
steps.expected: Report shows per-day OEE plus a range total.

steps.action:
For one day, hand-compute OEE from that day's stoppage and
completion. Compare to the daily row.
steps.expected: Match within 0.5 pp.

steps.action:
Range OEE: aggregate availability across the 7 days = (sum
planned time - sum stoppage) / sum planned time. Performance
and quality similarly aggregated. Compute composite.
steps.expected: Match the report's range OEE within 0.5 pp.

steps.action:
Confirm a stoppage from 1 day before the range start does
NOT influence the range OEE.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
OEE respects the date range and per-day numbers aggregate
correctly.
pass_criteria:
Per-day OEE matches hand computation within 0.5 pp AND range
OEE matches aggregated computation within 0.5 pp AND boundary
stoppages excluded.

title: Preventive maintenance summary reconciles scheduled, completed, and overdue PMs
goal:
Run the preventive maintenance summary for the period. Verify
scheduled PM count, completed-on-time count, and overdue count
reflect actual PM work-order history, and PM compliance % =
(completed on time) / (scheduled in period) × 100.
preconditions: At least one PM was scheduled and completed on time (P5-PM-001) and at least one is overdue (or completed late).

steps.action: Run the PM summary for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows scheduled, completed on-time, completed late,
overdue, and compliance %.

steps.action:
Pull PM work orders scheduled to be due in the period.
Count.
steps.expected: Compare to scheduled count. Match.

steps.action:
Of those, count completed on or before due date. Compare to
on-time count. Match.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action: Compliance % = on-time / scheduled × 100.
steps.expected: Match within 0.1 pp.

steps.action:
Confirm an asset's overdue PM appears in the overdue
bucket, not in completed.
steps.expected: Correct bucket.

expected_overall:
PM summary reflects scheduled, completion, and overdue status
accurately.
pass_criteria:
Counts match hand-summed PM history AND compliance % matches
within 0.1 pp AND overdue PMs are in the overdue bucket.
why_this_matters:
PM compliance is regulatory in some industries and operational
in all. A wrong overdue count masks safety and uptime risk.

title: P&L reconciles to source revenue, COGS, and expense postings
goal:
Run the income statement (P&L) for the closed period and verify that
revenue, cost of goods sold, and operating expense totals tie to the
underlying transactions that produced them.
preconditions: The period has been closed (P5-CLOSE-004).
At least one customer invoice was posted in the period (P4-INV-001).
At least one WO was completed in the period (P4-COMP-FINAL).
At least one vendor invoice was posted (P3-AP-001).

steps.action: Run the P&L for the closed period.
steps.expected:
Report renders. Revenue, COGS, gross margin, operating expense,
and net income are visible.

steps.action:
Total revenue: from the customer-invoice register, sum the
revenue lines posted in the period. Compare to the P&L's revenue.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Total COGS: from the WO completion postings, sum the cost of
goods sold posted in the period. Compare to the P&L's COGS.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Total operating expense: from vendor invoices and JEs posted to
expense accounts in the period, sum. Compare to the P&L total
expense.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
If the P&L supports drill-through, click into a revenue line to
verify the underlying invoice opens.
steps.expected: Drill-through resolves to the source transaction.

expected_overall:
P&L totals tie out to source transaction sums and drill-through
resolves correctly.
pass_criteria:
Revenue, COGS, and expense totals each reconcile within $0.01 to
the source-transaction sum.
why_this_matters:
P&Ls that don't tie out are a leadership-credibility bug. Fixing
the underlying postings is much harder than catching the
reconciliation gap before publishing.

title: P&L for a custom date range ties to postings dated within that range
goal:
Run the P&L for a date range that does not align with the closed
fiscal period (e.g., a single week or a partial month). Verify the
totals reconcile to the postings whose effective dates fall within
that exact range — no leakage in or out at the boundaries.
preconditions: At least one customer invoice and one vendor invoice have been posted in the closed period (P5-CLOSE-004).
Postings exist on multiple dates so a sub-period range is meaningful.

steps.action:
Run the P&L with a custom range starting after the period's
first posting and ending before the period's last posting.
steps.expected:
Report renders. Revenue, COGS, expense, and net income are
shown for the chosen range only.

steps.action:
Sum customer-invoice revenue dated within (and only within) the
chosen range. Compare to the report's revenue.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Pick one invoice dated one day before the start date. Confirm
its revenue is NOT in the report total.
steps.expected: Excluded.

steps.action:
Pick one invoice dated one day after the end date. Confirm
its revenue is NOT in the report total.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
P&L respects the date filter at both boundaries and totals match
the date-filtered source sum.
pass_criteria:
Revenue total matches the date-filtered invoice sum within $0.01
AND boundary postings are correctly included/excluded.
why_this_matters:
Off-by-one date filters are a classic reporting bug, especially at
midnight / time-zone boundaries. A controller running ad-hoc ranges
for a board pack must trust the filter.

title: P&L filtered by department or dimension ties to dimension-tagged postings
goal:
Run the P&L filtered to a single department, location, or other
GL dimension. Verify the totals equal the sum of postings tagged
with that dimension only.
preconditions: Postings in the closed period carry at least two distinct department/dimension tags (so filtering is meaningful).
Each tag has at least one revenue and one expense posting.

steps.action:
Run the P&L for the closed period filtered to one department
/ dimension value (e.g., "Operations" or location "Plant 1").
steps.expected:
Report renders showing only the chosen dimension's revenue,
COGS, and expense.

steps.action:
