From the JE / invoice register, sum revenue postings tagged
to that dimension. Compare to the filtered report.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Run the P&L with no dimension filter. The unfiltered total
should equal the sum of every per-dimension P&L.
steps.expected: Sum of dimension P&Ls = unfiltered P&L within $0.01.

steps.action:
Confirm a posting tagged to a different dimension does NOT
appear in the filtered report's revenue total.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
Dimension-filtered P&L reconciles to dimension-tagged source
postings and per-dimension P&Ls roll up to the consolidated total.
pass_criteria:
Filtered total matches dimension-tagged source within $0.01 AND
per-dimension sum equals the consolidated P&L within $0.01.

title: P&L drill-through lands on the underlying source transaction
goal:
Click drill-through on a P&L revenue line and a P&L expense line.
Verify each opens the exact source transaction (invoice or vendor
bill / JE) for the same amount.
preconditions: At least one customer invoice and one vendor invoice (or expense JE) are posted in the closed period.

steps.action: Run the P&L for the closed period.
steps.expected: Report renders.

steps.action:
Click into the revenue line. Drill down progressively (revenue
→ account detail → transaction list → individual invoice).
steps.expected:
Each drill level shows totals that sum to the parent level.
The deepest level lists individual invoices.

steps.action:
Open one invoice from the deepest list. Confirm its amount,
date, and customer match the system invoice record.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action:
Repeat for an operating-expense line. Drill down to the source
vendor bill or JE.
steps.expected: Source transaction opens with matching amount.

steps.action:
Sum all transactions in the deepest drill list. Compare to the
P&L line total.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Drill-through paths land on real source transactions and the
drill-level sums match the parent line.
pass_criteria:
Both drill-throughs (revenue and expense) open the correct source
AND the drill-list sum reconciles to the parent line within $0.01.
why_this_matters:
A drill-through that lands on the wrong record — or just summary
data — destroys auditor trust. Each level must reconcile.

title: Comparative P&L (current vs. prior period) reconciles each column
goal:
Run the P&L in comparative mode showing current period vs. prior
period. Verify each column independently ties to its underlying
postings, and the variance column equals (current - prior) line by
line.
preconditions: At least two consecutive fiscal periods have been closed (P5-CLOSE-004 has run twice for sequential periods).
Both periods have revenue, COGS, and expense postings.

steps.action: Run the comparative P&L (current period vs. prior period).
steps.expected: Report renders with current, prior, and variance columns.

steps.action:
Sum revenue postings in the current period only. Compare to
the current column's revenue.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Sum revenue postings in the prior period only. Compare to the
prior column's revenue.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
For each line, compute (current - prior). Compare to the
report's variance column.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01 line-by-line.

steps.action:
Confirm that postings dated in the period before "prior" do
NOT bleed into the prior column.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
Both columns tie to their period-bounded postings and the variance
is computed correctly line-by-line.
pass_criteria:
Both columns reconcile within $0.01 AND every variance row equals
current - prior within $0.01.

title: Open PO commitments report reconciles to PO line value not yet received
goal:
Run the open PO commitments report. Verify the total open
commitment equals the sum of (PO line qty - received qty -
short-closed qty) × unit price across all open POs, and the
vendor breakdown sums to the grand total.
preconditions: At least one PO is open with partial receipt (P3-RECV-002 partial) and at least one is fully open (no receipts yet).

steps.action: Run the open PO commitments report.
steps.expected: Report shows per-PO and per-vendor: open qty, open value.

steps.action:
For one partially received PO, compute open value = (line
qty - received qty - short-closed qty) × unit price. Sum
across lines.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01 to the report's PO row.

steps.action: Sum vendor subtotals. Compare to the grand total.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Confirm a fully received PO does NOT appear (zero open).
steps.expected: Excluded.

steps.action:
Confirm a short-closed PO line is treated as zero remaining
open (not as a backorder).
steps.expected: Short-close excludes the line from open commitments.

expected_overall:
Open commitment ties to per-PO open value and short-close /
receipt status are honored.
pass_criteria:
Per-PO open value matches hand computation within $0.01 AND
fully received and short-closed are excluded AND vendor sums
match grand total within $0.01.
why_this_matters:
Cash forecasting and inventory budgeting both depend on this
number. A wrong open commitment causes either cash surprises
or inventory shortages.

title: Quote conversion rate reconciles to issued quotes vs. quotes that became SOs
goal:
Run the quote conversion rate report for the period. Verify the
numerator (quotes converted to SOs) and denominator (quotes
issued) reflect actual quote history, and the conversion % is
computed correctly.
preconditions: At least 5 quotes were issued in the period, with at least one converted to an SO and one explicitly lost / expired.

steps.action: Run the quote conversion report for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows: quotes issued, quotes converted, quotes lost,
quotes pending, conversion %.

steps.action:
Pull the quote register filtered to the period. Count
issued.
steps.expected: Compare to the report's denominator. Match.

steps.action: Filter to converted (linked to an SO). Count.
steps.expected: Compare to the numerator. Match.

steps.action:
Verify conversion % = converted / issued × 100, rounded as
documented.
steps.expected: Match within 0.1 pp.

steps.action:
Confirm a still-pending quote is counted in "pending," not
converted or lost.
steps.expected: Correct status bucket.

expected_overall:
Quote conversion ratio reflects real quote history with correct
status bucketing.
pass_criteria:
Numerator and denominator match hand counts AND conversion %
matches within 0.1 pp AND status buckets are correct.
why_this_matters:
Sales pipeline metrics drive forecasting. Wrong conversion rate
distorts pipeline coverage analysis and quota planning.

title: Sales by customer report reconciles to invoice register
goal:
Run the sales-by-customer report for the period and verify each
customer's total equals the sum of their posted invoices in the
period.
preconditions: At least one customer invoice exists in the period.

steps.action: Run the sales-by-customer report for the period.
steps.expected:
Report lists each customer with totals (revenue, possibly count
and average).

steps.action:
For one customer, run the invoice register filtered to that
customer for the same period.
steps.expected: Sum of invoices equals the customer's row in the report.

steps.action:
Verify the report's grand total equals the P&L's revenue total
for the same period (less non-customer revenue items if any).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall: Sales-by-customer ties to the invoice register and the P&L.
pass_criteria:
Per-customer total matches the invoice register AND grand total
ties to the P&L revenue.

title: Sales-by-customer for a custom date range ties only to invoices in that range
goal:
Run the sales-by-customer report for a date range that doesn't
align with the fiscal period. Verify the totals respect the start
and end dates exactly — no leakage at the boundary.
preconditions: Customer invoices exist on multiple dates spanning more than a fiscal period.

steps.action:
Run sales-by-customer for a custom range (e.g., a single
week mid-period).
steps.expected: Report renders for the requested range only.

steps.action:
Pull the invoice register filtered to the same dates and one
customer. Sum invoice totals.
steps.expected: Match the customer's row in the report within $0.01.

steps.action:
Pick an invoice dated one day before the start. Confirm it
is excluded.
steps.expected: Excluded.

steps.action:
Pick an invoice dated one day after the end. Confirm it is
excluded.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
Date filter is exact at both boundaries and per-customer totals
reconcile to the date-filtered invoice register.
pass_criteria:
Per-customer total matches the date-filtered invoice sum within
$0.01 AND boundary invoices are correctly included/excluded.

title: Sales-by-customer with sales-rep breakdown ties to per-rep invoice attribution
goal:
Run the sales-by-customer report broken down (or filtered) by
sales rep. Verify each rep's total equals the sum of invoices
attributed to that rep, and per-rep totals roll up to the
unfiltered grand total.
preconditions: Invoices exist with at least two distinct sales rep attributions.

steps.action:
Run sales-by-customer grouped by sales rep, or filtered to
rep A.
steps.expected: Report shows revenue attributed to rep A.

steps.action:
From the invoice register, sum invoices attributed to rep A
in the period. Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Run filtered to rep B. Sum.
steps.expected: Available.

steps.action:
Confirm (rep A + rep B + ... unassigned) total equals the
unfiltered grand total.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Spot-check: a customer invoice attributed to rep A appears in
rep A's column, not rep B's.
steps.expected: Correct attribution.

expected_overall:
Rep attribution on each invoice flows through to the report and
per-rep sums equal the consolidated.
pass_criteria:
Per-rep total matches invoice register within $0.01 AND per-rep
sum equals consolidated within $0.01.
why_this_matters:
Commissions and quota attainment use this report. Mis-attributed
invoices lead to underpaid reps or paying the wrong rep — both
cause friction.

title: Sales-by-customer drill-through opens the customer's invoice list
goal:
From the sales-by-customer report, drill into one customer. Verify
the drill opens the list of invoices in scope, with totals matching
the parent row.
preconditions: At least one customer has multiple invoices in the period.

steps.action: Run sales-by-customer for the period.
steps.expected: Renders.

steps.action: Drill into one customer's row.
steps.expected: An invoice list opens, scoped to that customer and date range.

steps.action:
Sum the invoice totals in the drill list. Compare to the
parent row.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Open one invoice from the drill list. Confirm customer,
amount, and date match the system invoice.
steps.expected: Match.

expected_overall: Drill list contains the right invoices and totals reconcile.
pass_criteria:
Drill list sum matches parent row within $0.01 AND a spot-checked
invoice opens correctly from the drill.

title: Sales by product report reconciles to invoice lines
goal:
Run the sales-by-product report for the period and verify each
part's revenue equals the sum of that part's invoice lines.
preconditions: At least one customer invoice with one or more part lines exists in the period.

steps.action: Run the sales-by-product report for the period.
steps.expected: Report lists each part with units sold, revenue, possibly margin.

steps.action:
For one part, query the invoice lines: sum the (qty × unit price
- discount) for that part across all invoices in the period.
steps.expected: Sum equals the report row's revenue.

steps.action:
Grand total of revenue across all products equals the
sales-by-customer report's grand total (and the P&L revenue).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Sales-by-product ties to invoice line detail and to other revenue
reports.
pass_criteria:
Per-part revenue matches invoice line sum AND grand total
reconciles to the P&L.

title: Sales-by-product grouped by category reconciles per category and grand total
goal:
Run the sales-by-product report grouped by product category.
Verify each category's subtotal equals the sum of its parts, and
the categories' grand total matches the ungrouped grand total.
preconditions: Parts in the invoice register fall into at least two categories (P2-PART-001 with category assigned).

steps.action: Run sales-by-product grouped by category.
steps.expected: Report shows category subtotals.

steps.action:
Pick one category. Sum the parts within that category in the
report's detail. Compare to the category subtotal.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Sum all category subtotals. Compare to the report's grand
total.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Run the ungrouped sales-by-product report. Confirm the grand
total matches.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Category grouping reconciles bottom-up and the grand total is
invariant under grouping.
pass_criteria:
Per-category subtotal matches its parts within $0.01 AND grouped
grand total equals ungrouped grand total within $0.01.

title: Sales-by-product drill-through opens the part's invoice line detail
goal:
From the sales-by-product report, drill into one part. Verify the
drill opens the invoice lines that contributed to the row, with
qty × price - discount summing to the parent row.
preconditions: At least one part has multiple invoice lines in the period (sold to multiple customers or on multiple dates).

steps.action: Run sales-by-product for the period.
steps.expected: Renders.

steps.action: Drill into one part's row.
steps.expected:
Invoice line list opens for that part across all customers in
the date range.

steps.action:
For each line, compute (qty × unit price) - discount. Sum.
Compare to the part's row revenue.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Click into one line. Confirm the parent invoice opens with the
same customer and totals.
steps.expected: Match.

expected_overall: Drill exposes correct lines and reconciles to the parent row.
pass_criteria:
Sum of (qty × price - discount) on the drill list matches the
parent row within $0.01 AND the parent invoice opens correctly.

title: Top-N sales-by-product reflects actual revenue ranking
goal:
Run the sales-by-product report sorted by revenue descending and
limited to top 10 (or top N). Verify the listed parts are the
actual top revenue earners and that #11+ parts are correctly
excluded.
preconditions: At least 12 parts have revenue in the period (so a top-10 cutoff is meaningful).

steps.action:
Run the sales-by-product report unfiltered, sorted by revenue
descending. Note the top 10 parts and their revenue.
steps.expected: Top 10 list available.

steps.action:
Run the report with "top 10" or limit-10 applied. Compare the
list and per-row revenue.
steps.expected: Same 10 parts, same revenue per row.

steps.action:
Confirm the part ranked #11 in the unfiltered report does
NOT appear in the top-10 view.
steps.expected: Excluded.

steps.action:
Verify the sort is strictly descending (no ties causing
drop-outs).
steps.expected: Order matches the ranked unfiltered list.

expected_overall: Top-N filter is a clean ranking with the correct cutoff.
pass_criteria:
Listed top-10 matches the unfiltered top-10 exactly AND #11 is
excluded AND ordering is descending by revenue.

title: Sales tax / VAT return reconciles to taxable invoice lines and tax payable account
goal:
Run the sales tax (or VAT) return report for the period. Verify
each tax-rate row equals the sum of taxable invoice lines at that
rate × rate, and the total tax due equals the tax payable account
balance on the trial balance.
preconditions: The period has invoices spanning at least two tax rates / tax jurisdictions.
At least one invoice has tax-exempt lines.
VAT/tax codes are configured (P1-TAX-001).

steps.action: Run the sales tax / VAT return for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows per-rate / per-jurisdiction taxable base and
tax due, plus a total.

steps.action:
For one rate, sum invoice lines coded to that rate. Multiply
by the rate. Compare to the report's tax line.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Verify tax-exempt lines are excluded from the taxable base.
steps.expected: Excluded.

steps.action:
Sum tax due across all rates. Compare to the tax payable
account balance for the period (from trial balance).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Tax return ties to taxable invoice lines and to the tax payable
GL balance.
pass_criteria:
Per-rate tax matches (taxable base × rate) within $0.01 AND
total ties to tax payable GL within $0.01 AND exempt lines
excluded.
why_this_matters:
This report is filed with tax authorities. Underreporting risks
penalties; overreporting wastes cash. Either is a common audit
finding when source data and report don't reconcile.

title: Production schedule attainment reconciles to scheduled and completed WO history
goal:
Run the schedule attainment report for the period. Verify
attainment % = (WOs completed on or before scheduled finish) /
(WOs scheduled to finish in the period), and that on-time and
late WO counts reflect actual completion timestamps.
preconditions: At least one WO was scheduled to finish in the period and completed on time, and at least one was completed late (or is still open past its scheduled finish).

steps.action: Run the schedule attainment report for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows: scheduled WOs, on-time completions, late
completions, attainment %.

steps.action: Pull WOs scheduled to finish in the period. Count.
steps.expected: Compare to denominator. Match.

steps.action:
For each, compare actual completion date to scheduled
finish. Count on-time (completed ≤ scheduled).
steps.expected: Compare to numerator. Match.

steps.action: Verify attainment % = on-time / scheduled × 100.
steps.expected: Match within 0.1 pp.

steps.action:
Confirm a WO scheduled outside the period does NOT influence
the attainment metric.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
Schedule attainment reflects actual on-time vs. late
completions within the scheduling window.
pass_criteria:
Numerator and denominator match hand counts AND attainment %
matches within 0.1 pp AND outside-window WOs excluded.
why_this_matters:
Schedule attainment is a primary plant-floor KPI. A wrong
number either masks delivery problems or creates phantom ones,
both eroding ops manager credibility.

title: Scrap and rework summary reconciles to WO scrap reports and NCR detail
goal:
Run the scrap and rework summary for the period. Verify scrap
quantity × cost equals the sum of WO scrap entries, and rework
quantity × cost equals the sum of NCR rework dispositions.
preconditions: At least one WO recorded scrap (P4-COMP with reject qty) and at least one NCR was dispositioned to rework (P5-QC-NCR-001).

steps.action: Run the scrap and rework summary for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows scrap qty, scrap cost, rework qty, rework cost,
and totals.

steps.action:
Pull the WO scrap entries for the period. Sum (scrap qty ×
standard cost) per WO. Compare to the report's scrap cost.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Pull NCRs dispositioned to rework. Sum (rework qty × rework
labor cost). Compare to the report's rework cost.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Confirm the period's scrap GL postings (scrap variance
account) tie to the report's scrap cost.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Scrap and rework totals reconcile to the floor and quality
records that produced them.
pass_criteria:
Scrap cost matches WO entries within $0.01 AND rework cost
matches NCR dispositions within $0.01 AND scrap GL ties.
why_this_matters:
Scrap and rework are the headline waste KPIs. If the report
understates them, improvement programs miss the real opportunity.

title: Open SO backlog reconciles to unshipped / unbilled SO line value
goal:
Run the open SO backlog report. Verify the total open backlog
equals the sum of (SO line qty - shipped qty) × unit price
across all open SOs, and that the report's backlog ties to the
backlog figure on the BBB report (RPT-BBB-001).
preconditions: At least one SO is partially shipped (P4-SHIP-SPLIT-001) and at least one is fully open (no shipments yet).

steps.action: Run the open SO backlog report.
steps.expected:
Report shows per-SO and per-customer: open qty, open value,
requested ship date.

steps.action:
For one partially shipped SO, compute open value = (line qty
- shipped qty) × unit price. Sum across lines.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Sum customer subtotals. Compare to grand total.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Compare the grand total to RPT-BBB-001's backlog figure.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Confirm a fully shipped SO does NOT appear (zero open).
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
Backlog ties to per-SO open value and reconciles to the BBB
backlog figure.
pass_criteria:
Per-SO open value matches hand computation within $0.01 AND
fully shipped SOs excluded AND grand total ties to BBB backlog.

title: Stockout / backorder report lists unfulfilled SO demand and ties to open backorder lines
goal:
Run the stockout / backorder report. Verify it lists every SO
line that could not be fulfilled from on-hand stock as of the
report date, with shortage qty and required date matching the
SO and inventory state.
preconditions: At least one SO has a line whose qty exceeds on-hand at the fulfilling location (a backorder).
At least one SO line has been fully fulfilled (should NOT appear in the report).

steps.action: Run the stockout / backorder report.
steps.expected:
Report lists SO lines with backorder qty, required date,
and customer.

steps.action:
For one SO line on the report, hand-verify: SO qty - shipped
qty - reserved qty available = backorder qty.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action: Confirm a fully shipped SO line does NOT appear.
steps.expected: Excluded.

steps.action:
Confirm an SO line with stock on hand and reserved (but not
yet shipped) does NOT appear (it's not a stockout).
steps.expected: Excluded.

steps.action:
Sort the report by required date ascending. Verify the
earliest required-date row is the most urgent backorder.
steps.expected: Sort order correct.

expected_overall:
Backorder report identifies real shortages with the right
shortage qty and date.
pass_criteria:
Hand-computed shortage qty matches AND fully shipped / fully
reserved lines are excluded AND sort by required date works.
why_this_matters:
Customer service uses this list to communicate delivery dates.
False positives (saying we're short when we're not) cause lost
sales; false negatives (missing real shortages) cause silent
late shipments.

title: Trial balance shows debits = credits across all accounts
goal:
Run a trial balance for the closed period and verify that total
debits equal total credits, and that each account's balance is
derivable from posted JEs.
preconditions: The period has been closed.

steps.action: Run the trial balance as of the close date.
steps.expected: Report lists every account with debits and credits.

steps.action: Total debits across all accounts should equal total credits.
steps.expected:
Equal to the cent. If not, the books are out of balance — a
severe defect.

steps.action:
Pick one account (e.g., Cash). Sum the JE postings to that
account in the period. Compare to the trial-balance net.
steps.expected: Match.

steps.action:
Pick one income-statement account (e.g., Sales Revenue). Verify
the balance equals the sum of revenue postings in the period.
steps.expected: Match.

expected_overall: Trial balance is balanced and each account ties to its postings.
pass_criteria:
Debits = credits AND spot-checked accounts reconcile to JE
postings.

title: Adjusted trial balance differs from preliminary only by closing entries
goal:
Run the preliminary (pre-close) trial balance and the adjusted
(post-close) trial balance for the same period. The difference per
account should equal the sum of closing/adjusting JEs posted to
that account.
preconditions: The period has both pre-close and post-close states available (closing JEs were posted as part of P5-CLOSE-004).
At least one closing/adjusting JE was posted.

steps.action: Run the preliminary trial balance (pre-close, or "as posted").
steps.expected: Report renders showing accounts before closing JEs.

steps.action:
Run the adjusted (post-close) trial balance for the same
period end date.
steps.expected:
Report renders. Account balances may differ for accounts
affected by closing JEs.

steps.action:
Pick one account that had a closing JE (e.g., revenue moving
to retained earnings). Compute (preliminary - adjusted).
steps.expected:
Equals the sum of closing JE postings to that account within
$0.01.

steps.action: Both versions still have debits = credits.
steps.expected: Equal to the cent on both versions.

expected_overall:
Adjusted - preliminary equals the closing JE activity per account,
and both versions remain in balance.
pass_criteria:
Spot-checked account difference equals closing JE sum within $0.01
AND both trial balances remain debit = credit.
why_this_matters:
Misposted closing entries are easy to introduce and tedious to
catch. Comparing pre/post views is the auditor's standard check.

title: Per-dimension trial balance stays in balance for each dimension value
goal:
Run the trial balance filtered to one dimension value (e.g.,
department, location, or class). Verify it still has debits =
credits — i.e., postings tagged to a single dimension are also
internally balanced.
preconditions: Postings carry a department / location / class dimension and at least two values exist with their own activity.

steps.action: Run the trial balance filtered to dimension value A.
steps.expected: Report renders. Total debits = total credits for that slice.

steps.action: Run the trial balance filtered to dimension value B.
steps.expected: Same — debits = credits for that slice.

steps.action:
Run the unfiltered trial balance. Sum (A trial balance debits +
B debits + any other slices). Compare to unfiltered debits.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall:
Per-dimension trial balance is balanced for every slice and the
per-slice sum equals the consolidated.
pass_criteria:
Every per-dimension slice has debits = credits AND the per-slice
sum equals the consolidated within $0.01.
why_this_matters:
A common bug: a one-sided posting tagged to a dimension. The
consolidated trial balance still balances (offset elsewhere) but
the per-dimension view exposes the error.

title: Vendor performance report ties to receipt and quality history
goal:
Run the vendor performance report and verify it reflects actual
on-time, on-quality history from receipts and quality records.
preconditions: At least one vendor has multiple receipts (P3-RECV-001) over time.
At least one of those receipts had a partial / damaged quantity (P3-RECV-003).

steps.action:
Run the vendor performance report covering the relevant date
range.
steps.expected:
Report shows per vendor: on-time delivery percentage, defect
rate, fill rate.

steps.action:
For Pacific Steel Supply (or your test vendor), pull the
receipts log. Count: how many receipts were on-or-before the
promised date, how many were full, how many had any rejected
quantity.
steps.expected: Counts available.

steps.action:
Compute: on-time % = (on-time count / total receipts), defect %
= (rejected qty / total received qty), fill % = (received qty /
ordered qty for closed POs).
steps.expected: Numbers computed.

steps.action: Compare to the report.
steps.expected: Match within rounding.

expected_overall: Vendor performance numbers reconcile to receipt and quality records.
pass_criteria: All three computed metrics match the report within rounding.
why_this_matters:
Procurement uses this report to make sourcing decisions. If the
numbers don't reflect reality, vendor management is broken silently.

title: Vendor performance for a custom date range respects boundaries
goal:
Run the vendor performance report for a date range that excludes
some historical receipts. Verify on-time %, defect %, and fill
% are computed only from receipts dated within the range.
preconditions: One vendor has receipts spanning multiple periods (some good, some late, some with rejected qty), enough to make a date- bounded slice meaningful.

steps.action: Run vendor performance for a 60-day window.
steps.expected: Report renders for that window.

steps.action:
Pull only the receipts within that 60-day window. Hand-
compute on-time %, defect %, fill %.
steps.expected: Compute.

steps.action: Compare to the report.
steps.expected: Match within rounding.

steps.action:
Confirm a receipt from outside the window does NOT influence
the metrics.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
Performance metrics correctly bound to the date range with no
leakage.
pass_criteria:
All three metrics match the date-windowed hand computation within
rounding AND outside-window receipts are excluded.

title: Vendor performance grouped by commodity category aggregates correctly
goal:
Run the vendor performance report grouped by vendor commodity /
category. Verify the per-category averages reflect only vendors
in that category and that per-vendor numbers don't double-count
across categories.
preconditions: At least two vendor categories (e.g., "Steel," "Electronics") each have multiple vendors with receipt history.

steps.action: Run vendor performance grouped by category.
steps.expected: Report shows category headers with aggregated metrics.

steps.action:
For one category, list the vendors in it. Compute the
receipt-weighted average on-time %.
steps.expected: Compute.

steps.action: Compare to the report's category aggregate.
steps.expected: Match within rounding.

steps.action:
Confirm a vendor in a different category is NOT included in
the first category's aggregate.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall:
Category grouping aggregates only the right vendors and metrics
reconcile to a hand-weighted average.
pass_criteria:
Category aggregate matches receipt-weighted average within
rounding AND no cross-category leakage.

title: Drill-through from a vendor's row opens the underlying receipt history
goal:
From the vendor performance report, drill into one vendor's
performance row. Verify the drill opens the receipts (and
associated quality records) that produced the metrics.
preconditions: At least one vendor has multiple receipts including one with a quality issue.

steps.action: Run vendor performance for the relevant date range.
steps.expected: Renders.

steps.action: Drill into a vendor's row.
steps.expected:
A list of that vendor's receipts opens, with promised vs.
actual date and rejected quantity per row.

steps.action:
Count: on-time receipts, defective receipts, total receipts.
Recompute on-time % and defect %.
steps.expected: Numbers match the parent row within rounding.

steps.action:
Open one receipt from the drill list. Confirm PO number,
receipt date, and quality outcome match the receipt record.
steps.expected: Match.

expected_overall:
Drill exposes the receipts that produced the metrics and the
hand-recompute matches.
pass_criteria:
Hand-recompute from drill list matches parent row within rounding
AND a spot-checked receipt opens correctly.

title: Vendor statement reconciliation matches our AP ledger to the vendor's view
goal:
Generate the per-vendor statement (or vendor reconciliation
worksheet). Verify the listed invoices, credit memos, and
payments equal the AP ledger for that vendor and the ending
balance matches the vendor's row on AP aging.
preconditions: At least one vendor has period activity — an invoice, a payment, and a credit memo (P3-AP-CM-001).

steps.action:
Generate the vendor statement / reconciliation for one
vendor for the period.
steps.expected:
Statement lists invoices, payments, and credit memos in date
order with running balance.

steps.action: Compare each line to the vendor's entries in the AP ledger.
steps.expected: Every entry matches; no missing or extra.

steps.action:
Verify ending balance = opening + (invoices - payments -
credit memos applied).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Compare ending balance to the vendor's row on AP aging
(RPT-APAGE-001).
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall: Vendor statement equals AP ledger and ties to AP aging.
pass_criteria:
Every transaction in the period appears on the statement AND
ending balance ties to AP aging within $0.01.
why_this_matters:
Vendor reconciliation prevents double payments and missed
credit memos. A statement that doesn't tie to AP makes the
reconciliation untrustworthy.

title: Withholding remittance reconciles to per-vendor withholding postings and the payable account
goal:
Run the withholding remittance report (e.g., 1099 / 1042 /
contractor withholding) for the period. Verify per-vendor
withholding equals what was deducted on payments to that vendor,
and total withholding ties to the withholding payable GL.
preconditions: At least one vendor is flagged for withholding (P5-WHTAX-001).
At least one payment has been made with withholding deducted.

steps.action: Run the withholding remittance report for the period.
steps.expected:
Report shows per-vendor: gross paid, withholding amount, net
paid, and a total.

steps.action:
For one vendor, pull the period's payment register. Sum the
withholding deductions. Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action: Verify gross - withholding = net per row.
steps.expected: Holds within $0.01.

steps.action:
Sum total withholding. Compare to the withholding payable
GL balance for the period.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Confirm a non-withholding vendor does NOT appear on the
report.
steps.expected: Excluded.

expected_overall: Withholding totals reconcile per-vendor and at the GL.
pass_criteria:
Per-vendor withholding matches payment register within $0.01
AND total ties to withholding payable GL within $0.01 AND
non-withholding vendors are excluded.
why_this_matters:
Withholding remittance is filed with tax authorities and the
vendor uses it to file their own taxes. Errors create dual
liability — to the vendor and to the tax authority.

title: WO variance report reconciles to actual material and labor postings
goal:
Run the WO variance report for closed work orders. Verify the
material and labor variance numbers equal (actual posted - standard
per BOM/routing) for each WO.
preconditions: At least one WO has been completed and closed (P4-COMP-FINAL).

steps.action: Run the WO variance report for closed WOs.
steps.expected:
Report displays standard cost, actual cost, and variance per WO,
broken down by material and labor.

steps.action:
For one closed WO, look up the BOM standard quantities and the
actual material issued (from P4-MATL-ISSUE). Compute (actual -
standard) at unit cost.
steps.expected: Compute by hand or with a spreadsheet. Compare to the report.

steps.action:
Repeat for labor: routing standard hours × labor rate vs. actual
labor recorded (P4-LABOR).
steps.expected: Compute and compare.

steps.action:
Both variance lines on the report should match the hand
calculation within $0.01.
steps.expected: Match.

expected_overall: WO variance report numbers reconcile to source material / labor.
pass_criteria: Material and labor variance match the hand calculation within $0.01.

title: WO variance filtered by work center ties to that work center's WO postings
goal:
Run the WO variance report filtered to a single primary work
center. Verify the totals equal the sum of variance across only
WOs whose primary operation runs at that work center.
preconditions: WOs exist whose primary operation is at two or more distinct work centers (P4-COMP-FINAL run for multiple WOs).

steps.action: Run the WO variance report filtered to work center A.
steps.expected:
Report shows only WOs whose primary operation is at work
center A.

steps.action:
Pull the closed WOs primarily at work center A. For each,
compute (actual cost - standard cost). Sum.
steps.expected: Compute.

steps.action: Compare to the report's total variance.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Confirm a WO primarily at a different work center does NOT
appear in the filtered report.
steps.expected: Excluded.

steps.action:
Run the unfiltered report. Sum (filter A total + filter B
total + ...). Compare.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

expected_overall: Work-center filter correctly partitions WOs and totals reconcile.
pass_criteria:
Filtered total matches hand-computed work-center variance within
$0.01 AND per-work-center sum equals unfiltered total within $0.01.

title: WO variance drill-through opens material and labor detail
goal:
From the WO variance report, drill into one WO's variance line.
Verify the drill exposes per-material-issue and per-labor-entry
detail that sums to the WO's variance.
preconditions: At least one closed WO has both material and labor variance (P4-COMP-FINAL with quantities differing from standard).

steps.action: Run the WO variance report.
steps.expected: Report renders with a row per closed WO.

steps.action: Drill into one WO's variance.
steps.expected:
A breakdown opens showing per-component material variance and
per-operation labor variance.

steps.action:
Sum the per-component material variance lines. Compare to the
WO's total material variance on the parent report.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Sum the per-operation labor variance lines. Compare to the
WO's total labor variance on the parent report.
steps.expected: Match within $0.01.

steps.action:
Drill one more level into one material variance line. Confirm
it lists the actual issue transactions for that component.
steps.expected: Issue transactions list opens.

expected_overall:
Variance drill-through exposes the correct breakdown and each
level reconciles to the parent.
pass_criteria:
Material and labor detail sums each match the parent WO variance
within $0.01 AND the deepest drill exposes real source issues.
